Professor Madya TS Dr Mohd Hanafi Bin Ahmad Hijazi | **Responsibility Assignment:** - **Role Mapping:** Enfrasys Solutions shall define and map workspace roles to UMS operational requirements. - **User Assignment:** Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall assign mapped roles to appropriate personnel as per the table above. This role-based access model ensures that UMS maintains proper governance, security, and operational efficiency in its Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environment. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision This section details the Microsoft Fabric solution design for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), covering data workflow, access, platform, resource organization, and governance. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence in a single SaaS solution. It leverages OneLake as a unified data lake, Dataflow Gen2 for ETL, Lakehouse for layered data storage, and Power BI for visualization. The following table summarizes the key data workflow design considerations for UMS, including AutoML workflow elements: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | | DE01 | Cleaned data in Silver Lakehouse will serve as input for all ML experimentation; selected program from Staging DB | Data Engineering | | DE02 | Manual ML approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark/Python) for custom feature engineering and model optimization | Data Science | | DE03 | Model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning performed in Notebook environment for tailored UMS results | Data Science | | DE04 | Trained models registered in Fabric Model Registry for lifecycle management and versioning | Data Science | | DE05 | Scoring Notebooks apply trained models to new data; scored outputs stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Engineering | | DE06 | Gold Lakehouse as standardized repository for all modeled/scored results, ensuring consistency | Data Engineering | | DE07 | Semantic Model connects to Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake); Data Modelling implemented | Power BI | | DE08 | Power BI Desktop connects to Semantic Model via OneLake for visualization | Power BI | ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following data workflow design for UMS, integrating ETL, Lakehouse layering, machine learning, and Power BI visualization: | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Data ingestion via Azure Data Factory from UMS SQL Server staging area | Adopt Data Factory for robust pipeline orchestration | | 2 | Layered Lakehouse architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold) | Implement Bronze (raw), Silver (cleansed), Gold (curated) Lakehouses | | 3 | Data cleansing and transformation using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, Visual Query | Utilize Dataflow Gen2 and Notebooks for flexible ETL and ML integration | | 4 | Machine Learning using both AutoML and Manual ML in Fabric Notebooks | Enable both AutoML and custom ML workflows; register models in Fabric Model Registry | | 5 | Scoring and storing ML outputs in Gold Lakehouse | Store all scored/model outputs in Gold Lakehouse for consistency | | 6 | Semantic Model built on Gold Lakehouse, connected via Direct Lake (OneLake) | Implement Semantic Model for business analytics; leverage OneLake for unified access | | 7 | Power BI Desktop connects to Semantic Model for visualization | Use Power BI for interactive dashboards and reporting | Figure 1 below illustrates the end-to-end data workflow, including ETL, Lakehouse layering, ML process, and Power BI integration: ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\46645fc0-7de0-482c-a292-bc377db7c983\images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 2 below demonstrates the AutoML and manual ML workflow, showing data flow from Silver Lakehouse through ML experimentation to Gold Lakehouse and Power BI: ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\46645fc0-7de0-482c-a292-bc377db7c983\images\slide_021_img_005.png) ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Conditional Access Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft's cloud-based identity and access management service, providing authentication, authorization, and conditional access policies. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah enforce Conditional Access policies to secure access to Fabric resources, especially for privileged operations and external connections. ### Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a security mechanism requiring users to provide two or more verification factors to access resources. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS mandate MFA for all administrative and contributor roles within Fabric and Power BI workspaces. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|---------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Conditional Access Policy | Enforce for all privileged and external access to Fabric, OneLake, and Power BI resources| | 2 | MFA Requirement | Mandatory for all admin and contributor roles; recommended for members | | 3 | Guest Access | Restrict guest access except for approved external collaboration | | 4 | Role Assignment | Assign workspace roles based on least privilege principle | ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size For supporting ETL workloads, Notebooks, and Power BI integration, Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following VM specifications for UMS: | Component | Specification | |-------------------|----------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Azure Management Groups provide a hierarchical structure for organizing subscriptions and resources, enabling governance, policy enforcement, and access control at scale. Enfrasys Solutions will design the management group hierarchy; Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall maintain operational governance. ### Naming and Tagging Consistent naming and tagging conventions are critical for resource discoverability, automation, and cost governance. Enfrasys Solutions proposes the following conventions for Fabric resources: #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |------------------|-------------|------------------------------| | Subscription | sub | ums-prod-sub | | Resource Group | rg | ums-data-rg | | Storage Account | st | umsonelake01 | | VM | vm | ums-etl-vm01 | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming -- | |-----|------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK
Etc… | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF
Etc.. | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL
Etc.. | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | Resource | Abbreviation | |------------------|-------------| | Lakehouse | LK | | Dataflow | DF | | Semantic Model | SM | | Pipeline | PL | | Workspace | WS | ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### Governance Disciplines Enfrasys Solutions will establish governance disciplines for UMS covering cost management, resource lifecycle, access control, and compliance. | Azure Tool | Description | Cost Management Discipline | |----------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------| | Azure Cost Management| Monitor, analyze, and optimize cloud spending | Budgeting, alerting, resource tagging | | Azure Policy | Enforce resource standards and compliance | Policy enforcement, audit | | Azure Advisor | Provides recommendations for cost, security, performance| Optimization, review | ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-----------------------|----------------------------------------------|---------------------|---------------| | Unauthorized Access | Detection via Conditional Access logs | UMS | Active | | Resource Naming Violation | Automated policy audit | Enfrasys Solutions | Implemented | | Cost Overrun | Budget alert, review, and escalation | UMS | Active | | Policy Non-compliance | Azure Policy audit and remediation | Enfrasys Solutions | Scheduled | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for initial design and implementation; Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for ongoing operational governance and monitoring. # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) focusing on Azure Network Security Groups (NSG) and encryption standards for the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) supporting Microsoft Fabric and Power BI workloads. ## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSG) are a core security feature within Microsoft Azure, designed to filter network traffic to and from Azure resources in a virtual network. NSGs allow administrators to define inbound and outbound security rules based on source and destination IP addresses, ports, and protocols, thereby enforcing granular access control and reducing attack surfaces. Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for the configuration and maintenance of all NSG rules associated with OPDG virtual machines. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall monitor compliance and ensure that all NSG configurations align with organizational security policies. ### Outbound NSG Rules The following table details the outbound NSG rules required to enable secure connectivity from the OPDG VM to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services: | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |----------------------------|----------|------------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | *Note: No inbound rules are specified for OPDG in this design, as all gateway access is managed through outbound connectivity.* Enfrasys Solutions will implement these NSG configurations to ensure secure, compliant connectivity. UMS shall perform ongoing compliance monitoring and alerting. ## 7.2 Encryption Design Microsoft Fabric and Power BI require robust encryption standards to protect sensitive data both at rest and in transit. Below, Enfrasys Solutions defines the recommended encryption mechanisms for UMS’s deployment. ### SSL/TLS for Gateway Communication SSL/TLS (Secure Sockets Layer/Transport Layer Security) are industry-standard protocols for securing network communications. All data transmitted between the OPDG VM and Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Azure services shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher. This ensures confidentiality and integrity of data in transit, preventing interception or tampering. - **Gateway VM to Microsoft Cloud:** All outbound connections (e.g., HTTPS on port 443) utilize TLS encryption. - **User Authentication:** Identity verification and token exchanges with Microsoft Entra ID are protected via TLS. ### Data Encryption at Rest Data stored within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI is encrypted at rest using Azure-managed encryption keys. This includes: - **Azure Storage and Data Warehouse:** Data is encrypted using AES-256, managed by Azure Key Vault. - **Power BI Workspaces:** All datasets and reports are encrypted at rest, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards. ### Data Encryption in Transit - **Between OPDG and Microsoft Services:** All communications leverage HTTPS/TLS. - **Internal Azure Data Flows:** Azure backbone traffic is encrypted by default. Enfrasys Solutions will configure all gateway and cloud endpoints to enforce encryption policies. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall validate compliance through periodic security audits and reporting. --- **Responsibility Assignment:** - **NSG Configuration:** Enfrasys Solutions - **Encryption Policy Implementation:** Enfrasys Solutions - **Compliance Monitoring:** Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) **Summary:** The above security controls ensure that UMS’s OPDG deployment for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI adheres to Azure best practices, providing robust protection for data in transit and at rest, and minimizing exposure to unauthorized access. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the formal deployment and migration methodology recommended for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), including task assignments, pilot execution, production rollout, and post-migration support. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup Enfrasys Solutions recommends a structured pre-migration phase to ensure readiness and minimize risk. Responsibilities are clearly assigned between Enfrasys Solutions and Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) for each activity. | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|---------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------------|-------------|------------------------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for technical setup and environment configuration, while UMS is responsible for confirming data readiness and reviewing documentation. ## 8.2 Pilot The pilot phase is designed to validate the migration approach and ensure operational readiness before full production rollout. Enfrasys Solutions will provision a limited subset of the Fabric environment, connect to the staging area database, and configure the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) for initial data ingestion. Responsibilities: - **Enfrasys Solutions:** Setup pilot environment, configure OPDG, execute initial data ingestion, monitor performance, and document outcomes. - **UMS:** Provide access to staging area database, validate pilot data, and formally accept pilot results. The pilot will be conducted using representative workloads and datasets identified by UMS. Any issues encountered will be documented and addressed prior to production rollout. ## 8.3 Production Upon successful completion of the pilot, Enfrasys Solutions will proceed with the production rollout. This includes full deployment of the Fabric environment, migration of all required datasets, and configuration of operational workflows. Responsibilities: - **Enfrasys Solutions:** Execute production migration, finalize Fabric configuration, establish monitoring and alerting, and provide operational handover documentation. - **UMS:** Ensure all production data is available and accessible, perform acceptance testing, and sign off on production readiness. Production rollout will follow a phased approach, leveraging lessons learned from the pilot to ensure minimal disruption and high reliability. ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Enfrasys Solutions recommends a structured post-migration support model to ensure operational stability and facilitate knowledge transfer. The support timeline is organized as follows: ### Migration Timeline Table | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |--------------|----------------------------------------------------|---------------------|-------------| | D-7 | Pre-migration checks, environment validation | Enfrasys, UMS | Scheduled | | D0 | Migration cutover, initial data ingestion | Enfrasys | Scheduled | | D+1 | Post-migration support, issue resolution, training | Enfrasys, UMS | Scheduled | - **D-7 (Pre-Migration):** Enfrasys Solutions will conduct final environment validation and pre-migration checks. UMS will confirm data readiness and access permissions. - **D0 (Cutover):** Enfrasys Solutions will execute migration cutover, initiate data ingestion, and monitor system performance. - **D+1 (Post-Migration):** Enfrasys Solutions will provide post-migration support, resolve any issues, and deliver training to UMS personnel. UMS will participate in acceptance testing and confirm operational readiness. This approach ensures clear accountability, minimizes risk, and provides Universiti Malaysia Sabah with a robust foundation for ongoing operations. # 9.0 Appendix This section provides supplementary technical information, prerequisites, limits, and references for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) regarding computing, networking, identity & security, logging & monitoring, cloud governance, and Microsoft Fabric-specific design. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall ensure that all virtual machines (VMs) provisioned for the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) and related workloads meet the minimum technical requirements specified for compatibility with Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure services. - Microsoft Docs reference: [Azure Virtual Machines documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/) - Prerequisites: - Supported operating systems: Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) - .NET Framework version 4.8 or higher - Minimum vCPU: 8 cores - Minimum RAM: 8 GB ### Limits and Boundaries UMS must adhere to Azure VM quota limits and supported configurations. - Azure VM quota limits: [Azure subscription limits and quotas](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits) - VM size compatibility: Only VM sizes supporting 8 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM or above are permitted for OPDG workloads. ### Others - Additional guidance: [Azure VM best practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/best-practices) - For specialized workloads (e.g., large-scale data processing), consider scaling up VM resources as required. ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites UMS must configure Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) to allow secure outbound connectivity from OPDG VMs to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, Entra ID, and related endpoints. - Microsoft Docs reference: [Azure Network Security Groups documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview) - Prerequisites: - Ensure outbound TCP port 443 (HTTPS) is open to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core service domains. - Allow outbound TCP port 443 for authentication endpoints (login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, etc.). - Allow outbound TCP port 80 for connectivity test endpoints (*.msftncsi.com). ### Limits and Boundaries - Azure NSG rules per subnet: [Azure networking limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-limitations) - Maximum NSG rules: 500 per NSG. - Each rule must be uniquely prioritized and not conflict with existing security policies. ### Others - Additional guidance: [Azure NSG best practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview#best-practices) - For troubleshooting, use Azure Network Watcher to validate outbound connectivity. ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites UMS shall utilize Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) for authentication, authorization, and identity management across all Microsoft Fabric and Power BI workloads. - Microsoft Docs reference: [Microsoft Entra ID documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/) - Prerequisites: - All users must have valid Entra ID accounts. - Configure role-based access control (RBAC) for workspace and resource access. - Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) for privileged accounts. ### Limits and Boundaries - Entra ID object limits: [Azure AD service limits and restrictions](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/azure-ad-service-limits) - Maximum users per directory: 50,000 (default, can be increased). - Maximum groups per directory: 5,000 (default, can be increased). ### Others - Additional guidance: [Entra ID security best practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/security-best-practices) - Consider conditional access policies for enhanced security. ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites UMS shall implement Azure Monitor and related logging solutions to track, audit, and alert on activities within Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure resources. - Microsoft Docs reference: [Azure Monitor documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/) - Prerequisites: - Enable diagnostic logging on all VMs and cloud resources. - Configure Log Analytics workspace for centralized log collection. - Set up alert rules for critical events (e.g., authentication failures, service outages). ### Limits and Boundaries - Azure Monitor data retention: [Azure Monitor limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/platform/data-retention) - Maximum data ingestion: 500 MB/day per Log Analytics workspace (default, can be increased). - Maximum alert rules: 250 per subscription. ### Others - Additional guidance: [Azure Monitor best practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/best-practices) - For advanced analytics, integrate with Power BI for visualization. ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites UMS shall establish cloud governance policies using Azure Policy, Management Groups, and Resource Naming Conventions to ensure compliance, cost control, and resource organization. - Microsoft Docs reference: [Azure Policy documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/) - Prerequisites: - Define management groups for organizational hierarchy. - Apply Azure Policy for resource compliance (e.g., allowed VM sizes, location restrictions). - Implement resource naming conventions as per design. ### Limits and Boundaries - Azure Policy limits: [Azure Policy limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/limits) - Maximum policies per subscription: 5,000. - Maximum management groups: 10,000 per tenant. ### Others - Additional guidance: [Azure governance best practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/) - Regularly review policy compliance and audit logs. ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Fabric-specific ### Introduction/Prerequisites UMS shall follow Microsoft Fabric architecture guidelines for data workflows, workspace organization, and machine learning integration. - Microsoft Docs reference: [Microsoft Fabric documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/) - Prerequisites: - Organize Fabric items (capacity, workspace, lakehouse, dataflow, semantic model, pipeline, report) using the prescribed naming convention. - Implement both manual and AutoML machine learning workflows using Fabric Notebooks. - Register trained models in Fabric Model Registry for lifecycle management. ### Limits and Boundaries - Fabric workspace and capacity limits: [Microsoft Fabric limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/service-limits) - Maximum workspaces per capacity: 100. - Maximum lakehouses per workspace: 50. - Maximum pipelines per workspace: 50. ### Others - Additional guidance: [Fabric data engineering best practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-engineering/) - Refer to the architecture diagram below for the UMS AutoML workflow: Figure 1 below illustrates the AutoML and manual machine learning workflow within the UMS Fabric ecosystem, showing data flow from Silver Lakehouse tables through machine learning processes to Gold Lakehouse tables, with integration to Power BI via OneLake. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\46645fc0-7de0-482c-a292-bc377db7c983\images\slide_021_img_005.png)

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving the following components and data flow:

1. **Fabric-RG**:  
   - Represents a resource group in Azure, likely hosting resources related to the "UMS Fabric."  
   - The resource group acts as a logical container for managing and organizing Azure resources.

2. **UMS Fabric**:  
   - A service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.  
   - It appears to be the core system or platform that external users interact with.  

3. **Conditional Access**:  
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric.  
   - It enforces policies based on user identity, device compliance, location, and other conditions to ensure secure access.

4. **External User**:  
   - Represents an end-user or client accessing the UMS Fabric.  
   - The user is subject to Conditional Access policies before being granted access.

**Data Flow**:  
- The arrow indicates a unidirectional flow of access or interaction from the UMS Fabric to the External User.  
- Conditional Access acts as a gatekeeper, ensuring that only authorized and compliant users can access the UMS Fabric resources.

This architecture emphasizes secure access control for external users interacting with the UMS Fabric, leveraging Azure's Conditional Access policies.

This image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies. Below is a detailed description of the components and data flow:

1. **User Authentication Attempt**:  
   - The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric. This is the initial step where the user seeks access to the system.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:  
   - The authentication request is evaluated against Entra Conditional Access policies. These policies determine whether the user can proceed based on specific conditions.

3. **Signal Collection**:  
   - Signals are gathered to assess the context of the authentication request. Examples of signals include:
     - **Users and Groups**: Identifying the user or group attempting access.
     - **Network Location**: Evaluating the network from which the request originates.

4. **Decision Making**:  
   - Based on the collected signals, a decision is made regarding the user's access. This decision is influenced by the organization's security policies and the context of the request.

5. **Enforcement**:  
   - The decision is enforced. If the user meets the conditions, they are granted access. Otherwise, access is denied or additional actions (e.g., multi-factor authentication) are required.

6. **Access to Microsoft Fabric**:  
   - Upon successful authentication and policy compliance, the user gains access to Microsoft Fabric. This includes various components such as:
     - Data Factory
     - Synapse
     - Power BI
     - OneLake
     - Other integrated services within the Microsoft Fabric ecosystem.

This diagram effectively demonstrates the secure and conditional access workflow for Microsoft Fabric, ensuring compliance with organizational policies and safeguarding resources.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and analytics workflow within the Azure ecosystem, leveraging UMS Fabric and OneLake for data management and Power BI for visualization.

1. **Data Ingestion**:
   - Data originates from the **UMS Staging Area**, hosted on **Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse**.
   - The ingestion process is managed by **Azure Data Factory**, which extracts and loads the data into the pipeline.

2. **Bronze Layer**:
   - The ingested data is stored in **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**, representing the raw data layer.
   - This layer serves as the initial storage for unprocessed data.

3. **Silver Layer**:
   - Data from the Bronze layer is transformed and moved to **XX_Silver_LK Tables**.
   - This layer contains cleaned and enriched data, ready for further processing or analysis.

4. **Data Science and Processing**:
   - The **Data Factory** processes the Silver layer data, enabling advanced analytics and machine learning workflows.
   - **Data Science** tools are applied to derive insights and prepare data for downstream consumption.

5. **Gold Layer**:
   - Processed data is stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**, representing the curated and business-ready data layer.
   - This layer is optimized for reporting and analytics.

6. **Identity Management**:
   - The **Identity_SM** component ensures secure access and identity management for the Gold layer data.

7. **Integration with OneLake**:
   - The entire data pipeline is integrated with **OneLake**, providing unified data storage and management capabilities.

8. **Visualization**:
   - The curated data in the Gold layer is connected to **Power BI** via OneLake.
   - Power BI is used for creating interactive dashboards and reports for business intelligence.

This architecture demonstrates a modern data pipeline leveraging Azure services for efficient data ingestion, transformation, storage, and visualization.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating the data flow between components in a hybrid cloud environment.

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:  
   - This is the on-premises data warehouse where data is stored and managed. It serves as the source or destination for data being transferred.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:  
   - Acts as a bridge between the on-premises data warehouse and the cloud-based UMS Fabric.  
   - Facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened in the on-premises network.  
   - Establishes an outbound secure connection to the cloud, ensuring data security and compliance.

3. **UMS Fabric**:  
   - Represents the cloud-based service or platform that interacts with the on-premises data warehouse via the OPDG.  
   - Likely used for advanced analytics, processing, or integration with other cloud services.

**Data Flow**:  
- Data flows bidirectionally between the on-premises SQL Server 2017 data warehouse and the UMS Fabric through the On-Premise Data Gateway.  
- The OPDG ensures secure communication by initiating an outbound connection to the UMS Fabric, eliminating the need for inbound ports, which enhances security.  
- This setup supports hybrid cloud scenarios, enabling seamless integration of on-premises and cloud resources.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

### Description of the Architecture:

1. **Data Source: XX_Silver_LK Tables**
   - The data originates from **XX_Silver_LK Tables**, which are part of the **UMS Fabric** ecosystem. These tables serve as the intermediate layer of processed data.

2. **Machine Learning Pathways:**
   - **AutoML Pathway:**
     - The data from **XX_Silver_LK Tables** is fed into an **AutoML Experiment**.
     - The **AutoML Experiment** performs automated machine learning tasks, such as model selection and hyperparameter tuning.
     - The results of the AutoML process are scored using a **Notebook**, which likely involves evaluating the model's performance and generating predictions.
   - **Manual Machine Learning Pathway:**
     - Alternatively, the data from **XX_Silver_LK Tables** can be used for **Manual Machine Learning**.
     - This involves custom modeling and experimentation using a **Notebook**, allowing for more control over the machine learning process.

3. **Output Data:**
   - The outputs from both the **AutoML** and **Manual Machine Learning** pathways are stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**, which represent the final, refined data layer.
   - The **XX_Gold_LK Tables** are part of the **UMS Fabric** and are connected to **Identity_SM**, a component that likely handles identity management or metadata tagging.

4. **Integration with OneLake and Power BI:**
   - The **XX_Gold_LK Tables** are connected to **Power BI** via **OneLake**, enabling visualization and reporting of the processed data.
   - **OneLake** acts as a unified data storage layer, facilitating seamless integration and access to the data for analytics.

### Summary:
This architecture diagram illustrates a data processing and machine learning workflow within the **UMS Fabric** ecosystem. It supports both automated and manual machine learning approaches, with outputs stored in a refined data layer and integrated with **Power BI** for visualization through **OneLake**.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Power BI & Microsoft Fabric Solution Design, including project context, document purpose, and key stakeholders. ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) has initiated a strategic project to modernize its data analytics infrastructure by leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The primary business objective is to design and implement an integrated data workflow that supports ingestion, transformation, and visualization of institutional data. The project scope encompasses the establishment of a Microsoft Fabric workspace, development of end-to-end ETL pipelines, execution of Machine Learning processes, and seamless integration with Power BI for business-aligned analytics. This initiative is intended to align UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s advanced services, specifically: - **Fabric Data Factory:** Data integration and orchestration. - **Fabric Synapse:** Advanced data analytics and big data processing. - **Data Science:** Machine Learning application. The solution is designed to enable UMS to derive actionable insights, enhance decision-making processes, and support institutional objectives through modern, scalable analytics capabilities. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The purpose of this Solution Design Document is to formally outline the technical architecture, workflow design, and implementation approach for the UMS Power BI & Microsoft Fabric project. This document serves as the authoritative reference for all design decisions, proposed infrastructure, and workflow processes, as well as the next actions required for successful project execution. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the creation and maintenance of this document, ensuring that all technical recommendations are aligned with UMS’s business requirements and Microsoft best practices. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for reviewing and providing sign-off on the document to confirm alignment with institutional goals and compliance requirements. ## 2.3 Document Audience The Solution Design Document is intended for the following primary stakeholders: - **UMS IT Department:** Responsible for technical review, implementation oversight, and ongoing operational management. - **UMS Analytics Teams:** End users and process owners for data ingestion, transformation, machine learning, and visualization workflows. - **Enfrasys Solutions Project Team:** Architects, consultants, and engineers accountable for solution design, delivery, and technical support. This document is also relevant to UMS executive sponsors and compliance officers, ensuring transparency and alignment throughout the project lifecycle. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for document creation and technical accuracy, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for review and formal sign-off. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Overview This section provides a formal overview of the core Microsoft technologies selected for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) in support of the data workflow, analytics, and authentication requirements. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform designed to enable organizations to manage, integrate, and analyze data across multiple domains. Fabric consolidates data engineering, data science, data integration, and business intelligence into a single SaaS solution, streamlining the end-to-end data lifecycle from ingestion to visualization. ### Core Features of Microsoft Fabric - **Data Factory**: Microsoft Fabric Data Factory provides robust data integration and orchestration capabilities, allowing for seamless extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of data from diverse sources into OneLake, Fabric's centralized data repository. - **Synapse**: Fabric Synapse delivers advanced data analytics and big data processing, enabling UMS to perform scalable analytics and complex data transformations within a secure environment. - **Data Science**: The Data Science module within Fabric supports machine learning workflows, empowering UMS to build, train, and deploy predictive models leveraging integrated data assets. - **OneLake**: OneLake is the foundational data lake within Fabric, offering a unified storage solution for all data assets, facilitating secure, scalable, and governed data access across the organization. Enfrasys Solutions recommends Microsoft Fabric as the primary platform for UMS’s data workflow, ETL pipeline development, machine learning processes, and analytics integration. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall implement Fabric workspaces and adopt its services according to the design specifications. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business intelligence and analytics service, enabling organizations to visualize data, share insights, and drive informed decision-making. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, allowing data processed and stored in Fabric to be easily consumed for interactive reporting and dashboard creation. ### Integration Points with Microsoft Fabric - Direct connectivity to Fabric’s OneLake for real-time data visualization. - Seamless integration with Fabric Data Factory and Synapse for analytics-driven reporting. - Embedded analytics capabilities within Fabric workspaces for business-aligned insights. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for selecting Power BI as the visualization layer for UMS’s business analytics. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall adopt Power BI for operational reporting and strategic dashboarding as part of the overall solution. ## 3.3 Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. Entra ID provides secure authentication, authorization, and conditional access controls for users and applications across Microsoft cloud services. ### Role in Authentication and Conditional Access - Centralized user authentication for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. - Conditional access policies to enforce security and compliance requirements. - Role-based access control (RBAC) for workspace and resource management. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for recommending Entra ID as the authentication backbone for UMS’s cloud analytics environment. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall implement Entra ID for user management, access control, and compliance enforcement across all Microsoft services. --- **Responsibility Assignment Table** | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------|---------| | Technology Selection | Evaluate and recommend Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID for analytics and authentication | Enfrasys Solutions | Complete| | Service Adoption | Provision Fabric workspace, Power BI integration, Entra ID configuration | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending | --- This section establishes the foundational Microsoft technologies for UMS’s analytics and authentication requirements, with clear responsibilities assigned to Enfrasys Solutions and Universiti Malaysia Sabah. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture, connectivity, and gateway deployment for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) to ensure secure, hybrid integration between on-premises and Microsoft Fabric environments. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall implement a hybrid network architecture that enables secure data flow between on-premises resources and Microsoft Fabric. The design leverages Azure resource groups, conditional access, and outbound-only data gateway connectivity. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\965ab0dc-820d-4dd0-9bfc-ec106a16afb4\images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 1 above illustrates the secure access control architecture for UMS Fabric, where external users access the platform via Azure Conditional Access policies. The Fabric-RG acts as a logical container for Azure resources, and Conditional Access enforces security based on user identity, device compliance, and location. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\965ab0dc-820d-4dd0-9bfc-ec106a16afb4\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2 demonstrates the authentication flow for Microsoft Fabric, utilizing Entra Conditional Access policies. The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) acts as a secure bridge, facilitating outbound-only connections from the on-premises environment to the cloud. This eliminates the need for inbound ports, significantly enhancing security and compliance. ## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway ### Definition: Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway The Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway, specifically the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG), is a secure connectivity agent that enables cloud services such as Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure Data Factory to access data sources located within an organization's on-premises network. The gateway establishes outbound connections to Microsoft cloud services, ensuring data is transferred securely without opening inbound firewall ports. ### OPDG Deployment Design Enfrasys Solutions recommends deploying the OPDG agent on a dedicated virtual machine within UMS’s on-premises environment. This VM will establish outbound, encrypted connections to Microsoft Fabric, acting as the sole conduit for data transfer from the on-premises SQL Server 2017 data warehouse to the cloud. Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for the installation, configuration, and maintenance of the OPDG agent. Administrators in Microsoft Fabric are responsible for managing workspaces, configuring access controls, and ensuring compliance with organizational policies. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall be responsible for the ongoing assignment and management of users within each workspace role to ensure operational continuity and data security. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Design and Decision This section outlines the solution design for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) regarding Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, including data workflow, access, platform sizing, resource organization, and governance. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a single SaaS solution. It leverages OneLake for data storage, Data Factory for orchestration, Synapse for analytics, and Power BI for visualization. Power BI is a business analytics service that enables interactive visualizations and business intelligence capabilities with an interface simple enough for end users to create reports and dashboards. Enfrasys Solutions has compiled the following data workflow considerations for UMS, covering both ETL and ML workloads: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering| | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering| | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering| | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering| | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | | DE01 | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation. The chosen data will be one of the programs selected from the overall Staging DB. | Data Engineering| | DE02 | The Project will primarily adopt the Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python) to enable custom feature engineering, algorithm tuning, and model optimization. | Data Science | | DE03 | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment to achieve more accurate and explainable results tailored to UMS’s datasets. | Data Science | | DE04 | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry to support model lifecycle management, versioning, and potential reuse across further adoption. | Data Science | | DE05 | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data. The scored outputs, predictions, probabilities, and performance metrics will be stored in the Gold Lakehouse. | Data Engineering| | DE06 | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results, ensuring consistency between AutoML and manual ML outputs. | Data Engineering| | DE07 | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) and Data Modelling will be implemented | Power BI | | DE08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS implement a multi-layered data workflow leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, as illustrated in the referenced diagrams. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\965ab0dc-820d-4dd0-9bfc-ec106a16afb4\images\slide_009_img_003.png) *Figure 1: End-to-end ETL and reporting workflow integrating Data Factory, Lakehouse layers, OneLake, and Power BI.* ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\965ab0dc-820d-4dd0-9bfc-ec106a16afb4\images\slide_021_img_005.png) *Figure 2: Machine Learning workflow using AutoML and manual ML, with results stored in Gold Lakehouse and visualized via Power BI.* **Summary of Design Decisions:** - Data is ingested from UMS’s SQL Server staging area using Data Factory pipelines. - Bronze Lakehouse stores raw data; An NSG contains security rules that allow or deny inbound and outbound traffic to Azure resources, based on source, destination, port, and protocol. NSGs are essential for isolating workloads, protecting sensitive data, and ensuring compliance with organizational and regulatory requirements. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the configuration, deployment, and validation of NSG rules as outlined below. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall monitor compliance and maintain operational oversight. ### Outbound NSG Rules for OPDG VM The following table summarizes the outbound NSG rules required to enable secure connectivity for the OPDG virtual machine to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and authentication endpoints: | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |----------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | Enfrasys Solutions shall implement these NSG rules to ensure secure outbound connectivity from the OPDG VM. UMS is responsible for ongoing compliance monitoring and reporting. ## 7.2 Encryption Design ### SSL/TLS Encryption SSL/TLS (Secure Sockets Layer/Transport Layer Security) are cryptographic protocols designed to secure communications over computer networks. SSL/TLS ensures that data transmitted between client devices, OPDG, and Microsoft cloud services is encrypted, protecting against interception and tampering. - All outbound connections from OPDG to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints utilize HTTPS (TCP port 443), secured via SSL/TLS. - Enfrasys Solutions shall validate SSL/TLS configurations during deployment. - UMS shall monitor certificate validity and enforce periodic reviews. ### Data-at-Rest Encryption Data-at-rest encryption protects stored data from unauthorized access, even if the storage media is compromised. Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure services natively encrypt data at rest using industry-standard algorithms such as AES-256. - Microsoft Fabric: Data stored in Fabric is encrypted at rest using Azure Storage encryption. - Power BI: Datasets, reports, and models are encrypted at rest within the Power BI service. - OPDG VM: Disk encryption shall be enabled using Azure Disk Encryption, leveraging BitLocker or DM-Crypt. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure and verify data-at-rest encryption settings across all relevant platforms. UMS is responsible for maintaining encryption compliance and reviewing audit logs. ### Data-in-Transit Encryption Data-in-transit encryption ensures that data moving between OPDG, Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID endpoints is protected from eavesdropping and man-in-the-middle attacks. - All communications between OPDG and Microsoft cloud services occur over encrypted HTTPS channels. - Authentication and token exchanges with Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) are secured using TLS 1.2 or higher. Enfrasys Solutions shall verify that all endpoints enforce data-in-transit encryption. UMS shall monitor and report any exceptions or vulnerabilities. --- **Responsibility Assignment Table** | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|-------------| | NSG Rule Configuration | Define, implement, and validate NSG outbound rules for OPDG VM | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Encryption Validation | Verify SSL/TLS, data-at-rest, and data-in-transit encryption across all components | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Compliance Monitoring | Monitor NSG rule compliance, encryption status, and operational security posture | UMS | Ongoing | --- **Figure 1 below illustrates the outbound network security architecture for OPDG connectivity to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI:** *(No diagram file_path provided in slide data; if available, insert as per instructions.)* --- Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall implement the above security controls in partnership with Enfrasys Solutions to ensure a robust, compliant, and secure environment for Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and OPDG operations. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the formal deployment and migration approach for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), detailing phases, responsibilities, and timelines for the rollout of the Microsoft Fabric solution. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup (D-7) The pre-migration phase is critical for establishing readiness and ensuring all prerequisites are met prior to pilot and production deployment. [Azure Monitor Best Practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/best-practices) - [Diagnostic Settings Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings) ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites UMS should adopt Azure governance frameworks for resource management and compliance: - [Azure Governance Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/) - [Azure Policy Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/) ### Limits and Boundaries Governance tools have operational boundaries: - [Azure Policy Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts#limits) - [Management Group Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#management-group-limits) ### Others Additional resources for cost control and management: - [Azure Cost Management Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/) - [Azure Blueprints Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/) ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI ### Introduction/Prerequisites UMS should refer to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI documentation for analytics and reporting: - [Microsoft Fabric Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/) - [Power BI Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/) ### Limits and Boundaries Fabric and Power BI have specific workspace and dataset limits: - [Microsoft Fabric Capacity Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/limits) - [Power BI Service Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/service-limits) ### Others Supplementary analytics resources: - [Power BI Security Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/admin/service-security) - [Microsoft Fabric Best Practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/get-started/fabric-best-practices) --- **Responsibility Assignment:** - Enfrasys Solutions: Compilation and update of appendix content. - Universiti Malaysia Sabah: Operational review and validation of appendix material. For detailed technical design reference, please consult the corresponding main solution design sections.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving the following components and data flow:

1. **Fabric-RG**:  
   - Represents a resource group in Azure, likely hosting resources related to the "UMS Fabric."  
   - The resource group acts as a logical container for managing and organizing Azure resources.

2. **UMS Fabric**:  
   - A service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.  
   - It appears to be the core system or platform that external users interact with.

3. **Conditional Access**:  
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric.  
   - It enforces policies based on conditions such as user identity, device compliance, location, or risk level.

4. **External User**:  
   - Represents an end-user or client accessing the UMS Fabric.  
   - Access is mediated by the Conditional Access policies to ensure secure and compliant interactions.

5. **Data Flow**:  
   - The arrow indicates a unidirectional flow of access or interaction from the UMS Fabric to the External User.  
   - Conditional Access acts as a gatekeeper, ensuring that only authorized users can access the UMS Fabric.

This architecture emphasizes secure access control for external users interacting with the UMS Fabric, leveraging Azure's Conditional Access policies.

This image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**:  
   - The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:  
   - The authentication request is evaluated against Entra Conditional Access policies. This involves three key stages:
     - **Signal (Step 3)**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are analyzed. Examples include:
       - Users and groups.
       - Network location.
     - **Decision (Step 4)**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
     - **Enforcement (Step 5)**: The decision is enforced, allowing or denying access based on the policy evaluation.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**:  
   - If access is granted, the user is directed to Microsoft Fabric, which includes various components such as:
     - Data Factory.
     - Synapse Analytics.
     - Power BI.
     - OneLake (a unified data lake for storage).

This diagram highlights the integration of conditional access policies with Microsoft Fabric to ensure secure and policy-driven access control.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and analytics workflow within the Azure ecosystem, leveraging UMS Fabric, Data Factory, OneLake, and Power BI.

1. **Data Source**:  
   - The process begins with data ingestion from the **UMS Staging Area**, hosted on **Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse**. This serves as the primary data source.

2. **Data Ingestion**:  
   - **Azure Data Factory** is used to ingest data from the staging area into the UMS Fabric environment. This is the first step in the ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) pipeline.

3. **Bronze Layer**:  
   - The ingested raw data is stored in **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**. This layer acts as the raw data storage, preserving the original data format for traceability and auditing.

4. **Silver Layer**:  
   - Data is transformed and cleaned using **Azure Data Factory** and stored in **XX_Silver_LK Tables**. This layer contains processed and structured data, ready for further analysis.

5. **Data Science Integration**:  
   - The **Data Science** component is integrated into the pipeline, enabling advanced analytics, machine learning, or predictive modeling on the Silver Layer data.

6. **Gold Layer**:  
   - The final, refined data is stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**. This layer contains business-ready data optimized for reporting and visualization.

7. **Identity Management**:  
   - The **Identity_SM** component is used for managing security, access control, and identity-related operations within the Gold Layer.

8. **Data Lake Integration**:  
   - The entire data pipeline is integrated with **OneLake**, which serves as a unified data lake for centralized storage and management.

9. **Visualization and Reporting**:  
   - The processed data in the Gold Layer is connected to **Power BI** via OneLake for visualization, reporting, and business intelligence.

This architecture demonstrates a modern data pipeline leveraging Azure services to enable efficient data ingestion, transformation, storage, and visualization.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a data flow between three main components:

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:
   - Represents an on-premises SQL Server 2017 database used as a staging area or data warehouse.
   - It stores and processes data locally before it is transferred to other systems.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:
   - Acts as a bridge between the on-premises SQL Server and cloud-based services.
   - Facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened in the on-premises network.
   - Establishes an outbound secure connection to the cloud, ensuring data security and compliance.

3. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Represents a cloud-based service or platform that consumes data from the on-premises SQL Server.
   - The data is transferred securely via the On-Premise Data Gateway.

**Data Flow**:
- Data originates in the on-premises SQL Server 2017 database.
- The On-Premise Data Gateway securely connects to the SQL Server, retrieves the data, and transmits it to the UMS Fabric.
- The connection between the On-Premise Data Gateway and UMS Fabric is outbound and secure, eliminating the need for inbound ports, which enhances security.

This architecture supports hybrid data integration scenarios, ensuring secure and efficient data movement between on-premises and cloud environments.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

### Description:
The diagram illustrates a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure-based ecosystem, integrating components like **UMS Fabric**, **OneLake**, **AutoML**, **Manual Machine Learning**, and **Power BI** for data analysis and visualization.

1. **Data Source:**
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Acts as the primary data source, likely containing pre-processed or intermediate data stored in **OneLake**.

2. **Machine Learning Pathways:**
   - **AutoML Pathway**:
     - Data from **XX_Silver_LK Tables** is fed into an **AutoML Experiment**.
     - The experiment performs automated machine learning tasks, such as model selection and hyperparameter tuning.
     - The results are scored using a **Notebook**, which likely involves evaluating the model's performance.
   - **Manual Machine Learning Pathway**:
     - Alternatively, data from **XX_Silver_LK Tables** is used for **Manual Machine Learning**.
     - This involves custom modeling and experimentation using a **Notebook** for greater control over the process.

3. **Output Storage:**
   - The results from both pathways are stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**, representing refined or final datasets.
   - These tables are also stored in **OneLake** for centralized data management.

4. **Identity Management:**
   - **Identity_SM**: A component integrated with **XX_Gold_LK Tables**, possibly for managing data access, security, or user-specific transformations.

5. **Visualization and Reporting:**
   - The **XX_Gold_LK Tables** are connected to **Power BI** via **OneLake**.
   - This enables advanced data visualization and reporting for business insights.

### Connections:
- **Dashed Arrows**: Represent data flow between components.
- **OR Condition**: Indicates that either the **AutoML** or **Manual Machine Learning** pathway can be used for processing data.
- **OneLake**: Serves as the central data repository, ensuring seamless integration across components.

This architecture supports scalable, flexible machine learning workflows with robust data management and visualization capabilities.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.
