# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides an overview of the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Microsoft Fabric solution design, its business objectives, and the intended audience for formal review and approval. ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) has initiated a project to modernize its data analytics and business intelligence capabilities through the adoption of Microsoft Fabric. The primary business objective is to design and implement a comprehensive data workflow that spans data ingestion, transformation, and visualization, thereby enabling data-driven decision-making for UMS’s business stakeholders. Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform provided by Microsoft, offering integrated services for data integration, orchestration, advanced analytics, and visualization. Core components include: - **Fabric Data Factory:** Enables data integration and orchestration across diverse sources. - **Fabric Synapse:** Delivers advanced analytics and big data processing capabilities. - **Data Science:** Supports machine learning applications for predictive analytics. - **Power BI:** Provides business-aligned analytics and interactive visualization. The scope of this project encompasses: - Establishment of a dedicated Fabric workspace for UMS. - Development of end-to-end ETL pipelines. - Execution of machine learning processes. - Integration of Power BI for business analytics. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the technical design, implementation guidance, and solution review, while UMS will provide business requirements, participate in design validation, and ensure alignment with organizational objectives. ## 2.2 Document Purpose This Solution Design Document serves as the formal blueprint for the Microsoft Fabric implementation at UMS. It details the proposed architecture, workflow, technology components, and operational considerations. The document is intended for structured review, validation, and sign-off by all stakeholders to ensure the solution meets UMS’s business and technical requirements. ## 2.3 Document Audience The intended audience for this document includes: - **Enfrasys Solutions:** Lead architect, technical consultants, and implementation specialists. - **UMS IT Team:** Technical leads, system administrators, and support personnel. - **UMS Business Stakeholders:** Department heads, analytics users, and project sponsors. Responsibility for review and sign-off is jointly assigned to Enfrasys Solutions and Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). Both parties shall ensure the design aligns with business goals, technical standards, and compliance requirements before proceeding to implementation. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric, Power BI & Entra ID Overview This section provides formal definitions, core features, and project relevance for Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID. Enfrasys Solutions will deliver technical guidance, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall validate business alignment. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, and business intelligence capabilities. It consolidates multiple Microsoft services—including Data Factory, Synapse, and Data Science—into a single SaaS solution, enabling organizations to manage data workflows from ingestion to visualization seamlessly. ### Core Features - **Data Factory**: Provides robust data integration and orchestration services, supporting ETL pipelines and data movement across various sources. - **Synapse**: Delivers advanced analytics and big data processing, offering capabilities for data warehousing, real-time analytics, and batch processing. - **Data Science**: Enables machine learning workflows, supporting model training, deployment, and integration within the data pipeline. ### Typical Use Cases - End-to-end data workflow management (ingestion, transformation, visualization) - Centralized workspace for data engineering, analytics, and machine learning - Integration of disparate data sources for unified analytics ### Relevance to UMS Project For Universiti Malaysia Sabah, Microsoft Fabric will serve as the backbone for designing the data workflow. It will establish a Fabric workspace, develop ETL pipelines, facilitate machine learning processes, and integrate analytics—all aligned with UMS’s business requirements. Enfrasys Solutions will provide technical guidance on Fabric implementation, while UMS will validate alignment with business objectives. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business intelligence platform, designed to transform raw data into interactive dashboards and reports. It enables users to visualize and share insights across the organization, supporting informed decision-making. ### Core Features - Interactive dashboards and real-time reporting - Integration with multiple data sources, including Microsoft Fabric - Advanced data modeling and analytics capabilities - Secure sharing and collaboration features ### Typical Use Cases - Business-aligned analytics and visualization - Departmental and executive reporting - Data-driven decision support ### Relevance to UMS Project Power BI will be integrated within the Microsoft Fabric workspace to provide business-aligned analytics for Universiti Malaysia Sabah. Enfrasys Solutions will guide the technical integration and report development, while UMS will ensure that analytics outputs meet business needs and strategic goals. ## 3.3 Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is a cloud-based identity and access management service. It enables secure authentication, authorization, and user management across Microsoft cloud services and third-party applications. ### Core Features - Single sign-on (SSO) for Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and other Azure services - Role-based access control (RBAC) and policy enforcement - Integration with on-premises directories and external identity providers - Conditional access and security monitoring ### Typical Use Cases - Secure user authentication for data and analytics platforms - Centralized access management for cloud resources - Compliance and governance for identity and access ### Relevance to UMS Project Entra ID will underpin access management for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, ensuring secure and compliant authentication for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s users. Enfrasys Solutions will design and configure Entra ID policies, while UMS will validate user roles and business access requirements. --- **Responsibility Assignment** - Enfrasys Solutions: Technical guidance, platform configuration, integration, and policy design - Universiti Malaysia Sabah: Business validation, role mapping, and alignment with organizational objectives # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section details the network architecture and connectivity decisions for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), focusing on secure integration between on-premises resources, the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG), and Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s solution architecture requires seamless and secure data flow between its on-premises environment, the OPDG virtual machine, and Microsoft Fabric. The network design ensures that data is transferred securely, leveraging outbound connections and conditional access policies. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\285846c4-9112-441b-b1d2-9a34436efdf4\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1 illustrates the data gateway architecture, showing the flow from UMS’s on-premises SQL Server (staging/data warehouse) through the OPDG VM to UMS Fabric in the cloud. The OPDG establishes a secure outbound connection, ensuring compliance and minimizing exposure. ### Connectivity Summary - **On-premises SQL Server**: Hosts staging/data warehouse. - **OPDG VM**: Deployed within UMS’s network perimeter; acts as a secure bridge. - **UMS Fabric**: Cloud-hosted platform for analytics and integration. - **Data Flow**: Outbound only from OPDG to Fabric; no inbound ports required. - **Security**: Conditional Access policies enforced for all external access. ## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway ### Microsoft Fabric Definition Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform provided by Microsoft, integrating data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. It consolidates services such as Data Factory, Synapse, Power BI, and OneLake, enabling organizations to manage, analyze, and visualize data securely and efficiently. ### On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) Overview The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that facilitates secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and cloud services such as Microsoft Fabric. The OPDG is installed on a virtual machine within the UMS network and establishes outbound connections, ensuring that data remains secure and compliant with organizational policies. #### OPDG VM Specification | Component | Specification | |------------------|-----------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | #### Deployment Responsibilities | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |--------------|---------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|---------------| | OPDG VM Provisioning | Architect and deploy OPDG VM with required specs | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | | OPDG Installation | Install and configure Data Gateway agent | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | | On-premises Access | Provide network access and credentials | UMS | Pending | Enfrasys Solutions shall architect and deploy the OPDG VM, including installation and configuration of the Data Gateway agent. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide necessary on-premises access, including network connectivity and credentials required for integration. ### Data Gateway Security and Connectivity The OPDG ensures secure, outbound-only connectivity, eliminating the need for inbound firewall rules. All data transfers are encrypted and authenticated, aligning with Microsoft best practices for hybrid connectivity. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\285846c4-9112-441b-b1d2-9a34436efdf4\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2 demonstrates the authentication and access control flow, where Entra Conditional Access policies evaluate signals, enforce decisions, and grant access to Microsoft Fabric components. ## 4.3 Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Data Gateway VM Specification | Minimum 8 vCPU, 8 GB RAM, Windows 10/Server 2019| | 2 | Gateway Deployment Responsibility | Enfrasys Solutions to architect and deploy | | 3 | On-premises Access Responsibility | UMS to provide network access and credentials | | 4 | Data Flow Architecture | Outbound-only connectivity from OPDG to Fabric | | 5 | Security Enforcement | Conditional Access policies applied to Fabric | Enfrasys recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah strictly adhere to these design decisions to ensure secure, reliable, and scalable connectivity between on-premises resources and Microsoft Fabric. # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI This section defines the roles and access structure recommended for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environments. ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a single SaaS solution. It enables organizations to manage, analyze, and visualize data across multiple sources securely and efficiently. The Fabric Administrator is responsible for the overall governance, configuration, and operational management of the Microsoft Fabric environment. Administrators ensure compliance, data security, access control, and optimal performance across analytics workloads. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS assign Fabric Administrator responsibilities as follows: | Capability | Description | |------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Environment Governance | Oversee workspace creation, access policies, and resource allocation. | | Security Management | Configure authentication, enforce role-based access, and monitor activity logs. | | Data Integration | Manage data connectors, ingestion pipelines, and integration with external sources. | | Resource Optimization | Monitor usage, optimize compute/storage, and enforce quotas. | | Compliance Oversight | Ensure adherence to regulatory requirements and internal policies. | | Incident Response | Address security incidents, troubleshoot issues, and coordinate with Enfrasys Solutions. | UMS shall assign personnel to the Fabric Administrator role to maintain operational integrity and compliance. ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric and Power BI support a range of native roles tailored to analytics and business intelligence workflows. Below is a summary of key roles recommended for UMS: - **Data Engineer:** Designs, builds, and maintains data pipelines. Responsible for ETL, data transformation, and integration tasks. - **Business User Admin:** Manages access for business users, configures workspace settings, and ensures data availability for reporting. - **Data Analyst:** Develops reports and dashboards, performs data modeling, and delivers actionable insights to stakeholders. - **Stakeholder:** Reviews analytics outputs, provides feedback, and participates in decision-making based on data-driven evidence. - **Project Director:** Oversees project progress, resource allocation, and ensures alignment with organizational goals. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS assign these roles based on personnel expertise and project requirements. ## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following workspace roles and access mappings for Universiti Malaysia Sabah. UMS shall assign personnel to each role as listed below. | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-----|---------------------|-----------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias

Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall assign and maintain personnel for each workspace role as listed above. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision This section outlines the design, workflow, access, resource organization, and governance considerations for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric deployment. All referenced diagrams, tables, and standards are included as per formal consulting requirements. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations ### Definition: Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence on a single SaaS foundation. It leverages OneLake for storage, provides orchestration via Data Factory, and enables advanced analytics and visualization through Power BI. The following table summarizes the key data workflow design considerations for UMS’s Fabric environment: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following workflow architecture to optimize UMS’s data processing and analytics lifecycle: - Data is ingested from the UMS Staging Area (Microsoft SQL Server 2017) using Azure Data Factory. - Data is staged in Bronze Lakehouse, cleansed in Silver Lakehouse, and transformed for business consumption in Gold Lakehouse. - Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and Visual Query are used for transformation and enrichment. - Machine learning is performed using both AutoML and manual notebook approaches; results are stored in Gold Lakehouse. - Power BI connects via OneLake to Gold Lakehouse for visualization. Figure 1 below illustrates the end-to-end data workflow, showing the flow from SQL Server through Data Factory, Lakehouse layers, and Power BI integration. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\285846c4-9112-441b-b1d2-9a34436efdf4\images\slide_009_img_003.png) **AutoML Workflow Decision:** UMS will primarily leverage manual machine learning via Fabric Notebooks (PySpark/Python) for feature engineering and model optimization, with results scored and stored in Gold Lakehouse. Model registry will be used for lifecycle management. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\285846c4-9112-441b-b1d2-9a34436efdf4\images\slide_021_img_005.png) **Data Gateway Decision:** A dedicated VM will host the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG), bridging secure outbound connectivity between UMS’s SQL Server and Fabric. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\285846c4-9112-441b-b1d2-9a34436efdf4\images\slide_011_img_004.png) ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions UMS’s Fabric environment will be secured using Microsoft Entra ID Conditional Access and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA). ### Definition: Entra ID Conditional Access Microsoft Entra ID Conditional Access is a policy-driven access control engine that enables organizations to enforce security requirements based on user, device, location, and risk signals. ### Conditional Access | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|-----------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------| | 1 | Require MFA for all administrative access | Enforced for all Fabric admins | | 2 | Restrict access to Fabric only from trusted IP ranges| Enforced for UMS campus and VPN locations | | 3 | Block legacy authentication protocols | Enforced | | 4 | Require device compliance for Power BI access | Enforced for all business users | Enfrasys Solutions will design and implement these policies. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide user lists and feedback for policy tuning. ### Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) MFA will be mandatory for all users accessing Fabric administrative interfaces and Power BI reports containing sensitive data. ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size ### Definition: On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) The On-Premises Data Gateway is a Microsoft agent that enables secure connectivity between on-premises data sources and cloud services such as Power BI, Power Automate, and Fabric. | Component | Specification | |-------------------|--------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | Enfrasys Solutions will provision and configure the VM. UMS is responsible for providing network access and monitoring. ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Definition: Azure Management Groups Azure Management Groups are containers for managing access, policy, and compliance across multiple subscriptions, providing hierarchical governance. ### Management Groups UMS’s Fabric resources will be grouped under a dedicated management group for analytics workloads, ensuring separation from production and test environments. ### Naming and Tagging #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |------------------|-------------|---------------------------| | Resource Group | rg | ums-analytics-rg | | Virtual Machine | vm | ums-opdg-vm01 | | Storage Account | st | ums-onelake-st01 | | Data Factory | df | ums-ingest-df01 | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming -- | |-----|------------------|--------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | Abbreviation | Meaning | |--------------|----------------------| | LK | Lakehouse | | DF | Dataflow | | SM | Semantic Model | | PL | Pipeline | | WS | Workspace | Enfrasys Solutions will enforce naming and tagging standards. UMS shall comply and provide feedback for continuous improvement. ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### Definition: Azure Cost Management Azure Cost Management is a suite of tools for monitoring, allocating, and optimizing cloud expenditure, supporting budgeting, forecasting, and chargeback. ### Governance Disciplines | Azure Tool | Description | Cost Management Discipline | |----------------------|------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------| | Cost Management + Billing | Tracks usage and spending across subscriptions | Budgeting, Forecasting | | Azure Policy | Enforces resource standards and compliance | Resource Governance | | Azure Advisor | Provides optimization recommendations | Cost Optimization | | Management Groups | Hierarchical resource organization | Access Control, Compliance | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing and enforcing governance disciplines. UMS shall comply and provide feedback. ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |---------------------|-------------------------------------------|---------------------|---------| | Naming violation | Audit resource names, enforce correction | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | | Policy violation | Remediate non-compliant resources | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | | Budget overrun | Notify stakeholders, recommend optimizations | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | | Unauthorized access | Investigate, revoke access, report | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | --- **Summary:** Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah implement the above design, access, resource organization, and governance standards for Microsoft Fabric. Enfrasys will design, enforce, and monitor; UMS shall comply and provide operational feedback. All diagrams and tables referenced are included as required for formal documentation. # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture and operational controls for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), focusing on Azure Network Security Groups (NSG) and encryption standards for Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG). ## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSG) are a core Azure security feature that allows administrators to filter network traffic to and from Azure resources using inbound and outbound rules. NSGs enforce granular control over access, ensuring only permitted traffic flows to critical workloads. Enfrasys Solutions recommends configuring NSG rules to restrict and monitor all network traffic associated with OPDG, Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall validate compliance with these rules as part of their security governance. ### Inbound Rules Inbound NSG rules control traffic entering the OPDG VM. For this deployment, only essential inbound access is permitted, minimizing attack surface. | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|---------------------|------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_Admin | 100 | UMS Admin IP Range | * | OPDG VM | 3389 | TCP | Allow | | Deny_All_Other_Inbound | 200 | * | * | OPDG VM | * | * | Deny | **Explanation:** The Allow_OPDG_Admin rule permits remote administration from designated UMS IP ranges via RDP. All other inbound traffic is denied by default. ### Outbound Rules Outbound NSG rules govern traffic leaving the OPDG VM, ensuring secure connectivity with Microsoft services. | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |---------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | | Deny_All_Other_Outbound | 200 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | * | * | * | Deny | **Explanation:** These rules ensure OPDG can securely communicate with Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and authentication endpoints using encrypted protocols. All other outbound traffic is blocked. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure the above NSG rules. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for validating that these rules meet internal and regulatory compliance requirements. ## 7.2 Encryption Design Encryption is critical for protecting data in transit and at rest across all Microsoft workloads. Below are formal definitions and design decisions for each technology in scope: ### Microsoft Fabric Encryption Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, and business intelligence. Fabric enforces SSL/TLS encryption for all service communications, ensuring data confidentiality and integrity during transit between client applications and Fabric endpoints. **Design Decision:** Enfrasys Solutions shall ensure that all connections to Microsoft Fabric utilize SSL/TLS 1.2 or higher. UMS shall validate that encryption standards align with university policy and regulatory requirements. ### Power BI Encryption Power BI is a Microsoft business analytics service that provides interactive visualizations and business intelligence capabilities. Power BI encrypts data in transit using HTTPS (SSL/TLS) and offers encryption at rest for datasets stored in the service. **Design Decision:** Enfrasys Solutions shall configure Power BI workspaces and gateways to enforce HTTPS for all user and data traffic. UMS shall review encryption compliance for sensitive reports and datasets. ### On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) Encryption The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) enables secure data transfer between on-premises sources and Microsoft cloud services. OPDG uses SSL/TLS for all outbound connections to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, ensuring that data is encrypted during transit. **Design Decision:** Enfrasys Solutions shall configure OPDG to require SSL/TLS for all outbound connections. UMS shall validate gateway logs and connection settings for encryption compliance. **Responsibility Assignment:** - Enfrasys Solutions: Configuration and implementation of NSG rules and encryption protocols. - Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS): Validation of compliance and ongoing security governance. --- **Summary:** This security design mandates strict network controls and industry-standard encryption for all UMS workloads, ensuring data protection and regulatory alignment. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the end-to-end deployment and migration strategy for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), detailing key phases, responsibilities, and timelines to ensure a successful rollout of the new data platform. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup The pre-migration phase focuses on foundational activities required to prepare the environment and stakeholders for migration. Enfrasys Solutions will lead all technical setup, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) will provide validation and operational support. | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------|---------------|------------|-------------------------------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started| | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started| | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started| | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started| | **Timeline:** - D-7: Design Document delivery and review - D-5: Server confirmation and staging DB readiness - D-3: Fabric environment setup - D-0: On-premises data gateway installation ## 8.2 Pilot The pilot phase is designed to validate migration processes and platform stability with a limited subset of data and users. Enfrasys Solutions will execute the pilot migration, configure initial workloads, and monitor system performance. UMS shall validate data integrity, user access, and provide feedback on operational aspects. **Responsibilities:** - **Enfrasys Solutions:** Lead pilot migration, configure workloads, monitor performance, resolve technical issues. - **UMS:** Validate migrated data, test user access, provide feedback, escalate any operational concerns. **Pilot Timeline:** - D0: Pilot migration kickoff - D+1: Validation and feedback - D+3: Issue resolution and readiness assessment ## 8.3 Production The production phase marks the full migration and go-live of the platform. Enfrasys Solutions will oversee the migration of all remaining workloads, ensure system optimization, and coordinate cutover activities. UMS will support user onboarding, validate business processes, and ensure operational continuity. **Responsibilities:** - **Enfrasys Solutions:** Lead production migration, optimize workloads, perform cutover, provide technical guidance. - **UMS:** Support user onboarding, validate business processes, monitor operational continuity, escalate issues. **Production Timeline:** - D0: Production migration and cutover - D+1: User onboarding and business validation - D+3: Full operational handover ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Post-migration support ensures continued operational stability and addresses any residual issues. Enfrasys Solutions will provide hypercare support, monitor system health, and conduct knowledge transfer sessions. UMS will participate in operational reviews, validate system performance, and transition to steady-state operations. **Responsibilities:** - **Enfrasys Solutions:** Provide hypercare support (D+1 to D+7), monitor health, conduct knowledge transfer, resolve outstanding issues. - **UMS:** Participate in reviews, validate performance, transition to steady-state, escalate support needs. **Post-Migration Timeline:** - D+1: Hypercare support begins - D+7: Knowledge transfer and operational review - D+14: Transition to steady-state operations --- Enfrasys Solutions recommends strict adherence to the phased approach and timeline to minimize risk and ensure a seamless migration for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). All activities and responsibilities are clearly assigned to ensure accountability throughout the deployment lifecycle. # 9.0 Appendix This section provides supplementary information, official documentation links, and current Microsoft platform boundaries for each major design area. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for compiling this appendix; Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall review and reference these materials as needed during implementation and operations. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Azure provides scalable computing resources, including Virtual Machines (VMs), App Services, and container platforms. These services enable UMS to deploy workloads flexibly and securely. For foundational guidance, refer to [Azure Compute Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/compute/). ### Limits and Boundaries Azure imposes quotas and boundaries to ensure platform stability and fair resource allocation. Review the latest VM and compute limits at [Azure Subscription and Service Limits, Quotas, and Constraints](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits). ### Others For best practices and troubleshooting, consult [Azure VM Troubleshooting Guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-machines/) and [Azure Compute FAQ](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/compute/faq). ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure networking provides robust connectivity solutions including Virtual Networks (VNets), Network Security Groups (NSGs), VPN Gateways, and ExpressRoute. These components are essential for secure and scalable network architecture. See [Azure Networking Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/networking/). ### Limits and Boundaries Networking resources have defined quotas, such as maximum VNets per subscription and NSG rules per network interface. Review current boundaries at [Azure Networking Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits#networking-limits). ### Others For advanced scenarios and troubleshooting, refer to [Azure Network Troubleshooting Guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/networking/) and [Azure Networking FAQ](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/networking/faq). ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is the cloud-based identity and access management service for Azure. It secures user authentication, application access, and resource permissions. See [Microsoft Entra ID Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/). ### Limits and Boundaries Entra ID enforces limits on directory objects, group memberships, and application registrations. Review the latest boundaries at [Microsoft Entra ID Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/azure-ad-service-limits). ### Others For security best practices and troubleshooting, consult [Entra ID Security Guidance](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/security-guidance) and [Entra ID Troubleshooting Guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/active-directory/). ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor and Log Analytics provide comprehensive logging, monitoring, and alerting capabilities. These tools help UMS track performance, diagnose issues, and maintain compliance. See [Azure Monitor Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/). ### Limits and Boundaries Log Analytics workspaces and Azure Monitor have ingestion and retention limits. Review these at [Azure Monitor Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/service-limits). ### Others For integration and troubleshooting, refer to [Azure Monitor Troubleshooting Guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/azure-monitor/) and [Azure Monitor FAQ](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/faq). ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure governance tools, including Management Groups, Azure Policy, and Cost Management, enable UMS to enforce standards, monitor compliance, and control costs. See [Azure Governance Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/). ### Limits and Boundaries Governance resources have quotas, such as maximum policies per scope and management group hierarchies. Review boundaries at [Azure Policy Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts#limits) and [Management Group Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#management-group-limits). ### Others For practical guidance and troubleshooting, consult [Azure Policy Troubleshooting Guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/governance/policy/) and [Azure Governance FAQ](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/faq). ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Fabric-specific ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that combines data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. It enables UMS to unify data workflows and deliver actionable insights. See [Microsoft Fabric Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/). ### Limits and Boundaries Fabric has defined limits for workspace size, data ingestion rates, and concurrent workloads. Review the latest boundaries at [Microsoft Fabric Service Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/service-limits). ### Others For advanced configuration and troubleshooting, refer to [Microsoft Fabric Troubleshooting Guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/troubleshoot/) and [Microsoft Fabric FAQ](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/faq).

This is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving the following components and data flow:

1. **Fabric-RG**:  
   - Represents a resource group in Azure, likely hosting resources related to the "UMS Fabric."  
   - The resource group acts as a logical container for managing and organizing Azure resources.

2. **UMS Fabric**:  
   - A service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.  
   - It appears to be the core system or platform that external users interact with.

3. **Conditional Access**:  
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric.  
   - It enforces policies based on conditions such as user identity, device compliance, or location.

4. **External User**:  
   - Represents an end-user or client accessing the UMS Fabric.  
   - Access is mediated through the Conditional Access layer to ensure security.

**Data Flow**:  
- The arrow indicates a unidirectional flow from the UMS Fabric to the External User.  
- Before the external user can access the UMS Fabric, their request is evaluated against the Conditional Access policies.  
- If the conditions are met, access is granted, ensuring secure interaction with the UMS Fabric.

This architecture emphasizes secure access control for external users interacting with the UMS Fabric, leveraging Azure's resource grouping and Conditional Access capabilities.

This image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**:  
   - The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:  
   - The authentication request is evaluated against Entra Conditional Access policies. This step involves three sub-processes:
     - **Signal**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are analyzed. Examples include:
       - Users and groups.
       - Network location.
     - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
     - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, which could result in granting or denying access, or requiring additional authentication steps.

3. **Access to Microsoft Fabric**:  
   - If access is granted, the user is allowed into Microsoft Fabric.  
   - Microsoft Fabric includes various components and services, such as:
     - Data Factory.
     - Synapse.
     - Power BI.
     - OneLake (a unified data lake for storage).

This diagram effectively demonstrates the integration of Entra Conditional Access policies with Microsoft Fabric to ensure secure and conditional access to its services.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and analytics workflow within the Azure ecosystem, leveraging UMS Fabric, Data Factory, OneLake, and Power BI.

1. **Data Source**:
   - **UMS Staging Area**: The process begins with data ingestion from a Microsoft SQL Server 2017-based data warehouse, labeled as the UMS Staging Area.

2. **Data Ingestion**:
   - **Data Factory**: Azure Data Factory is used to ingest data from the UMS Staging Area into the UMS Fabric environment.

3. **Data Lakehouse Layers**:
   - **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**: The ingested raw data is stored in the Bronze layer of the lakehouse, representing the initial, unprocessed data.
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Data is then transformed and cleaned, moving to the Silver layer for further processing and enrichment.
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The final, curated, and business-ready data is stored in the Gold layer for consumption.

4. **Data Processing and Enrichment**:
   - **Data Factory**: Azure Data Factory is used for orchestrating data movement and transformation between the Bronze, Silver, and Gold layers.
   - **Data Science**: Advanced analytics and machine learning models are applied to the data in the Silver layer to derive insights and predictions.

5. **Identity Management**:
   - **Identity_SM**: A component for managing identity and security, ensuring secure access to the Gold layer data.

6. **Data Storage**:
   - **OneLake**: The data is stored in OneLake, a unified data lake storage solution, enabling seamless integration and accessibility.

7. **Data Visualization**:
   - **Power BI**: The curated data in the Gold layer is connected to Power BI via OneLake for visualization and reporting, enabling business users to derive actionable insights.

This architecture demonstrates a modern data lakehouse approach, integrating Azure services for scalable, secure, and efficient data processing and analytics.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a data flow between three components:

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:
   - Represents an on-premises SQL Server 2017 database used as a staging area or data warehouse.
   - It stores and processes data before it is transferred to other systems.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:
   - Acts as a bridge between the on-premises SQL Server and cloud-based services.
   - Facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened in the on-premises network.
   - Establishes an outbound secure connection to the cloud, ensuring data security and compliance.

3. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Represents a cloud-based service or platform that consumes data from the on-premises SQL Server via the On-Premise Data Gateway.
   - The UMS Fabric likely provides advanced data processing, analytics, or integration capabilities.

**Data Flow**:
- Data flows from the SQL Server database to the On-Premise Data Gateway.
- The On-Premise Data Gateway securely transmits the data to the UMS Fabric using an outbound connection.
- The UMS Fabric processes or utilizes the data for further operations.

This architecture ensures secure and seamless integration between on-premises and cloud environments.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure-based ecosystem.

### Components and Data Flow:
1. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Acts as the overarching framework for the workflow, encompassing all components.

2. **Data Source**:
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Represents the intermediate data layer (Silver tables) stored in **OneLake**. This serves as the input data for machine learning processes.

3. **Machine Learning Workflows**:
   - **AutoML**:
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Automates the machine learning process, including model selection and hyperparameter tuning.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: Outputs the scored results from the AutoML experiment for further processing.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modeling (Using Notebook)**: Allows for custom model development and experimentation using notebooks.

4. **Data Transformation and Storage**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Represents the refined data layer (Gold tables) where processed and scored data is stored for downstream consumption.
   - **Identity_SM**: Likely a specialized service or module for identity management or data enrichment.

5. **Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**:
     - Connected to the **XX_Gold_LK Tables** via **OneLake** for real-time data visualization and reporting.

### Connections:
- Data flows from **XX_Silver_LK Tables** to either the **AutoML** or **Manual Machine Learning** workflows.
- Processed data from both workflows is stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**.
- **Power BI** accesses the **XX_Gold_LK Tables** through **OneLake** for visualization purposes.

This architecture demonstrates a hybrid approach to machine learning, combining automated and manual processes, with seamless integration into a unified data lake and visualization platform.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides an overview of the project, the purpose of this document, and the intended audience for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s Power BI & Fabric solution design. ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is undertaking a strategic initiative to modernize its data analytics capabilities by leveraging Microsoft Power BI and Microsoft Fabric. The primary business objective is to enable robust, end-to-end data workflows—from ingestion to transformation and visualization—within Microsoft Fabric, tailored to UMS’s business use cases. The project scope includes: - Establishing a dedicated Microsoft Fabric workspace for UMS. - Designing and implementing an end-to-end ETL pipeline to streamline data integration and orchestration using Fabric Data Factory. - Conducting advanced analytics and big data processing via Fabric Synapse. - Enabling machine learning applications through Fabric’s Data Science services. - Integrating Power BI for business-aligned analytics and visualization. This initiative supports UMS’s cloud adoption strategy, ensuring alignment with Microsoft Fabric’s capabilities and best practices for data-driven decision-making. *Responsibility Assignment:* Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting the solution design and technical architecture. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for reviewing and validating the proposed design in alignment with business requirements. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The purpose of this Solution Design Document is to formally define the technical architecture, workflow design, and implementation approach for UMS’s Power BI and Microsoft Fabric deployment. This document will: - Provide authoritative guidance on the integration and configuration of Microsoft Fabric components, including Data Factory, Synapse, and Data Science. - Specify design decisions, technical standards, and operational responsibilities. - Serve as the baseline for subsequent implementation, migration, and governance activities. *Responsibility Assignment:* Enfrasys Solutions will author and maintain the Solution Design Document. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall review, provide feedback, and approve the document prior to execution. ## 2.3 Document Audience This document is intended for the following stakeholders: - Enfrasys Solutions (Consulting and Implementation Team) - Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) IT Department - Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Business Stakeholders All parties are expected to contribute to the review and validation process to ensure the solution meets UMS’s business and technical objectives. *Responsibility Assignment:* Enfrasys Solutions drafts the documentation. UMS IT and business stakeholders review and validate the content. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Overview This section provides an overview of Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, including their definitions, core features, and their roles in Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) data workflow. Supporting technologies such as Entra ID and On-Premises Data Gateway are also defined. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric ### Definition Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, data integration, and business intelligence within a single cloud-based environment. Fabric provides a comprehensive suite of services, including Data Factory for orchestration, Synapse for advanced analytics, OneLake for unified data storage, and built-in machine learning capabilities. ### Core Features - **Data Factory**: Enables robust data integration and orchestration, allowing seamless ingestion and transformation of data from diverse sources. - **Synapse**: Offers advanced analytics and big data processing, supporting real-time and batch analytics workloads. - **OneLake**: Serves as a unified data lake, consolidating storage across the Fabric environment for easy access and management. - **Data Science & AutoML**: Facilitates machine learning workflows, including automated model training and deployment, as well as manual experimentation. - **End-to-End ETL Pipeline**: Supports extraction, transformation, and loading processes, ensuring data flows efficiently from source to visualization. - **Workspace Management**: Organizes resources and data assets into logical workspaces for collaboration and governance. Enfrasys Solutions provides the technical definition and recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) review the scope and applicability of Fabric’s features for their business use case. ### Reference Architecture Figure 1 below illustrates the data workflow architecture within UMS Fabric, showing data ingestion from the UMS Staging Area (SQL Server 2017), processing through Bronze, Silver, and Gold data layers, integration with Data Science and AutoML, unified storage via OneLake, and visualization in Power BI. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\da369824-31b3-4e84-a8ad-6b429e736795\images\slide_009_img_003.png) **Figure 1:** UMS Fabric Data Workflow Architecture ### AutoML Workflow Integration UMS Fabric supports both automated and manual machine learning workflows, leveraging OneLake for data storage and Power BI for visualization. It offers interactive dashboards, real-time analytics, and integration with a wide range of data sources, including Microsoft Fabric. ### Integration Points with Fabric - **Direct Integration with OneLake**: Power BI connects seamlessly to Fabric’s OneLake, enabling real-time access to curated data layers (Gold Layer) for reporting and analytics. - **ETL Pipeline Output**: Power BI consumes data processed through Fabric’s ETL pipelines, ensuring business users access reliable, business-ready datasets. - **Machine Learning Insights**: Results from Fabric’s AutoML and manual ML workflows are visualized in Power BI, supporting advanced business analytics. Enfrasys Solutions provides the technical definition and recommends that UMS review Power BI’s integration points with Fabric to maximize business analytics capabilities. ### Business Analytics Role Power BI empowers UMS stakeholders to: - Create interactive dashboards and reports. - Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) derived from Fabric’s curated datasets. - Enable self-service analytics for business units. - Facilitate data-driven decision-making across the organization. ## 3.3 Supporting Technologies ### Entra ID **Definition:** Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is a cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides authentication, authorization, and governance for users, applications, and resources across Microsoft cloud services, including Fabric and Power BI. Enfrasys Solutions provides the technical definition and recommends that UMS review Entra ID configurations to ensure secure access to Fabric and Power BI environments. ### On-Premises Data Gateway **Definition:** The Microsoft On-Premises Data Gateway is a secure bridge that enables cloud services such as Power BI, Fabric Data Factory, and Logic Apps to access on-premises data sources (e.g., SQL Server, Oracle) without exposing them directly to the internet. Enfrasys Solutions provides the technical definition and recommends that UMS review gateway deployment and configuration to support hybrid data workflows. --- **Responsibility Assignment:** - Enfrasys Solutions: Provide technical definitions and architecture recommendations. - Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS): Review and validate technology scope, integration points, and workflow applicability. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture and connectivity decisions for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), focusing on secure integration between on-premises SQL Server, the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM, and the UMS Fabric platform. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Enfrasys Solutions recommends a network topology that ensures secure, reliable, and high-performance connectivity between UMS’s on-premises SQL Server environment, the OPDG virtual machine, and the UMS Fabric hosted in Azure. The connectivity model is designed as follows: - The on-premises SQL Server 2017 database (staging area/data warehouse) is the primary data source. - The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM acts as a secure bridge, facilitating outbound data transfer from the SQL Server to UMS Fabric. - The UMS Fabric platform, hosted within Azure, consumes data from the OPDG for analytics, reporting, and integration. Key design principles include: - No inbound ports are opened on the UMS network, minimizing exposure and enhancing security. - All data transfer is initiated outbound from OPDG, leveraging secure, encrypted channels. - Conditional Access policies are enforced at the entry point to UMS Fabric, ensuring that only authorized users and systems can access cloud resources. UMS is responsible for providing detailed information on the on-premises network, SQL Server configuration, and firewall policies to enable accurate gateway placement and connectivity validation. Figure 1 below illustrates the data gateway architecture and connectivity flow: ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\da369824-31b3-4e84-a8ad-6b429e736795\images\slide_011_img_004.png) *Figure 1: Data flow between UMS SQL Server, OPDG VM, and UMS Fabric.* ## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway ### Definition The Microsoft On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a secure, lightweight agent that enables cloud services (such as Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure Logic Apps) to access data residing in on-premises environments. The gateway establishes outbound connections from the local network to Azure, ensuring that data transfers are encrypted and compliant with organizational security policies. No inbound ports are required, reducing attack surface and simplifying firewall management. ### Role in UMS Architecture Enfrasys Solutions will design and deploy the OPDG agent within a dedicated virtual machine, which will: - Authenticate and securely connect to the UMS on-premises SQL Server 2017 database. - Facilitate data transfer to UMS Fabric for analytics and reporting workloads. - Operate as a managed bridge, ensuring data privacy, compliance, and operational integrity. UMS shall provide all necessary details regarding the SQL Server instance, network topology, and access requirements to enable proper gateway configuration and connectivity testing. ### VM Specification The OPDG VM must meet the following minimum specifications to ensure optimal performance and reliability: | Component | Specification | |------------------|-----------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing and provisioning the OPDG VM in accordance with these specifications. UMS is responsible for ensuring that the VM can access the on-premises SQL Server and that all relevant firewall rules and network policies are in place. ### Task/Migration Rollout | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|---------------| | On-premises Network Review | Provide network and SQL Server details to Enfrasys | UMS | Pending | | OPDG VM Provisioning | Design and deploy OPDG VM in Azure or on-premises | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Gateway Agent Installation | Install and configure OPDG agent on VM | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Connectivity Testing | Validate secure data transfer between SQL Server and Fabric | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Firewall Policy Adjustment | Update firewall rules as required for outbound connectivity | UMS | Pending | ## 4.3 Architecture Diagram: Conditional Access Integration Figure 2 below demonstrates how Conditional Access policies are applied to secure inbound access to UMS Fabric, ensuring only authorized users and systems can interact with cloud-hosted data and analytics services. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\da369824-31b3-4e84-a8ad-6b429e736795\images\slide_006_img_002.png) *Figure 2: Conditional Access enforcement for Microsoft Fabric services.* --- Enfrasys Solutions will lead all design, deployment, and validation activities. Fabric enables organizations to manage data workflows, analytics, and governance across multiple workloads. The Fabric Administrator is responsible for overseeing the configuration, governance, and security of the Fabric environment. NSGs consist of rules that define allowed or denied traffic based on source/destination, port, and protocol. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS implement NSG outbound rules for the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM to ensure secure connectivity to Microsoft cloud services. The table below presents the outbound NSG rules required for the OPDG VM: | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |--------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services| 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication| 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for specifying and deploying these NSG rules. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall review and validate compliance with their internal security policies. ## 7.2 Encryption Design ### Microsoft Fabric Encryption Overview Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. Security is a core design principle, and Microsoft Fabric enforces encryption for data both at rest and in transit. ### Power BI Encryption Overview Power BI is a business analytics service that enables data visualization and sharing. All data processed by Power BI is encrypted at rest and in transit using industry-standard protocols. ### SSL/TLS for Gateway Services The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) facilitates secure connectivity between on-premises data sources and Microsoft cloud services. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS enforce SSL/TLS encryption for all outbound communications from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID endpoints. This ensures that sensitive data is protected from interception and tampering during transit. #### Encryption at Rest and In Transit - **Encryption at Rest:** All data stored within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI is automatically encrypted using Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) and Transparent Data Encryption (TDE). - **Encryption in Transit:** Communications between the OPDG VM and Microsoft cloud services are secured using TLS 1.2 or higher. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for specifying encryption configurations and validating gateway SSL/TLS settings. UMS shall review compliance and ensure alignment with Universiti Malaysia Sabah's data protection standards. ## 7.3 Security Responsibilities | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------------------------------------- |--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------|------------| | NSG Rule Specification and Deployment | Define and implement outbound NSG rules for OPDG VM | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Encryption Configuration | Configure SSL/TLS for gateway, validate encryption at rest and in transit | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Compliance Review | Review NSG and encryption settings for compliance with UMS security policy | Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) | Pending | ## 7.4 Security Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-----------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | OPDG VM outbound NSG rules | Allow only HTTPS/HTTP traffic to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, Entra ID, and connectivity endpoints | | 2 | Gateway SSL/TLS enforcement | Require SSL/TLS for all outbound gateway communications | | 3 | Encryption at rest and in transit | Leverage Microsoft-managed encryption for all data processed by Fabric and Power BI | ## Enfrasys Solutions will provide initial support, documentation, and training, followed by handover to UMS for ongoing management. | No | Activities | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|-----------------------------------------------|------------------|------------|-----------------------------------| | 1 | Operational monitoring and health checks | Enfrasys | Pending | | | 2 | Knowledge transfer and administrator training | Enfrasys, UMS | Pending | | | 3 | Issue resolution and escalation | Enfrasys, UMS | Pending | | Enfrasys Solutions will monitor the environment post-migration, resolve any outstanding issues, and conduct knowledge transfer sessions. UMS shall participate in training and assume responsibility for day-to-day operations upon completion of the support period. # 9.0 Appendix This section provides supplementary technical references, prerequisites, and boundary conditions for each major domain supporting Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) solution design. Enfrasys Solutions has drafted the following appendices; UMS shall review and validate applicability to their environment. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah review the official Microsoft Azure Compute documentation to understand supported VM types, prerequisites for deployment, and regional availability. Key prerequisites include Azure subscription access, quota approvals, and network connectivity. - [Azure Virtual Machines documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/) - [Azure Compute Resource Providers](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/resource-provider-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries It is critical for UMS to be aware of the current Azure compute limits and quotas, which may affect VM deployments, scale sets, and resource allocation. - [Azure subscription and service limits, quotas, and constraints – Compute](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits#compute-limits) ### Others Additional resources for troubleshooting, best practices, and performance optimization: - [Azure VM troubleshooting guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/troubleshooting/) - [Azure VM best practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/performance) ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Enfrasys Solutions advises UMS to familiarize themselves with Azure networking fundamentals, including virtual networks, subnets, and network security groups (NSGs). Prerequisites include proper subscription configuration and understanding of Azure networking topologies. - [Azure Virtual Network documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/) - [Azure Networking Overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/networking/) ### Limits and Boundaries UMS must ensure compliance with Azure network quotas and boundaries, such as maximum number of VNETs, subnets, and NSG rules per subscription. - [Azure subscription and service limits, quotas, and constraints – Networking](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits#networking-limits) ### Others Additional resources for network troubleshooting and advanced configuration: - [Azure Network Watcher documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/network-watcher/) - [Azure ExpressRoute documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/) ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS review Azure identity and security prerequisites, including Azure Active Directory (Entra ID), role-based access control (RBAC), and conditional access policies. - [Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/) - [Azure RBAC documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/) ### Limits and Boundaries UMS should be aware of Azure identity and security limits, such as maximum directory objects, application registrations, and policy boundaries. - [Azure AD service limits and restrictions](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/azure-ad-service-limits) ### Others Additional resources for security best practices and compliance: - [Azure Security Center documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/) - [Azure AD troubleshooting](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/troubleshoot/) ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS implement Azure logging and monitoring prerequisites, including Log Analytics workspace setup, diagnostic settings, and monitoring agent deployment. - [Azure Monitor documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/) - [Azure Log Analytics documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries UMS must adhere to Azure logging and monitoring boundaries, such as workspace retention limits, data ingestion quotas, and alert thresholds. - [Azure Monitor limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/service-limits) ### Others Additional resources for monitoring best practices and troubleshooting: - [Azure Monitor troubleshooting guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/troubleshoot/) - [Azure Application Insights documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/app-insights-overview) ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS review Azure governance prerequisites, including management groups, policies, and cost management setup. - [Azure Governance documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/) - [Azure Policy documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/) ### Limits and Boundaries UMS must be aware of Azure governance boundaries, such as maximum management groups, policy assignment limits, and cost management constraints. - [Azure Management Group limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#management-group-tree-limits) - [Azure Policy limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/overview#limits) ### Others Additional resources for governance best practices and compliance: - [Azure Cost Management documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/) - [Azure Blueprints documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/) ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Fabric-specific ### Introduction/Prerequisites Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS review Microsoft Fabric prerequisites, including workspace setup, data integration requirements, and supported connectors. - [Microsoft Fabric documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/) - [Microsoft Fabric workspace overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/workspaces/overview) ### Limits and Boundaries UMS must consider Microsoft Fabric boundaries, such as workspace limits, dataset size restrictions, and connector quotas. - [Microsoft Fabric service limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/service-limits) - [Microsoft Fabric workspace limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/workspaces/limits) ### Others Additional resources for Fabric troubleshooting, best practices, and roadmap: -
