Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall maintain user assignments and update personnel as organizational requirements evolve. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision This section details the design, workflow, access, and governance decisions for the Microsoft Fabric implementation at Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a single SaaS solution. It integrates with OneLake, a unified data lake, enabling seamless data management and analytics. The following table summarizes the data workflow considerations for ETL and ML workloads in UMS’s environment. | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | | DE01 | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation. | Data Engineering | | DE02 | The Project will primarily adopt the Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python) to enable custom feature engineering, algorithm tuning, and model optimization. | Data Science | | DE03 | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment to achieve more accurate and explainable results tailored to UMS’s datasets. | Data Science | | DE04 | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry to support model lifecycle management, versioning, and potential reuse across further adoption. | Data Science | | DE05 | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data. The scored outputs, predictions, probabilities, and performance metrics will be stored in the Gold Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DE06 | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results, ensuring consistency between AutoML and manual ML outputs. | Data Engineering | | DE07 | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) and Data Modelling will be implemented | Power BI | | DE08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | Figure 1 below illustrates the end-to-end data workflow architecture, including ETL, ML, and reporting integration: ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\4b5330db-9bf5-41e0-8c1c-bffb0dfb6627\images\slide_009_img_003.png) ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01| All ETL pipelines will utilize Azure Data Factory for ingesting and moving data between Lakehouse layers. | Adopt Azure Data Factory for pipeline orchestration. | | DD02| Data cleansing and transformation will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and Visual Query in SQL Analytics Endpoint.| Implement Dataflow Gen2 and Notebooks for transformation. | | DD03| Machine Learning will be executed using both AutoML and manual approaches within Fabric Notebooks. | Enable both AutoML and manual ML workflows. | | DD04| Model outputs and scoring will be stored in the Gold Lakehouse for standardized reporting and analytics. | Store ML results in Gold Lakehouse. | | DD05| Semantic Models will be built on Gold Lakehouse and exposed to Power BI for visualization. | Connect Power BI to Semantic Models via OneLake. | | DD06| Model registry will be used for lifecycle management and versioning of ML models. | Register models in Fabric Model Registry. | Figure 2 below demonstrates the ML workflow design, including AutoML and manual ML integration: ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\4b5330db-9bf5-41e0-8c1c-bffb0dfb6627\images\slide_021_img_005.png) ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Conditional Access Microsoft Entra ID is Microsoft's cloud-based identity and access management service, providing authentication, authorization, and security controls for Microsoft 365 and Azure services. Conditional Access policies allow organizations to enforce access controls based on user, location, device, and risk signals. | No. | Conditional Access | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | | DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | | DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | | DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | Figure 3 below illustrates the conditional access architecture for UMS Fabric: ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\4b5330db-9bf5-41e0-8c1c-bffb0dfb6627\images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 4 below shows the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies: ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\4b5330db-9bf5-41e0-8c1c-bffb0dfb6627\images\slide_006_img_002.png) ### MFA Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall enforce Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) at the tenant level for all users accessing Microsoft Fabric, as Conditional Access is not currently eligible for UMS’s account. Enfrasys Solutions recommends future evaluation of Conditional Access with additional licensing (Entra ID P1/P2). ### Workspace Permissions Enfrasys Solutions will design workspace-level permissions based on Microsoft Fabric role definitions. UMS shall assign personnel to roles as follows: | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS Personnel | |-----|---------------|---------------|---------------| | 1 | Admin | Data Owner | To be nominated by UMS | | 2 | Contributor | Data Engineer | To be nominated by UMS | | 3 | Member | Analyst | To be nominated by UMS | | 4 | Viewer | Stakeholder | To be nominated by UMS | ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size | Component | Specification | |------------------|--------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | OPDG (Operational Data Gateway) VM sizing is recommended as above for optimal performance in ETL and reporting workloads. ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Enfrasys Solutions recommends organizing Azure resources under management groups to enable policy enforcement, cost tracking, and operational separation for UMS’s Fabric workloads. ### Naming and Tagging Enfrasys Solutions will design and implement naming conventions for Azure and Fabric resources, while UMS shall operationalize tagging for cost and ownership tracking. #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |--------------------|-------------|------------------------| | Resource Group | RG | Fabric-RG | | Virtual Machine | VM | OPDG-VM | | Storage Account | SA | umsdatastorage01 | | Data Factory | DF | umsdatafactory01 | | Key Vault | KV | umskeyvault01 | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming -- | |-----|--------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK
Etc… | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF
Etc.. | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL
Etc.. | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | Abbreviation | Meaning | |--------------|-----------------------| | RG | Resource Group | | VM | Virtual Machine | | SA | Storage Account | | DF | Data Factory | | KV | Key Vault | | LK | Lakehouse | | DF | Dataflow | | SM | Semantic Model | | PL | Pipeline | ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### Governance Disciplines Enfrasys Solutions recommends adopting the following governance disciplines for Microsoft Fabric: | Azure Tool | Description | Cost Management Discipline | |----------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------| | Azure Cost Management| Monitor, analyze, and optimize cloud spend | Budgeting, cost allocation | | Azure Policy | Enforce compliance and resource standards | Resource consistency | | Azure Monitor | Track performance, usage, and operational health | Operational visibility | | Azure Security Center| Assess and improve security posture | Security compliance | UMS shall operationalize these tools for ongoing governance. ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |---------------------|----------------------------------------------------|--------------------|---------------| | Policy Violation | Detect non-compliant resource configuration | Enfrasys Solutions | Design | | Cost Overrun | Alert when budget threshold exceeded | UMS | Operational | | Security Incident | Investigate and remediate security alerts | UMS | Operational | | Resource Drift | Identify unauthorized changes to resource setup | Enfrasys Solutions | Design | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for governance design; Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for operational adoption and incident response. # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section details the security architecture and operational controls for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Azure-hosted On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine and associated Microsoft Fabric workloads. ## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are fundamental security constructs within Microsoft Azure, designed to filter network traffic to and from Azure resources based on user-defined rules. NSGs allow administrators to define granular inbound and outbound security policies, ensuring only authorized traffic is permitted and all communication adheres to organizational security standards. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah implement the following NSG outbound rules for the OPDG VM, ensuring secure connectivity to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft cloud identity services. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for NSG configuration, while UMS shall monitor ongoing compliance. | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |----------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ## 7.2 Encryption Design ### SSL/TLS Encryption Overview SSL (Secure Sockets Layer) and TLS (Transport Layer Security) are industry-standard cryptographic protocols that provide secure communications over computer networks. SSL/TLS ensures that data transmitted between clients, servers, and cloud endpoints is encrypted and protected from interception or tampering. ### Data-at-Rest Encryption Data-at-rest encryption refers to the protection of stored data using cryptographic algorithms. In Azure, this is achieved through technologies such as Azure Storage Service Encryption and Azure Disk Encryption, which utilize strong encryption standards (e.g., AES-256) to safeguard all data stored within virtual disks and cloud storage. ### Data-in-Transit Encryption Data-in-transit encryption ensures that all data exchanged between the OPDG VM, Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI endpoints is encrypted using HTTPS (TLS 1.2 or higher). This protects sensitive information from eavesdropping and unauthorized access during transmission. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure all SSL/TLS settings, enforce HTTPS for all outbound traffic, and enable Azure-native encryption for both data-at-rest and data-in-transit. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for continuous compliance monitoring and periodic security reviews to ensure adherence to regulatory and organizational requirements. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the phased deployment and migration strategy for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), including pre-migration setup, pilot execution, production rollout, and post-migration support. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup (D-7 Timeline) Enfrasys Solutions recommends a structured pre-migration phase to ensure operational readiness and minimize risk. The following table details the preparatory tasks, ownership, and planned start dates. | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|--------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------------|-------------|--------------------------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | UMS shall ensure all prerequisites are met and provide confirmation on server readiness. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for environment setup and gateway installation. ## 8.2 Pilot The pilot phase is designed to validate the migration approach and ensure system stability prior to full production rollout. Enfrasys Solutions shall provision a dedicated pilot environment, replicating the production configuration with limited data and user access. UMS is responsible for nominating pilot users, validating data, and confirming operational workflows. Enfrasys Solutions will monitor pilot performance, address issues, and document outcomes. Pilot tasks include: - Environment provisioning (Enfrasys Solutions) - User access assignment (UMS) - Data migration validation (Enfrasys Solutions, UMS) - Feedback collection and issue resolution (Enfrasys Solutions, UMS) ## 8.3 Production (D0 Timeline) The production phase marks the official go-live of the migrated workloads. Enfrasys Solutions shall execute the production cutover, migrating all relevant data and activating the production environment. UMS is responsible for operational readiness, user communication, and post-cutover validation. Production tasks include: - Final data migration and cutover (Enfrasys Solutions) - Production environment activation (Enfrasys Solutions) - User onboarding and communication (UMS) - Post-cutover validation and acceptance (UMS) All production activities are scheduled for D0, with Enfrasys Solutions leading deployment and UMS ensuring readiness. ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support (D+1 Timeline) Post-migration support is critical to ensure system stability and user satisfaction. Enfrasys Solutions shall provide immediate post-migration support, monitoring system health, and resolving any issues encountered. UMS is responsible for ongoing operational management and user support escalation. Post-migration tasks include: - System monitoring and health checks (Enfrasys Solutions) - Issue resolution and troubleshooting (Enfrasys Solutions) - User support and escalation (UMS) - Documentation and knowledge transfer (Enfrasys Solutions, UMS) Support activities commence at D+1 and continue until system stabilization and sign-off by UMS. # 9.0 Appendix This section provides reference links and documentation for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) and Enfrasys Solutions to support solution implementation, review, and ongoing governance. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Machines: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Subscription Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits ### Others Azure Compute SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/virtual-machines/ ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Network: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Networking Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-network-limits ### Others Azure Networking SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/virtual-network/ ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD): https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/ ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Entra ID Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-users/limits ### Others Microsoft Entra ID SLA: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/active-directory/ ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/platform/limits ### Others Azure Monitor SLA: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/monitor/ ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Governance Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Policy Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/definition-structure ### Others Azure Resource Manager SLA: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/resource-manager/ ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Microsoft Fabric-specific ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/ ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric Capacity Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/service-limits ### Others Microsoft Fabric SLA: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/fabric/

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

**Description:**

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving Azure Resource Groups, a UMS Fabric service, and Conditional Access for external users. The components and their interactions are as follows:

1. **Fabric-RG (Azure Resource Group):**
   - Represents an Azure Resource Group that contains and organizes resources related to the UMS Fabric service.
   - The Resource Group acts as a logical container for managing and deploying resources in Azure.

2. **UMS Fabric:**
   - A service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.
   - Likely represents a unified management system or platform that provides specific functionalities or services to users.

3. **Conditional Access:**
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric service.
   - Ensures that only authorized users, based on predefined policies, can access the system.

4. **External User:**
   - Represents an external entity or user attempting to access the UMS Fabric service.
   - Access is mediated through Conditional Access policies to ensure security and compliance.

5. **Data Flow:**
   - The arrow indicates a unidirectional flow of access or interaction from the external user to the UMS Fabric service.
   - The Conditional Access layer acts as a gatekeeper, enforcing security policies before granting access to the UMS Fabric.

This architecture emphasizes secure access control for external users interacting with the UMS Fabric service hosted in Azure.

This image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**:  
   - The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:  
   - The authentication request is evaluated against Entra Conditional Access policies.  
   - **Signal (Step 3)**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are analyzed.  
   - **Decision (Step 4)**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.  
   - **Enforcement (Step 5)**: The decision is enforced, allowing or denying access based on the policy configuration.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**:  
   - If access is granted, the user is directed to Microsoft Fabric.  
   - Microsoft Fabric includes various components such as data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence tools.  
   - **OneLake**: A unified data lake is highlighted as part of the Microsoft Fabric ecosystem, serving as a central repository for data storage and management.

This diagram effectively demonstrates the integration of conditional access policies with Microsoft Fabric to ensure secure and policy-driven access control.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

### Description of the Architecture:

1. **Data Source:**
   - The process begins with data ingestion from the **UMS Staging Area**, which is hosted on **Microsoft SQL Server 2017** and serves as the **Data Warehouse**.

2. **Data Ingestion:**
   - **Azure Data Factory** is used to ingest data from the staging area into the system. This is the first step in the data pipeline.

3. **UMS Fabric:**
   - The architecture operates within the **UMS Fabric**, which is the overarching framework for data processing and management.

4. **Bronze Layer (XX_Bronze_LK Tables):**
   - The ingested raw data is stored in the **Bronze Layer**. This layer contains unprocessed or minimally processed data.

5. **Silver Layer (XX_Silver_LK Tables):**
   - Data from the Bronze Layer is transformed and refined into the **Silver Layer**. This layer contains cleaned and enriched data, ready for further processing.

6. **Data Science and Transformation:**
   - **Azure Data Factory** and **Data Science tools** are used to process and analyze the data in the Silver Layer. This step involves advanced analytics, machine learning, or other data science operations.

7. **Gold Layer (XX_Gold_LK Tables):**
   - The processed and finalized data is stored in the **Gold Layer**. This layer contains high-quality, curated data ready for consumption.

8. **Identity Management (Identity_SM):**
   - The Gold Layer integrates with **Identity_SM**, which likely handles identity management, security, or metadata tagging for the data.

9. **OneLake Integration:**
   - The data is connected to **OneLake**, a unified data lake solution, enabling seamless data access and integration.

10. **Power BI:**
    - The final curated data is visualized and analyzed using **Power BI**, which connects to the Gold Layer via OneLake.

### Data Flow:
- Data flows sequentially from the **UMS Staging Area** to the **Bronze Layer**, then to the **Silver Layer**, and finally to the **Gold Layer**.
- Each layer involves specific transformations and processing steps, with **Azure Data Factory** orchestrating the data movement and processing.
- The final output is consumed by **Power BI** for reporting and visualization.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a data flow between three main components:

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:
   - Represents an on-premises SQL Server 2017 database used as a staging area or data warehouse.
   - It stores and processes data before it is transferred to other systems.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:
   - Acts as a bridge between the on-premises SQL Server and cloud-based services.
   - Facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened in the on-premises network.
   - Establishes an outbound secure connection to the cloud, ensuring data security and compliance.

3. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Represents a cloud-based service or platform that consumes data from the on-premises SQL Server via the On-Premise Data Gateway.
   - The UMS Fabric likely provides advanced data processing, analytics, or integration capabilities.

**Data Flow**:
- Data flows from the SQL Server database to the On-Premise Data Gateway.
- The On-Premise Data Gateway securely transmits the data to the UMS Fabric using an outbound connection.
- The UMS Fabric processes or integrates the data for further use.

This architecture ensures secure and efficient data transfer between on-premises and cloud environments without compromising network security.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

### Description:

1. **Data Source:**
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Represents the intermediate data layer stored in OneLake, part of the UMS Fabric ecosystem. This serves as the input data source for machine learning processes.

2. **Machine Learning Processes:**
   - **AutoML**:
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Automated machine learning experiments are conducted on the data from XX_Silver_LK Tables.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results of the AutoML experiments are scored and processed using notebooks.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modeling (Using Notebook)**: Manual machine learning models are developed and trained using notebooks, leveraging the same XX_Silver_LK Tables as input.

3. **Data Output:**
   - The outputs from both AutoML and Manual Machine Learning processes are stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**, representing the refined and processed data layer.

4. **Identity Management:**
   - **Identity_SM**: A component for identity or metadata management, integrated with the XX_Gold_LK Tables for further processing or governance.

5. **Visualization and Reporting:**
   - The XX_Gold_LK Tables are connected to **Power BI** via **OneLake**, enabling data visualization and reporting.

6. **Integration:**
   - The entire workflow is encapsulated within the **UMS Fabric**, ensuring seamless integration and data flow across components.

This architecture demonstrates a hybrid approach to machine learning, combining automated and manual processes, with a focus on data refinement, identity management, and visualization.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the project scope, objectives, and stakeholders for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s Microsoft Fabric and Power BI solution. ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) has initiated a strategic project to modernize its data workflow and analytics capabilities. The scope encompasses designing a comprehensive data workflow from ingestion through transformation and visualization, leveraging Microsoft Fabric. Key objectives include: - Establishing a dedicated Fabric workspace for UMS. - Developing an end-to-end ETL pipeline to streamline data integration. - Conducting machine learning processes to enable advanced analytics. - Integrating Power BI for business-aligned data visualization and reporting. - Aligning UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s core services: Fabric Data Factory (data integration and orchestration), Fabric Synapse (advanced analytics and big data processing), and Data Science (machine learning application). ## 2.2 Document Purpose This Solution Design Document formally details the architectural, technical, and operational blueprint for the implementation of Microsoft Fabric and Power BI at Universiti Malaysia Sabah. The document is intended to: - Provide authoritative guidance on technology selection and design decisions. - Serve as the reference for solution implementation, migration, and operational management. - Facilitate review, approval, and sign-off by both Universiti Malaysia Sabah and Enfrasys Solutions. ## 2.3 Document Audience The Solution Design Document is intended for the following stakeholders: - Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) management and project sponsors. - UMS technical teams responsible for implementation and ongoing operations. - Enfrasys Solutions consultants and architects leading the design and deployment. - Business users and analytics stakeholders utilizing the Power BI and Fabric platform. Responsibility for review and sign-off of this document is jointly assigned to Universiti Malaysia Sabah and Enfrasys Solutions. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric, Power BI & Entra ID Overview This section provides formal definitions of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID, followed by their application to Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) data workflow and analytics requirements. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Overview Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that consolidates data integration, engineering, warehousing, science, and business intelligence into a single SaaS solution. Fabric streamlines enterprise data workflows by providing a suite of core services: - **Data Factory**: Enables robust data integration and orchestration, supporting ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) pipelines across diverse sources. - **Synapse**: Delivers advanced analytics and big data processing capabilities, including data warehousing, real-time analytics, and Spark-based workloads. - **Data Science**: Facilitates machine learning development, model training, and operationalization within the same workspace. - **Lakehouse**: Combines the scalability of data lakes with the structure of data warehouses, supporting both analytics and AI workloads. Enfrasys Solutions provides these technical definitions and recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah validate their business relevance for UMS’s specific analytics and data transformation needs. | ID | Description | Workload Type | |----|-------------|--------------| | 1 | Unified analytics platform integrating ETL, warehousing, ML, and BI | Enterprise Data Workflow | | 2 | Data Factory for ETL pipeline orchestration | Data Integration | | 3 | Synapse for big data analytics and warehousing | Analytics & Reporting | | 4 | Data Science for ML model development | AI/ML | | 5 | Lakehouse for scalable storage and analytics | Data Storage & Analytics | ## 3.2 Power BI Overview Power BI is a business analytics service by Microsoft that enables organizations to visualize data, share insights, and make informed decisions. It integrates seamlessly with Microsoft Fabric, leveraging datasets, models, and dataflows for enhanced reporting and analytics: - **Business Analytics**: Power BI transforms raw data into interactive dashboards and reports, enabling actionable insights for stakeholders. - **Fabric Integration**: Power BI connects directly to Fabric workspaces, allowing real-time visualization of data processed through Data Factory, Synapse, and Lakehouse. - **Collaboration**: Supports secure sharing and collaboration across teams, ensuring data-driven decision-making. Enfrasys Solutions provides the technical overview, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall validate Power BI’s alignment with UMS’s business intelligence objectives. | ID | Description | Workload Type | |----|-------------|--------------| | 1 | Business analytics platform for visualization and reporting | Analytics | | 2 | Real-time integration with Fabric datasets and models | Data Visualization | | 3 | Interactive dashboards and collaborative reporting | Decision Support | | 4 | Secure sharing and access controls | Governance | ## 3.3 Entra ID Overview Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is a cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides authentication, authorization, and Conditional Access for users, applications, and resources across Microsoft 365 and Azure environments: - **Authentication**: Centralizes user identity management, enabling Single Sign-On (SSO) across Microsoft services. - **Conditional Access**: Enforces security policies based on user, device, location, and risk factors. - **Integration**: Powers secure access to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, ensuring compliance and governance. Enfrasys Solutions defines the technical capabilities, and Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for validating Entra ID’s relevance to UMS’s security and compliance requirements. | ID | Description | Workload Type | |----|-------------|--------------| | 1 | Identity and access management service | Security | | 2 | Authentication and Single Sign-On (SSO) | User Access | | 3 | Conditional Access policy enforcement | Compliance | | 4 | Integration with Fabric and Power BI | Platform Security | # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture, hybrid connectivity, and gateway deployment for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) to support secure integration between on-premises and Microsoft Fabric workloads. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) requires a secure and scalable network architecture to facilitate hybrid connectivity between on-premises SQL Server resources and Microsoft Fabric. The design leverages Azure Resource Groups, Conditional Access, and the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) to ensure seamless and secure data flows. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\577354cf-0d76-469f-b0e8-588645d107f7\images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 1: Secure access control architecture for UMS Fabric. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\577354cf-0d76-469f-b0e8-588645d107f7\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2: Conditional Access authentication and data flow for Microsoft Fabric. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\577354cf-0d76-469f-b0e8-588645d107f7\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 3: Hybrid data integration using On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG). ### Design Considerations | ID | Description | Workload Type | |----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------| | 1 | Secure hybrid connectivity between on-prem SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric | Data Integration | | 2 | Conditional Access enforcement for external user access | Security/Access | | 3 | Outbound-only gateway connection to avoid inbound firewall exceptions | Network Security | | 4 | Resource Group isolation for UMS Fabric workloads | Resource Management | ### Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------| | 1 | Use Azure Resource Groups for logical resource isolation | Approved | | 2 | Implement Conditional Access via Entra ID for secure user authentication | Approved | | 3 | Deploy On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) for hybrid data integration | Approved | | 4 | Configure OPDG with outbound-only connectivity | Approved | ## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway ### Microsoft Fabric Definition Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence capabilities. It enables organizations to consolidate data from multiple sources, analyze it, and deliver actionable insights using services such as Data Factory, Synapse, OneLake, and Power BI. ### On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) Definition The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that facilitates secure data transfer between on-premises data sources (such as SQL Server) and cloud services including Microsoft Fabric. The gateway establishes outbound connections, eliminating the need for inbound firewall rules, and enables hybrid data integration scenarios. #### Deployment and Responsibility Assignment Enfrasys Solutions shall design and implement the OPDG deployment in accordance with Microsoft best practices. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall provide and prepare the necessary on-premises virtual machine resources as specified below. #### VM Specification Table | Component | Specification | |-----------------|----------------------------------------------------| | Operating System| Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit)| | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | #### Task/Migration Rollout | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-----------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|----------------------|-------------| | Gateway Design | Architecture, sizing, and configuration planning | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | VM Provisioning | Prepare VM with required OS and specs | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending | | OPDG Installation | Install and configure OPDG agent | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Connectivity Testing | Validate secure outbound connectivity to Fabric | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Data Source Integration | Connect SQL Server to OPDG for Fabric access | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Operational Handover | Documentation and knowledge transfer | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | ### Data Flow Description The OPDG agent installed on the UMS-provided VM acts as a secure bridge, establishing outbound connections to Microsoft Fabric for data transfer from the on-premises SQL Server. This design ensures that no inbound ports are exposed, maintaining a strong security posture while enabling hybrid data workflows. --- # Appendix: Network Design ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Networking Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/networking/networking-overview On-Premises Data Gateway: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-onprem ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Gateway Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-onprem#limits Azure Resource Group Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits ### Others Azure Networking SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/networking/ Microsoft Fabric Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/ # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI This section outlines the roles, responsibilities, and access controls within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI for Universiti Malaysia Sabah. ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that combines data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a unified SaaS solution. It provides centralized governance, security, and management capabilities for enterprise data workflows. The Fabric Administrator role is responsible for overseeing the configuration, security, and operational management of the Fabric environment. Administrators have comprehensive permissions, including user management, workspace creation, resource allocation, and access control configuration. ### Administrator Capabilities | Capability | Description | |---------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | User Management | Add, remove, and update users and assign roles across Fabric and Power BI workspaces. | | Workspace Governance | Create, configure, and delete workspaces; manage workspace-level security and access. | | Resource Allocation | Monitor and allocate compute and storage resources; enforce quotas and limits. | | Security Configuration | Set up authentication, authorization, and compliance policies for data and analytics assets. | | Audit & Monitoring | Access activity logs, audit trails, and usage analytics for all Fabric components. | Enfrasys Solutions shall configure Fabric administrator roles and permissions. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall nominate personnel-in-charge (PIC) for administrator responsibilities. ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Power BI and Microsoft Fabric provide built-in workspace roles to facilitate granular access and operational control. These roles are: - **Admin**: Full control over workspace settings, content, and access permissions. - **Contributor**: Can create, edit, and publish datasets, reports, and dashboards within the workspace. - **Member**: Can view and interact with content but cannot edit or manage workspace settings. - **Viewer**: Read-only access to published content for review and analysis. ### Workspace-level Role Capabilities | Permission | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer | |---------------------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------| | Manage Workspace Settings | Yes | No | No | No | | Publish Content | Yes | Yes | No | No | | Edit Content | Yes | Yes | No | No | | View Content | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | | Share Content | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | | Assign Roles | Yes | No | No | No | ## 5.3 Client Workspace Role and Access Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall nominate designated personnel for each workspace role. Enfrasys Solutions will configure these roles in the Fabric and Power BI environments according to the mapping below. ### Client Workspace Role Mapping | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-----|---------------------|----------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias

Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall nominate and confirm PICs for each role prior to configuration. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision This section details the solution architecture, workflow, access, platform, resource organization, and governance for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric deployment. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence in a single SaaS solution. It leverages OneLake for unified data storage and supports seamless data movement, transformation, and visualization. UMS shall implement the following data workflow design considerations: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | | DE01 | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation. | Data Engineering | | DE02 | The Project will primarily adopt the Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python) to enable custom feature engineering, algorithm tuning, and model optimization. | Data Science | | DE03 | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment to achieve more accurate and explainable results tailored to UMS’s datasets. | Data Science | | DE04 | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry to support model lifecycle management, versioning, and potential reuse across further adoption. | Data Science | | DE05 | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data. The scored outputs, predictions, probabilities, and performance metrics will be stored in the Gold Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DE06 | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results, ensuring consistency between AutoML and manual ML outputs. | Data Engineering | | DE07 | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) and Data Modelling will be implemented | Power BI | | DE08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following key design decisions for UMS’s Fabric data workflow: | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Data ingestion from SQL Server via Azure Data Factory | Adopted as primary pipeline for initial data movement | | 2 | Layered Lakehouse architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold) | Implemented for raw, cleansed, and transformed data separation | | 3 | Data cleansing/transformation using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, Visual Query | Standardized for Silver and Gold layer processing | | 4 | Machine Learning via AutoML and Manual ML in Fabric Notebooks | Both paths supported; results stored in Gold Lakehouse | | 5 | Model registry and scoring notebooks for lifecycle and prediction management | Enabled for model versioning and operationalization | | 6 | Semantic Model and Power BI integration via OneLake | Direct Lake connection for real-time analytics | | 7 | Unified repository for modeled/scored results in Gold Lakehouse | Ensures consistency and accessibility | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\577354cf-0d76-469f-b0e8-588645d107f7\images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: High-level data workflow architecture. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\577354cf-0d76-469f-b0e8-588645d107f7\images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: Machine Learning workflow integration. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Conditional Access Conditional Access is a feature of Microsoft Entra ID that enables organizations to enforce access controls based on user, location, device, and application risk signals. It is essential for securing data access in Microsoft Fabric. | ID | Description | |------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | CA01 | Conditional Access policies will be configured to restrict access to Fabric resources based on user role and device compliance. | | CA02 | Access to Gold Lakehouse and Semantic Model will require compliant devices and trusted locations. | | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------| | 1 | Conditional Access for data engineering workloads | Enforced for all privileged accounts | | 2 | Conditional Access for Power BI reporting | Enforced for external access | ### Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a security mechanism that requires users to provide two or more verification factors to gain access to resources, reducing the risk of unauthorized access. | ID | Description | |------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | MFA01| MFA will be mandatory for all administrative and contributor roles in Fabric workspaces. | | MFA02| Access to sensitive data layers (Gold, Silver) will require MFA for all users. | | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------| | 1 | MFA for administrative access | Mandatory for all Fabric admins | | 2 | MFA for data science and engineering contributors | Mandatory for contributors | ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following VM specifications for supporting Fabric development and data engineering workloads at UMS: | Component | Specification | |-------------------|---------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit)| | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Azure Management Groups provide a hierarchical structure for organizing subscriptions and resources. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS implement a three-tier hierarchy: - Root Management Group (UMS) - Production Management Group (UMS-Prod) - Subscription: UMS-Fabric-Prod - Development Management Group (UMS-Dev) - Subscription: UMS-Fabric-Dev This structure enables clear separation of environments, centralized governance, and scalable policy enforcement. ### Naming and Tagging #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |------------------|-------------|-------------------------------| | Subscription | SUB | UMS-Fabric-Prod-SUB | | Resource Group | RG | UMS-Fabric-Dev-RG | | Storage Account | STG | UMS-Fabric-Prod-STG | | Data Factory | DF | UMS-Fabric-Dev-DF | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming -- | |-----|------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK
Etc… | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF
Etc.. | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL
Etc.. | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |------------------|-------------|-------------------------------| | Lakehouse | LK | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK | | Dataflow | DF | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF | | Semantic Model | SM | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | Pipeline | PL | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL | Enfrasys Solutions shall architect and document the naming conventions; Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall validate and approve all naming and tagging standards. ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### Governance Disciplines UMS must adopt Azure governance disciplines to ensure operational excellence, cost control, and compliance. | Azure Tool | Description | Cost Management Discipline | |-----------------|----------------------------------------------|------------------------------------| | Cost Management | Monitor, analyze, and optimize cloud spend | Budgeting, allocation, optimization| | Policy | Enforce resource compliance and standards | Resource governance | | Advisor | Provide recommendations for cost savings | Continuous improvement | ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Trigger | Action | Responsible Party | |------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------|--------------------------| | Naming convention violation | Issue remediation ticket; update resource| Enfrasys Solutions | | Unauthorized access attempt | Alert security team; enforce policy | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | | Cost overrun warning | Notify owner; review budget allocation | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | | Resource tagging omission | Automated tagging enforcement | Enfrasys Solutions | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for architecting and documenting governance controls. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall validate and enforce governance policies and respond to violation triggers. # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture, network controls, and encryption standards for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) deployment. ## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSG) are a core security feature within Microsoft Azure, providing granular control over inbound and outbound network traffic to resources. NSGs allow administrators to define rules that permit or deny traffic based on source, destination, protocol, and port, thereby enforcing network segmentation and minimizing attack surfaces. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure the NSG rules for the OPDG virtual machine as follows. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall validate compliance with these controls. | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |---------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ## 7.2 Encryption Design ### Microsoft Fabric Encryption Overview Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. Fabric enforces encryption at rest using Azure-managed keys and supports encryption in transit via TLS 1.2 or higher for all service endpoints. ### Power BI Encryption Overview Power BI is a cloud-based business intelligence service that enables data visualization, reporting, and analytics. Power BI encrypts datasets at rest using Microsoft-managed keys and ensures all data transfers between client devices and the service occur over encrypted HTTPS channels. ### On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) Encryption Overview The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a secure bridge that facilitates encrypted data transfer between on-premises sources and Microsoft cloud services such as Fabric and Power BI. The OPDG uses TLS/SSL for all outbound connections, ensuring data is encrypted in transit. #### Encryption at Rest - All data stored in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI is encrypted at rest using Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) and Microsoft-managed keys. - OPDG does not persist sensitive data locally; any temporary files are protected by Windows encryption standards. #### Encryption in Transit - All communications between OPDG, Fabric, and Power BI utilize HTTPS (TCP port 443) with TLS 1.2+. - Authentication and token exchanges are encrypted via secure identity endpoints (e.g., login.microsoftonline.com). Enfrasys Solutions shall configure encryption settings for all relevant resources and endpoints. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall validate that these configurations meet institutional compliance requirements. ![](opdg_network_architecture.png) Figure 1: OPDG network architecture with NSG rule enforcement and encrypted data flows. ## 7.3 Security Task Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------|------------| | NSG Rule Configuration | Implement outbound NSG rules for OPDG VM as per table in Section 7.1 | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Encryption Implementation | Configure encryption at rest and in transit for Fabric, Power BI, and OPDG | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Compliance Validation | Review and validate NSG and encryption configurations against Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s security policies | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending | # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the structured approach for deploying and migrating the new data platform solution for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup Enfrasys Solutions will lead all preparatory activities, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide necessary resources and approvals. The following table details the pre-migration tasks, responsible parties, and timelines. | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|---------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|---------------|-------------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys / UMS| Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys / UMS| Not-Started | | ## 8.2 Pilot The pilot phase will validate the migration process and ensure platform readiness. Enfrasys Solutions will configure a subset of workloads and data flows in the new environment, with Universiti Malaysia Sabah providing test data and user feedback. Key activities include: - Setup of pilot workspace and ingestion pipelines. - Functional and performance testing of data workflows. - Validation of user access and security controls. - Documentation of outcomes and issue resolution. UMS shall review pilot results and provide sign-off before proceeding to production rollout. ## 8.3 Production Rollout The production migration will follow a structured timeline to minimize disruption and ensure operational continuity. Enfrasys Solutions will execute migration waves, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah will support with resource allocation and sign-off. ### Migration Timeline | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------------|-------------------------------------------------------|---------------|-------------| | D-7 | Finalize migration checklist, user communication | Enfrasys / UMS| Pending | | D0 | Execute migration cutover, monitor platform health | Enfrasys | Pending | | D+1 | Post-migration validation, resolve outstanding issues | Enfrasys / UMS| Pending | ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Enfrasys Solutions will provide post-migration support, including issue resolution, monitoring, and knowledge transfer. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall designate personnel for operational handover and escalation. - Enfrasys Solutions will maintain a dedicated support channel for D+1 to D+7. - UMS will review migrated workloads and confirm operational status. - Formal handover documentation and training will be provided by Enfrasys Solutions. - Escalation procedures will be agreed upon for unresolved incidents. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for signing off on the completed migration and ongoing operational management post-handover. # 9.0 Appendix ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Machines: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Subscription Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits ### Others Azure Compute SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/virtual-machines/ ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Network Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Networking Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-network-limits ### Others Azure Networking SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/virtual-network/ ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/active-directory-whatis ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Entra ID Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/active-directory-service-limits-azure-portal ### Others Azure Security Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/ ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/platform/limits ### Others Azure Log Analytics SLA: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/monitor/ ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Governance Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Policy and Management Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/overview ### Others Azure Resource Manager Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/ ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Fabric-specific ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/ ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric Capacity and Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/service-limits ### Others Microsoft Fabric SLA: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/fabric/

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

**Description:**

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving Azure Resource Groups, a UMS Fabric service, and Conditional Access for external users. The components and their interactions are as follows:

1. **Fabric-RG (Azure Resource Group):**
   - Represents a logical container in Azure that holds related resources for the UMS Fabric service.
   - The Azure Resource Group is depicted as the foundational layer for managing and organizing the UMS Fabric service.

2. **UMS Fabric:**
   - A service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.
   - Likely represents a unified management system or a platform providing specific functionalities to users.

3. **Conditional Access:**
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric service.
   - Enforces policies based on conditions such as user identity, device compliance, or location.

4. **External User:**
   - Represents an end-user or client accessing the UMS Fabric service.
   - The user is subject to Conditional Access policies before being granted access.

5. **Data Flow:**
   - The arrow indicates a unidirectional flow of access or interaction from the UMS Fabric service to the external user.
   - Conditional Access acts as a gatekeeper, ensuring that only authorized users can interact with the UMS Fabric service.

This architecture emphasizes secure access control for external users interacting with the UMS Fabric service, leveraging Azure's Conditional Access policies.

This image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**:  
   - The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:  
   - The authentication request is evaluated against Entra Conditional Access policies. This involves three key stages:
     - **Signal**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are collected. Examples include "Users and groups" and "Network location."
     - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
     - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether the user is granted or denied access.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**:  
   - If access is granted, the user is allowed inbound access to Microsoft Fabric.

4. **Microsoft Fabric Components**:  
   - Microsoft Fabric includes various components for data and analytics, such as:
     - Data Factory
     - Synapse Data Engineering
     - Synapse Data Science
     - Synapse Data Warehousing
     - Synapse Real-Time Analytics
     - Power BI
     - OneLake (a unified data lake for storage).

This diagram effectively demonstrates the integration of conditional access policies with Microsoft Fabric to ensure secure and controlled access to its components.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and analytics workflow within the Azure ecosystem, leveraging UMS Fabric, Data Factory, OneLake, and Power BI.

1. **Data Source**:  
   - The process begins with data ingestion from the **UMS Staging Area**, which is hosted on **Microsoft SQL Server 2017** as a data warehouse.

2. **Data Ingestion**:  
   - **Azure Data Factory** is used to ingest data from the staging area into the UMS Fabric environment.

3. **Bronze Layer**:  
   - The ingested raw data is stored in **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**, representing the initial layer where raw, unprocessed data is maintained.

4. **Silver Layer**:  
   - Data is transformed and refined using **Azure Data Factory** and stored in **XX_Silver_LK Tables**, representing the intermediate layer with cleaned and structured data.

5. **Data Science Integration**:  
   - The **Data Science** component is integrated at this stage, enabling advanced analytics, machine learning, or predictive modeling on the Silver Layer data.

6. **Gold Layer**:  
   - The processed and enriched data is stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**, representing the final layer optimized for analytics and reporting.  
   - **Identity_SM** is used for identity management or security purposes, ensuring secure access to the Gold Layer data.

7. **Data Lake Integration**:  
   - The entire workflow is connected to **OneLake**, which serves as a unified data lake for centralized storage and management.

8. **Visualization and Reporting**:  
   - The Gold Layer data is connected to **Power BI** via OneLake, enabling interactive dashboards and business intelligence reporting.

This architecture demonstrates a modern data pipeline leveraging Azure services for efficient data processing, transformation, and visualization.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a data flow between three components:

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:
   - Represents an on-premises SQL Server 2017 database used as a staging area or data warehouse.
   - It stores and processes data before it is transferred to the next component.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:
   - Acts as a bridge between the on-premises SQL Server and the cloud-based UMS Fabric.
   - Facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened in the on-premises network.
   - Establishes an outbound secure connection to the UMS Fabric.

3. **UMS Fabric**:
   - A cloud-based service or platform that consumes data from the on-premises SQL Server via the On-Premise Data Gateway.
   - Likely used for advanced analytics, reporting, or integration with other cloud services.

**Data Flow**:
- Data originates in the on-premises SQL Server 2017 database.
- The On-Premise Data Gateway securely connects to the SQL Server and transfers data to the UMS Fabric.
- The connection is outbound-only, ensuring no inbound ports are required, enhancing security.

This architecture supports hybrid data integration, enabling secure communication between on-premises and cloud environments.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure-based ecosystem.

### Components and Data Flow:
1. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Acts as the overarching framework for the workflow, encompassing all components.

2. **Data Source: XX_Silver_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the intermediate data layer (Silver tables) stored in **OneLake**.
   - Serves as the input for machine learning processes.

3. **Machine Learning Pathways**:
   - **AutoML**:
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Automates the machine learning process, including feature selection, model training, and evaluation.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: Outputs scored results from the AutoML experiment for further processing.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modeling (Using Notebook)**: Allows for custom model development and experimentation using notebooks.

4. **Processed Data: XX_Gold_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the refined data layer (Gold tables) generated after machine learning processes.
   - Stores the final outputs for downstream consumption.

5. **Identity_SM**:
   - A specialized component for identity or metadata management, integrated with the Gold tables.

6. **Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**:
     - Connects to the Gold tables via **OneLake** for data visualization and reporting.

### Workflow Summary:
- Data from **XX_Silver_LK Tables** is processed through either the **AutoML** or **Manual Machine Learning** pathway.
- The outputs are stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables** and managed by **Identity_SM**.
- The refined data is then visualized in **Power BI**, leveraging the connectivity provided by **OneLake**.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.
