# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the project, the purpose of this Solution Design Document, and identifies the key stakeholders and intended audience. ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) has initiated a project to modernize its data analytics landscape by adopting Microsoft Fabric. The primary business objectives are to establish an integrated data workflow—from ingestion to transformation and visualization—leveraging Microsoft Fabric’s unified analytics platform. The scope encompasses the creation of a Fabric workspace, implementation of end-to-end ETL pipelines, execution of machine learning processes, and seamless integration with Power BI for business-aligned analytics. The solution will utilize Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Fabric Data Science for machine learning applications. This approach aligns UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s capabilities to deliver scalable, secure, and actionable insights. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The purpose of this Solution Design Document is to formally define the technical architecture, design decisions, and implementation roadmap for UMS’s Microsoft Fabric adoption. The document serves as the authoritative reference for all stakeholders, ensuring clarity, alignment, and traceability throughout the project lifecycle. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for authorship, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for review and sign-off. ## 2.3 Document Audience This document is intended for the following stakeholders and roles: | Stakeholder/Role | Organization | Intended Reader Purpose | |----------------------------------|---------------------|----------------------------------------| | Lead Enterprise Architect | Enfrasys Solutions | Solution design, technical oversight | | Project Manager | Enfrasys Solutions | Project governance, delivery tracking | | Data Engineer | Enfrasys Solutions | Implementation, workflow development | | Cloud Security Architect | Enfrasys Solutions | Security controls, compliance review | | IT Director | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Strategic alignment, sign-off | | Data Analytics Team Lead | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Business requirements, validation | | System Administrator | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Platform operations, support | | Business Analyst | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Use case mapping, requirements | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for document preparation and technical accuracy. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for reviewing the document and providing formal sign-off. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric Overview This section outlines the foundational Microsoft technologies selected for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) data analytics solution, detailing their definitions, features, and integration roles. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform designed to unify data engineering, data science, data integration, and business intelligence within a single SaaS environment. Fabric consolidates multiple Microsoft data services—including Data Factory, Synapse, and Power BI—into a cohesive workspace, enabling organizations to streamline workflows from data ingestion to transformation and visualization. Core features of Microsoft Fabric include: - Unified workspace for end-to-end analytics. - Native integration of Data Factory (for ETL), Synapse (for big data analytics), and Data Science (for machine learning). - Centralized governance and security through Microsoft Entra ID. - OneLake, a single data lake for all workloads. Business value for Universiti Malaysia Sabah: - Accelerates data-driven decision making by providing seamless data movement, transformation, and visualization. - Reduces operational complexity through unified management and security controls. - Enables scalable analytics and machine learning capabilities for business-aligned outcomes. UMS shall implement a Fabric workspace to orchestrate the ETL pipeline, conduct machine learning processes, and deliver actionable insights via integrated analytics. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s business intelligence platform that enables organizations to visualize data, share insights, and drive informed decisions. Power BI offers interactive dashboards, robust reporting, and real-time analytics capabilities. Within Microsoft Fabric, Power BI is natively integrated, allowing users to connect directly to datasets stored in OneLake, leverage data transformed in Synapse, and visualize results from machine learning models. This integration ensures that UMS’s analytics workflows—from data ingestion to visualization—are seamless, secure, and governed under a unified platform. Enfrasys Solutions recommends Universiti Malaysia Sabah utilize Power BI within Fabric to deliver business-aligned analytics and enable self-service reporting for stakeholders. ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. Entra ID provides authentication, authorization, and conditional access for users, applications, and resources across Microsoft cloud environments. In the context of Microsoft Fabric, Entra ID is responsible for: - Authenticating UMS users accessing Fabric, Power BI, and related services. - Enforcing conditional access policies to secure sensitive data and analytics workflows. - Managing workspace-level permissions and roles for data engineers, analysts, and administrators. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall leverage Entra ID to ensure robust security, centralized identity management, and compliance across the Fabric analytics platform. ## 3.4 OneLake OneLake is Microsoft Fabric’s unified data lake, designed to provide a single, scalable repository for all analytics workloads. OneLake supports multiple data formats and integrates natively with Fabric’s Data Factory, Synapse, and Power BI components. Key roles of OneLake in the UMS solution include: - Centralizing data storage for ingestion, transformation, analytics, and visualization. - Enabling seamless access and governance across the Fabric workspace. - Supporting advanced analytics and machine learning by providing a consistent data foundation. Enfrasys Solutions recommends Universiti Malaysia Sabah utilize OneLake as the primary storage layer for all data workflows within Fabric, ensuring unified data management and optimal performance. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture, connectivity, and gateway deployment for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) in support of Microsoft Fabric integration. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall implement a secure, outbound-only network connectivity model to facilitate communication between on-premises data sources and Microsoft Fabric. The architecture is designed to minimize exposure by avoiding inbound connections and leveraging Azure-native security controls. Enfrasys Solutions recommends an outbound-only gateway approach, ensuring that all data transfers from UMS’s on-premises environment to Microsoft Fabric occur through secure, encrypted channels. The gateway deployment will be managed by Enfrasys Solutions, while UMS is responsible for ensuring on-premises network readiness, including firewall configuration and internet access. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\c9c5c66c-c2c1-47a5-9848-dd08bb4e1fed\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Secure outbound data gateway architecture between UMS on-premises data warehouse and Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway ### Definition: Microsoft On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) The Microsoft On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a secure bridge that enables cloud services such as Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, Azure Data Factory, and Logic Apps to access data stored in on-premises environments. The gateway establishes outbound connections, eliminating the need for inbound firewall rules and reducing security risks. Enfrasys Solutions shall deploy the OPDG agent in a dedicated virtual machine within UMS’s network. The gateway will facilitate secure, encrypted data transfers between UMS’s on-premises SQL Server data warehouse and Microsoft Fabric. ### VM Specification Table | Component | Specification | |-------------------|---------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ### Secure Outbound Connection The OPDG VM will initiate outbound HTTPS connections to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. No inbound ports will be opened, ensuring that the VM cannot be accessed externally. All communication is encrypted via SSL/TLS, adhering to Azure networking best practices. ## 4.3 Outbound Network Security Rules ### NSG Rules Table | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |--------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services| 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication| 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ## 4.4 Task and Deployment Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |---------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------|----------| | Gateway VM Provisioning | Provision VM as per specification and install OPDG agent | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Network Readiness | Configure on-premises firewall and ensure outbound internet access | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending | | NSG Rule Configuration | Implement outbound NSG rules for gateway VM | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Connectivity Testing | Validate secure outbound connectivity from OPDG VM to Microsoft Fabric endpoints | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Data Source Integration | Connect OPDG to SQL Server data warehouse and validate end-to-end data flow | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | ## 4.5 Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------| | DD01| Deploy OPDG in a dedicated VM with outbound-only connectivity | Approved | | DD02| Enforce outbound NSG rules to restrict communication to Microsoft endpoints only | Approved | | DD03| Assign Enfrasys Solutions for gateway deployment and Universiti Malaysia Sabah for on-premises network readiness| Approved | | DD04| Exclude Private Link for initial rollout due to licensing and eligibility limitations | Approved | ## 4.6 Design Considerations | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------| | DC01 | Outbound-only gateway architecture minimizes attack surface | Data Integration | | DC02 | Secure HTTPS communication ensures compliance with Azure networking standards | Data Integration | | DC03 | VM sizing supports concurrent data transfer and gateway reliability | Data Integration | | DC04 | NSG rules restrict traffic to only required Microsoft endpoints | Security | | DC05 | Exclusion of Private Link due to licensing and eligibility constraints | Security/Connectivity | # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric This section details the roles, responsibilities, and access management for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) within the Microsoft Fabric platform. ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single SaaS solution. Fabric enables organizations to manage, analyze, and visualize data at scale, leveraging Microsoft’s cloud infrastructure and advanced governance capabilities. The Fabric Administrator is responsible for the overall governance, configuration, and management of the Fabric environment. Key responsibilities include: | Capability | Description | |------------|-------------| | Environment Management | Oversee creation, configuration, and lifecycle of Fabric workspaces. | | Security & Access Control | Define and enforce access policies, role assignments, and permissions. | | Resource Allocation | Manage compute, storage, and workspace resources within Fabric. | | Monitoring & Compliance | Track usage, audit logs, and ensure compliance with organizational policies. | | Integration Oversight | Coordinate integration with external data sources and other Microsoft services. | **Responsibility Assignment:** - Enfrasys Solutions shall conduct initial role mapping and governance policy setup for Fabric Administrator assignments. - Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall designate personnel for ongoing Fabric Administrator duties and user management. ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric provides several built-in workspace roles to facilitate granular access control: ### Workspace Admin - Full administrative control over workspace resources, including user management, content publishing, and workspace settings. ### Workspace Contributor - Can create, modify, and publish datasets, reports, and other analytics artifacts within the workspace but cannot manage workspace settings or user access. ### Workspace Viewer - Can view published content (reports, dashboards, datasets) but cannot modify or create new content. | Permission | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer | |------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------| | Manage Workspace Settings | Yes | No | No | No | | Add/Remove Users | Yes | No | No | No | | Publish Content | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | | Modify Content | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | | View Content | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | ## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following role mapping for UMS’s Fabric workspaces. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for mapping workspace roles to organizational requirements, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for assigning users to each role. | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-----|---------------------|-------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias

Professor Madya TS Dr Mohd Hanafi Bin Ahmad Hijazi | **Role Mapping:** Enfrasys Solutions **User Assignment:** Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision This section outlines the design and decision framework for implementing Microsoft Fabric within Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), covering data workflow, access, VM sizing, resource organization, and governance. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. It leverages OneLake for centralized data storage and provides seamless integration with Power BI for visualization. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS adopt a layered data workflow architecture, utilizing Azure Data Factory for ingestion, Lakehouse for data storage, and Power BI for reporting. | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, Visual Query in SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (bronze to silver, silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process; results stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\c9c5c66c-c2c1-47a5-9848-dd08bb4e1fed\images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: High-level data workflow architecture. ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions has determined the following key decisions for each workflow stage: | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Data ingestion from UMS Staging Area using Azure Data Factory | Azure Data Factory pipelines to Bronze Lakehouse | | 2 | Raw data storage in Bronze Lakehouse | Bronze Lakehouse for unprocessed data | | 3 | Cleansing and transformation in Silver Lakehouse | Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, Visual Query for Silver Lakehouse | | 4 | Curation and modeling in Gold Lakehouse | Gold Lakehouse for processed, scored, and modeled data | | 5 | Machine Learning experimentation | Manual ML and AutoML via Fabric Notebooks; results in Gold | | 6 | Model registry and lifecycle management | Fabric Model Registry for versioning and reuse | | 7 | Data visualization and reporting | Power BI connects to Semantic Model via OneLake | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\c9c5c66c-c2c1-47a5-9848-dd08bb4e1fed\images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: AutoML and manual ML workflow architecture. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Conditional Access Conditional Access in Microsoft Fabric is a security mechanism that enforces access policies based on user, location, device, and risk factors. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS implement Conditional Access policies to protect sensitive data and ensure compliance. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|---------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Require MFA for all administrative and privileged roles | Enforced for all UMS administrators and contributors | | 2 | Restrict access to Gold Lakehouse to trusted devices | Only compliant devices permitted | | 3 | Block access from risky locations or IPs | Access denied from non-whitelisted locations | | 4 | Enforce session controls for Power BI and Fabric | Session timeout and re-authentication required | ### MFA (Multi-Factor Authentication) Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a core security feature that requires users to provide two or more verification methods. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS enforce MFA for all users accessing Microsoft Fabric, especially those with access to Gold Lakehouse and Power BI. ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size Microsoft Fabric workloads may require dedicated VM resources for integration, orchestration, or legacy compatibility. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following VM specifications for UMS: | Component | Specification | |-------------------|--------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Azure Management Groups are containers for organizing subscriptions and resources at scale. Enfrasys Solutions will establish management groups for UMS to ensure structured governance and resource separation. ### Naming and Tagging Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS adopt standardized naming conventions for all Fabric resources to facilitate management, automation, and cost tracking. #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |------------------|-------------|--------------------------| | Subscription | sub | ums-sub-prod | | Resource Group | rg | ums-rg-studentaffairs | | Storage Account | st | ums-st-onelake | | Data Factory | df | ums-df-ingest | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming -- | |-----|------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK, StudentAffairs_Silver_LK, etc. | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF, StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF, etc. | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL, etc. | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | No. | Acronym | Definition | |-----|--------------|--------------------------| | 1 | LK | Lakehouse | | 2 | DF | Dataflow | | 3 | SM | Semantic Model | | 4 | PL | Pipeline | | 5 | WS | Workspace | ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### Governance Disciplines Microsoft Fabric governance encompasses cost management, resource access, compliance, and operational monitoring. Enfrasys Solutions will configure governance disciplines; UMS shall maintain operational compliance. | Azure Tool | Description | Cost Management Discipline | |--------------------|-----------------------------------------------|---------------------------------| | Azure Cost Management | Provides cost analysis, budgeting, and alerts | Budgeting, forecasting, anomaly detection | | Azure Policy | Enforces resource compliance and tagging | Policy enforcement, tagging | | Azure Monitor | Tracks resource utilization and performance | Usage monitoring, alerting | ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-----------------------|------------------------------------------------|--------------------|--------------| | Unauthorized Access | Detect and alert on access policy violation | Enfrasys Solutions | Configured | | Resource Naming Violation | Audit and enforce naming standards | Enfrasys Solutions | Configured | | Cost Overrun | Budget threshold alert and escalation | Enfrasys Solutions | Configured | | Operational Compliance| UMS to review and remediate flagged issues | UMS | Ongoing | --- **Boilerplate Generation Instructions:** For each sub-section, Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for design, setup, and enforcement of technical controls. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for operational compliance, user adoption, and ongoing review. All tables and naming conventions must be generated according to the schema above. Governance triggers and actions must be explicitly assigned. # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture and implementation decisions for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) in the context of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and associated Azure resources. ## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a core security feature within Microsoft Azure, designed to control inbound and outbound traffic to networked resources. NSGs use rules to permit or deny traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol, thereby enforcing network segmentation and minimizing exposure to threats. Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for the configuration and deployment of NSG rules as per Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s requirements. UMS shall be responsible for ongoing monitoring and incident response related to network traffic. ### Inbound NSG Rules No inbound rules are required for the On-Premises Data Gateway VM in this design, as all communication is initiated outbound. ### Outbound NSG Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-----------------------------|----------|--------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ## 7.2 Encryption Design ### Microsoft Fabric Encryption Overview Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. It delivers end-to-end data protection through robust encryption mechanisms at rest and in transit. ### SSL/TLS Encryption for Gateway Communication Enfrasys Solutions recommends that all communication between the On-Premises Data Gateway VM and Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Azure services be secured using SSL/TLS encryption. This ensures that data transmitted over the network is protected from interception and tampering. - **Gateway-to-Cloud Communication:** All outbound traffic from the gateway VM to Microsoft endpoints (including *.fabric.microsoft.com and *.powerbi.com) shall utilize HTTPS (TCP port 443), leveraging SSL/TLS protocols for data-in-transit encryption. - **Authentication Traffic:** Outbound authentication traffic to Microsoft Entra ID domains (e.g., login.microsoftonline.com) shall also be encrypted using HTTPS. ### Fabric Encryption at Rest and In Transit Microsoft Fabric provides encryption for data at rest using Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) with Microsoft-managed keys, and optionally, customer-managed keys. Data in transit between Fabric components and external endpoints is protected by TLS 1.2 or higher. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure encryption settings for all Fabric workloads and gateway VM communication. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall be responsible for monitoring encryption compliance and responding to any security incidents. ## 7.3 Security Responsibilities | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-----------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------|---------| | NSG Rule Configuration | Design, implement, and validate outbound NSG rules for gateway VM | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | | Encryption Setup | Configure SSL/TLS for gateway VM and validate Fabric encryption at rest and in transit | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | | Monitoring & Incident Response | Monitor NSG rule effectiveness, encryption compliance, and respond to security alerts/incidents | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Planned | ![](fabric_gateway_network.png) Figure 1: Secure outbound network flow from gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints. ## 7.4 Security Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | All outbound gateway traffic to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints must use HTTPS (SSL/TLS) | Enforced via NSG and gateway configuration | | 2 | Data at rest in Microsoft Fabric must be encrypted | Enabled via Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) | | 3 | Authentication traffic to Entra ID must use encrypted channels | Enforced via NSG and gateway configuration | | 4 | UMS will monitor and respond to network and encryption incidents | Assigned to Universiti Malaysia Sabah | # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the formal deployment and migration approach for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), detailing pre-migration, pilot, production, and post-migration phases. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup The pre-migration phase establishes foundational readiness for the Microsoft Fabric deployment and associated data integration. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for technical deployment tasks, while UMS is responsible for environment and data readiness. | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|------------|--------------|-----------|--------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys
UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys
UMS | Not-Started | | ### D-7 Timeline (Pre-Migration) | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|------------|-----------|--------| | 1 | Design Document Review & Sign Off | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | 2 | Server Confirmation for Staging Area DB | UMS | Not-Started | ## 8.2 Pilot The pilot phase validates the migration process and environment stability with a subset of data and workloads. Enfrasys Solutions shall execute the pilot deployment, including Fabric environment configuration and OPDG installation. UMS shall provide access to staging data and participate in pilot testing. Pilot Activities: - Deploy Microsoft Fabric environment in pilot mode. - Install and configure On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) for secure data connectivity. - Ingest sample data from UMS staging area. - Conduct validation tests with UMS stakeholders. - Document pilot outcomes and address any issues. Responsibilities: - Enfrasys Solutions: Deployment, configuration, pilot migration, issue resolution. - UMS: Data access provisioning, pilot testing, feedback. ### D0 Timeline (Pilot Cutover) | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|------------|-----------|--------| | 1 | Fabric Environment Setup | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | 2 | OPDG Installation & Connectivity Test | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | ## 8.3 Production The production phase involves full-scale migration and transition to live operations. Enfrasys Solutions shall perform the production deployment and migration, ensuring all workloads are transitioned. UMS is responsible for ensuring environment readiness and post-migration validation. Production Activities: - Deploy Microsoft Fabric environment for production workloads. - Complete OPDG installation and verify ongoing connectivity. - Migrate full data warehouse workloads to Fabric. - Conduct final validation and sign-off with UMS. Responsibilities: - Enfrasys Solutions: Production deployment, migration execution, validation. - UMS: Environment readiness, data validation, sign-off. ### D+1 Timeline (Post-Go-Live) | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|------------|-----------|--------| | 1 | Production Data Migration | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | 2 | Post-Migration Validation | UMS | Not-Started | ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Post-migration support ensures operational stability and addresses any issues arising after cutover. Enfrasys Solutions shall provide post-migration support, monitor environment health, and resolve incidents. UMS shall monitor business processes and report issues. Support Model: - Enfrasys Solutions: Provide Tier 2/3 support, monitor Fabric environment, resolve incidents, conduct regular reviews. - UMS: Monitor business operations, escalate issues to Enfrasys, participate in review sessions. Responsibilities: - Enfrasys Solutions: Incident resolution, environment monitoring, support reviews. - UMS: Issue reporting, operational monitoring, feedback. --- ![](architecture/fabric_migration_flow.png) Figure 1: High-level migration workflow for Microsoft Fabric deployment. # 9.0 Appendix This section provides reference documentation, technical boundaries, and supplementary resources for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) to support solution operations and governance. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Machines: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/ Windows Server Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/ .NET Framework Guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/framework/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Subscription Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits Azure VM Size Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes ### Others Azure Compute SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/virtual-machines/ VM Image Marketplace: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/marketplace/cloud-partner-portal/virtual-machine-offer-creation ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Networking Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/networking/ Azure Virtual Network Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Networking Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits#networking-limits NSG Rules and Best Practices: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview ### Others Azure Network SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/networking/ Azure Connectivity Troubleshooting: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/network-troubleshooter/ ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/ Azure Security Center Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/ ### Limits and Boundaries Entra ID Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/licensing-service-limits-quotas Azure Security Boundaries: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/security-boundaries ### Others Azure Security SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/active-directory/ Azure Identity Management Best Practices: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/security-best-practices ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/ Azure Log Analytics Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/limits Log Analytics Workspace Boundaries: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-overview ### Others Azure Monitoring SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/monitor/ Azure Diagnostic Settings: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Governance Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/ Azure Resource Manager Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Policy Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/definition-structure#limits Management Group Boundaries: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview ### Others Azure Governance SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/governance/ Resource Tagging Best Practices: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/tag-resources ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Fabric-specific ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/ Power BI Service Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/service/ Azure Machine Learning Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/ ### Limits and Boundaries Fabric Capacity Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/overview Power BI Dataset Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-dataset-size Azure ML Workspace Boundaries: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/concept-workspace ### Others Fabric SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/fabric/ Power BI SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/power-bi/ AutoML Workflow Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/concept-automated-ml ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\c9c5c66c-c2c1-47a5-9848-dd08bb4e1fed\images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 1: Azure-based machine learning and data workflow architecture.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving the following components and data flow:

1. **Fabric-RG**:  
   - Represents a resource group in Azure, likely hosting resources related to the "UMS Fabric."  
   - The resource group acts as a logical container for managing and organizing Azure resources.

2. **UMS Fabric**:  
   - A service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.  
   - It appears to be the core system or platform that external users interact with.

3. **Conditional Access**:  
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric.  
   - It enforces policies based on conditions such as user identity, device compliance, or location.

4. **External User**:  
   - Represents an end-user or client accessing the UMS Fabric.  
   - Access is mediated through the Conditional Access layer to ensure security.

**Data Flow**:  
- The arrow indicates a unidirectional flow of interaction or access from the External User to the UMS Fabric.  
- Before reaching the UMS Fabric, the request is evaluated by the Conditional Access layer, which enforces security policies to determine whether the user is granted access.

This architecture emphasizes secure access to the UMS Fabric by leveraging Azure's Conditional Access policies, ensuring that only authorized external users can interact with the system.

This image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**:  
   - The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:  
   - The authentication request is evaluated against Entra Conditional Access policies.  
   - **Signal (Step 3)**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are analyzed.  
   - **Decision (Step 4)**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.  
   - **Enforcement (Step 5)**: The decision is enforced, allowing or denying access based on the policy configuration.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**:  
   - If access is granted, the user is directed to Microsoft Fabric.  
   - Microsoft Fabric includes various components such as data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and visualization tools.  
   - **OneLake**: A unified data lake is highlighted as part of the Microsoft Fabric ecosystem, serving as a central repository for data storage and management.

This diagram effectively demonstrates the integration of conditional access policies with Microsoft Fabric to ensure secure and policy-driven access control.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

### Description of the Architecture:

1. **Data Source**:
   - **UMS Staging Area**: The data originates from a Microsoft SQL Server 2017-based data warehouse, referred to as the UMS Staging Area.

2. **Data Ingestion**:
   - **Data Factory**: Azure Data Factory is used to ingest data from the UMS Staging Area into the system. This is the first step in the data pipeline.

3. **Data Lake Layers**:
   - **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**: The ingested raw data is stored in the Bronze layer of the data lake. This layer contains unprocessed data.
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Data is transformed and cleaned in the Silver layer. This layer contains semi-processed data.
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The Gold layer contains fully processed, curated data ready for analytics and reporting.

4. **Data Processing**:
   - **Data Factory**: Azure Data Factory is used to orchestrate data movement and transformation between the Bronze, Silver, and Gold layers.
   - **Data Science**: Advanced analytics and machine learning models are applied to the data in the Silver layer to derive insights.

5. **Identity Management**:
   - **Identity_SM**: A component for managing identity and security, ensuring secure access to the Gold layer data.

6. **Data Storage**:
   - **OneLake**: A unified data lake storage solution is used to store data across all layers (Bronze, Silver, and Gold).

7. **Data Visualization**:
   - **Power BI**: The curated data in the Gold layer is connected to Power BI via OneLake for visualization and reporting.

### Data Flow:
1. Data is ingested from the UMS Staging Area into the Bronze layer using Azure Data Factory.
2. Data is transformed and moved to the Silver layer for further processing.
3. Data Science models are applied to the Silver layer data.
4. Processed data is moved to the Gold layer for final curation.
5. The Gold layer data is secured using Identity_SM and stored in OneLake.
6. Power BI connects to the Gold layer via OneLake for reporting and analytics.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a data flow between three components:

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:  
   - This represents the on-premises data warehouse where data is stored. It is depicted as a database icon.  
   - The data warehouse serves as the source or destination for data being processed.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:  
   - This is a gateway component that facilitates secure communication between the on-premises data warehouse and external systems.  
   - The gateway establishes an **outbound secure connection**, ensuring no inbound ports are required, which enhances security.  
   - It acts as a bridge for data transfer, enabling seamless integration between on-premises and cloud-based systems.

3. **UMS Fabric**:  
   - This represents a cloud-based service or platform that interacts with the on-premises data warehouse via the gateway.  
   - The UMS Fabric likely processes, analyzes, or utilizes the data transferred through the gateway.

**Data Flow**:  
- Data flows bidirectionally between the **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse** and the **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**.  
- The **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)** establishes a secure outbound connection to the **UMS Fabric**, enabling data exchange without requiring inbound ports.  
- This architecture ensures secure, efficient, and seamless data integration between on-premises and cloud environments.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure-based ecosystem.

### Components and Data Flow:
1. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Acts as the overarching framework for the workflow, encompassing all components.

2. **Data Source**:
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Represents the intermediate data layer (Silver tables) stored in **OneLake**. This serves as the input for machine learning processes.

3. **Machine Learning Workflows**:
   - **AutoML**:
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Automates the machine learning process, including feature selection, model training, and evaluation.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: Outputs the scored results from the AutoML experiment for further processing.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modeling (Using Notebook)**: Allows for custom machine learning model development and experimentation using notebooks.

4. **Data Output**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Represents the refined data layer (Gold tables) where processed and scored data is stored.
   - **Identity_SM**: A specific component or service for identity or metadata management, integrated with the Gold tables.

5. **Visualization**:
   - **Power BI**:
     - Connected to the **XX_Gold_LK Tables** via **OneLake** for data visualization and reporting.

### Connections:
- Data flows from **XX_Silver_LK Tables** to either the **AutoML** or **Manual Machine Learning** workflows.
- Processed data from both workflows is stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**.
- **Power BI** accesses the Gold tables through **OneLake** for visualization and insights.

This architecture demonstrates a hybrid approach to machine learning, combining automated and manual processes, with seamless integration into a unified data lake and visualization platform.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the project scope, document intent, and primary stakeholders involved in the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI solution for Universiti Malaysia Sabah. ## 2.1 Project Overview The project is scoped to design and implement a comprehensive data workflow for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) utilizing Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The objectives include: - Establishing a dedicated Fabric workspace for UMS. - Developing an end-to-end ETL pipeline covering data ingestion, transformation, and visualization. - Implementing machine learning processes for advanced analytics. - Integrating Power BI to deliver business-aligned analytics and reporting. - Aligning UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s core services: Fabric Data Factory (data integration and orchestration), Fabric Synapse (advanced analytics and big data processing), and Data Science (machine learning applications). ## 2.2 Document Purpose The Solution Design Document serves as the authoritative reference for the technical architecture, design decisions, and implementation roadmap for the UMS Power BI & Fabric project. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for authoring this document, ensuring alignment with best practices and Microsoft standards. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall review and sign off on the document to validate the solution meets business and technical requirements. ## 2.3 Document Audience This document is intended for: - Universiti Malaysia Sabah IT management and technical teams - UMS analytics and business intelligence stakeholders - Enfrasys Solutions enterprise architects and implementation specialists All parties are expected to use this document as the basis for design review, implementation planning, and ongoing governance of the Fabric and Power BI solution. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric, Power BI & Entra ID Overview This section provides formal definitions and design relevance for Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID as applied to Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates multiple data and analytics services into a single SaaS solution. It provides organizations with end-to-end capabilities for data ingestion, transformation, analytics, and visualization. Key components include Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Data Science for machine learning applications. Enfrasys Solutions recommends Universiti Malaysia Sabah implement Microsoft Fabric to establish a centralized workspace, enabling seamless ETL pipeline development, machine learning processes, and integration with business analytics tools. This approach aligns with UMS’s cloud adoption strategy and supports scalable, secure, and efficient data workflows. ### Core Features | ID | Description | Workload Type | |----|-------------|---------------| | 1 | Data Factory: Data integration and orchestration | ETL/Data Integration | | 2 | Synapse: Advanced analytics and big data processing | Analytics/Big Data | | 3 | Data Science: Machine learning application | AI/ML | ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s business intelligence platform that enables users to visualize, analyze, and share data insights across an organization. It integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, allowing data flows and analytics outputs to be directly consumed for reporting and dashboarding. Enfrasys Solutions shall guide Universiti Malaysia Sabah in leveraging Power BI within Fabric workspaces to deliver business-aligned analytics, supporting data-driven decision-making and operational visibility. ### Integration and Business Intelligence Role | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-----------------|----------| | 1 | Power BI integration with Fabric workspace | Enabled for seamless data visualization | | 2 | Business intelligence dashboards | Developed for UMS business use cases | ## 3.3 Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is a cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides authentication, authorization, and Conditional Access controls for users and applications across Microsoft 365 and Azure environments. Enfrasys Solutions assigns responsibility to Universiti Malaysia Sabah for user account provisioning and role assignment, while Enfrasys Solutions will configure Entra ID to secure access to Fabric and Power BI resources, enforcing Conditional Access policies to protect sensitive data. ### Authentication and Conditional Access Role | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|--------|----------| | 1 | Authentication via Entra ID | All access to Fabric and Power BI secured by Entra ID | | 2 | Conditional Access enforcement | Enabled for privileged and sensitive operations | # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture, connectivity, and security decisions for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) in the Microsoft Fabric solution. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview UMS's solution architecture establishes secure connectivity between on-premises SQL Server, the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine, Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI. The OPDG VM acts as a bridge, enabling secure outbound data transfer from UMS's internal SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI services, without exposing the internal network to inbound traffic. NSGs use rule-based filtering to permit or deny traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol, thereby safeguarding cloud workloads from unauthorized access. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure NSG rules to ensure secure connectivity between the OPDG virtual machine and Microsoft cloud services. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for reviewing and approving compliance of these rules. ### NSG Rules for OPDG Connectivity | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ## 7.2 Encryption Design ### Microsoft Fabric Encryption Overview Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, and business intelligence capabilities. It leverages Azure’s security framework to ensure data protection through encryption at rest and in transit. **Encryption at Rest:** All data stored within Microsoft Fabric is encrypted using Azure Storage encryption, which employs AES-256 encryption. This ensures that data is protected against unauthorized access when stored in the cloud. **Encryption in Transit:** Data transmitted between Fabric components and external endpoints uses SSL/TLS protocols, ensuring confidentiality and integrity during transfer. ### Power BI Encryption Overview Power BI is a cloud-based business analytics service that enables visualization and sharing of insights. Power BI utilizes Azure’s encryption standards to secure data both at rest and in transit. **Encryption at Rest:** Power BI datasets and reports are encrypted using Azure Storage encryption (AES-256), protecting sensitive business intelligence information. **Encryption in Transit:** All communication between Power BI clients, services, and gateways is secured with SSL/TLS encryption. ### On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) Encryption Overview The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) enables secure data transfer between on-premises sources and Microsoft cloud services. It is designed to meet enterprise security requirements for hybrid connectivity. **Encryption at Rest:** Configuration files and credentials stored on the OPDG VM are encrypted using Windows Data Protection API (DPAPI) and Azure Key Vault integration where applicable. **Encryption in Transit:** All data transfers between the OPDG and Microsoft Fabric/Power BI endpoints utilize HTTPS (TCP port 443) with SSL/TLS encryption, ensuring secure communication. ### Encryption Configuration Responsibilities | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|-------------| | NSG Rule Deployment | Configure outbound NSG rules for OPDG VM as per design | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Encryption Configuration | Enable and validate encryption at rest and in transit for Fabric, Power BI, and OPDG | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Compliance Review | Review and approve NSG and encryption configurations for regulatory and internal policy adherence | UMS | Pending | ![](./images/network_security_gateway_opdg.png) Figure 1: OPDG network connectivity and security rule architecture. ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/ Azure Log Analytics Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/limits Log Analytics Workspace Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits ### Others Azure Monitoring SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/monitor/ Azure Monitor Pricing: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/monitor/ ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Governance Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/ Azure Policy Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Policy Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts#limits Management Group Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#management-group-limits ### Others Azure Governance SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/policy/ Azure Cost Management: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/ ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Fabric-specific ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/ Fabric Dataflow Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/dataflows/ Fabric Lakehouse Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/lakehouse/ ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/service-limits Fabric Workspace Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/workspaces/service-limits ### Others Microsoft Fabric SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/fabric/ Fabric Pricing: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/pricing/

This is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving the following components and data flow:

1. **Fabric-RG**:  
   - Represents a resource group in Azure, likely hosting resources related to the "UMS Fabric."  
   - The resource group acts as a logical container for managing and organizing Azure resources.

2. **UMS Fabric**:  
   - A service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.  
   - It appears to be the core system or platform that provides specific functionalities or services.

3. **Conditional Access**:  
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric.  
   - It enforces policies based on conditions such as user identity, device compliance, location, or risk level.

4. **External User**:  
   - Represents an external entity or user attempting to access the UMS Fabric.  
   - Access is mediated through the Conditional Access layer.

**Data Flow**:  
- The external user initiates a request to access the UMS Fabric.  
- The request is intercepted by the Conditional Access layer, which evaluates the access request against predefined security policies.  
- If the conditions are met, the user is granted access to the UMS Fabric hosted within the Fabric-RG.  
- The arrow between the UMS Fabric and the Conditional Access layer indicates the flow of authentication and authorization processes.

This architecture emphasizes secure access control for external users interacting with the UMS Fabric, leveraging Azure's Conditional Access policies.

This image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**:  
   - The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:  
   - The authentication request is evaluated against Entra Conditional Access policies.  
   - **Signal (Step 3)**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are collected.  
   - **Decision (Step 4)**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.  
   - **Enforcement (Step 5)**: The decision is enforced, allowing or denying access based on the policy configuration.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**:  
   - If access is granted, the user is directed to Microsoft Fabric.  
   - Microsoft Fabric includes various components such as data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence tools.  
   - The diagram also highlights **OneLake**, a unified data lake within Microsoft Fabric, as a central repository for data storage and management.

This flow ensures secure and conditional access to Microsoft Fabric resources based on predefined policies.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

### Description of the Architecture:

1. **Data Source:**
   - The process begins with data ingestion from the **UMS Staging Area**, which is hosted on **Microsoft SQL Server 2017**. This serves as the data warehouse where raw data is stored.

2. **Data Ingestion:**
   - **Azure Data Factory** is used to ingest data from the UMS Staging Area into the system. Data Factory orchestrates and automates the data movement and transformation processes.

3. **Data Lake Layers:**
   - The ingested data is stored in **OneLake**, which acts as the unified data lake for the architecture. The data is organized into three layers:
     - **XX_Bronze_LK Tables:** This layer contains raw, unprocessed data.
     - **XX_Silver_LK Tables:** This layer contains cleaned and enriched data, prepared for further processing.
     - **XX_Gold_LK Tables:** This layer contains curated, business-ready data optimized for analytics and reporting.

4. **Data Transformation and Processing:**
   - **Azure Data Factory** is used to move and transform data between the Bronze, Silver, and Gold layers.
   - **Data Science** tools are integrated into the pipeline, enabling advanced analytics, machine learning, or AI-driven insights on the Silver layer data.

5. **Identity Management:**
   - The **Identity_SM** component is used for managing identity and security, ensuring that data access is controlled and compliant with organizational policies.

6. **Data Consumption:**
   - The curated data in the Gold layer is connected to **Power BI** via **OneLake**. Power BI is used for creating dashboards, reports, and visualizations for business intelligence purposes.

7. **UMS Fabric:**
   - The entire architecture is encapsulated within the **UMS Fabric**, which provides a unified framework for managing and orchestrating the data flow and processing.

### Connectivity:
- The data flow is linear, starting from the UMS Staging Area, passing through the Bronze, Silver, and Gold layers, and finally being consumed in Power BI.
- **OneLake** acts as the central storage layer, ensuring seamless integration and accessibility across the pipeline.

This architecture demonstrates a modern data pipeline leveraging Azure services for scalable, secure, and efficient data processing and analytics.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a data flow between three components:

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:
   - Represents an on-premises SQL Server 2017 database used as a staging area or data warehouse.
   - It stores and processes data before it is transferred to other systems.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:
   - Acts as a bridge between the on-premises SQL Server and cloud-based services.
   - Facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened in the on-premises network.
   - Establishes an outbound secure connection to the cloud, ensuring data security and compliance.

3. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Represents a cloud-based service or platform that consumes data from the on-premises SQL Server.
   - The connection to the UMS Fabric is established via the On-Premise Data Gateway.

**Data Flow**:
- Data flows from the SQL Server database to the On-Premise Data Gateway.
- The On-Premise Data Gateway securely transmits the data to the UMS Fabric using an outbound connection.
- The architecture ensures secure communication without exposing the on-premises environment to inbound traffic.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

### Description:
The diagram illustrates a data processing and machine learning workflow within a unified data platform, leveraging components such as **UMS Fabric**, **OneLake**, and **Power BI** for data storage, processing, and visualization.

1. **Data Source:**
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: This represents the intermediate data layer stored in OneLake. It serves as the input for machine learning workflows.

2. **Machine Learning Workflows:**
   - **AutoML**:
     - The **AutoML Experiment** component automates the machine learning process, including model selection and hyperparameter tuning.
     - The results are scored using a notebook, which likely involves evaluating the model's performance and generating predictions.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - This involves custom modeling using notebooks, allowing for more control over the machine learning process compared to AutoML.

3. **Data Output:**
   - The outputs from both AutoML and Manual Machine Learning workflows are stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**, representing the refined and processed data layer.

4. **Identity_SM**:
   - This component appears to handle identity or security-related metadata, ensuring proper access control and governance for the processed data.

5. **Visualization and Reporting:**
   - The **XX_Gold_LK Tables** are connected to **Power BI** via **OneLake**, enabling data visualization and reporting for business insights.

6. **Integration:**
   - **UMS Fabric** provides the overarching framework for managing and orchestrating the data and machine learning workflows.
   - **OneLake** serves as the unified data storage layer, facilitating seamless data access and integration across components.

This architecture supports automated and manual machine learning processes, secure data management, and business intelligence through Power BI.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.
