# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the project scope, document intent, and target audience for the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Power BI & Fabric solution design. ## 2.1 Project Overview The Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Power BI & Fabric project is scoped to design and implement a comprehensive data workflow encompassing ingestion, transformation, and visualization within Microsoft Fabric. The solution will establish a dedicated Fabric workspace, develop an end-to-end ETL pipeline, facilitate machine learning processes, and integrate Power BI for business-aligned analytics. The design leverages Microsoft Fabric components including Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Data Science for machine learning applications. The objective is to align UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with the capabilities of Microsoft Fabric to deliver scalable, secure, and business-driven analytics. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The Solution Design Document serves as the authoritative blueprint for the implementation of the UMS Power BI & Fabric solution. It details the technical architecture, workflow design, security considerations, and operational procedures required to achieve the project objectives. The document ensures alignment between Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s business requirements and Enfrasys Solutions’ recommended best practices for Microsoft Fabric deployment. ## 2.3 Document Audience The intended audience for this Solution Design Document includes: - UMS IT administrators and technical teams - UMS business stakeholders and data owners - Enfrasys Solutions enterprise architects and project managers All parties are responsible for reviewing and signing off on the Solution Design Document to ensure completeness, accuracy, and mutual understanding of the design and implementation approach. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric, Power BI & Entra ID Overview This section provides formal definitions and outlines the roles of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID within the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) solution architecture. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, data integration, and business intelligence capabilities into a single SaaS offering. Fabric provides a comprehensive suite of services—including Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Data Science for machine learning applications—allowing organizations to build end-to-end data workflows in the cloud. Core features of Microsoft Fabric include: - Centralized workspace management for collaborative analytics projects. - Unified data ingestion, transformation, and visualization pipelines. - Native integration with Power BI for business intelligence and reporting. - Advanced analytics capabilities through Synapse and Data Science modules. Within the UMS solution, Microsoft Fabric serves as the backbone for data workflow design, supporting ingestion, transformation, and visualization aligned to UMS’s business requirements. Enfrasys Solutions will provide technical guidance on Fabric workspace setup, ETL pipeline development, and machine learning process implementation. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall supply business context and requirements for workflow and analytics design. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business analytics solution that enables organizations to visualize data, share insights, and drive informed decision-making. Power BI offers robust capabilities for interactive reporting, dashboard creation, and data exploration, leveraging direct integration with Microsoft Fabric to streamline analytics workflows. Key analytics capabilities include: - Real-time data visualization and dashboarding. - Seamless integration with Fabric datasets and pipelines. - Self-service analytics for business users. - Advanced data modeling and DAX calculations. In the UMS solution, Power BI is integrated with Microsoft Fabric to deliver business-aligned analytics and visualization. Enfrasys Solutions will guide the integration process and ensure Power BI dashboards reflect UMS’s business objectives. Universiti Malaysia Sabah will define the business metrics and reporting requirements. ## 3.3 Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is a cloud-based identity and access management service that provides secure authentication, authorization, and user management for Microsoft cloud solutions. Entra ID enables organizations to control access to resources, enforce security policies, and streamline user provisioning across platforms. Core identity management features include: - Single sign-on (SSO) for Microsoft and third-party applications. - Role-based access control (RBAC) for granular permissions. - Multi-factor authentication (MFA) and conditional access policies. - Centralized user and group management. In the UMS solution, Entra ID is utilized to manage access control for Fabric and Power BI workspaces, ensuring only authorized UMS personnel can access sensitive data and analytics resources. Enfrasys Solutions will provide technical guidance on Entra ID configuration and RBAC implementation. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall identify personnel and assign business roles for access management. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section details the network connectivity and gateway architecture for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) to enable secure integration between on-premises SQL, OPDG, and Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) requires seamless and secure connectivity between its on-premises SQL Server, the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG), and Microsoft Fabric. The solution architecture is designed to ensure robust data flows, secure access, and compliance with Microsoft best practices. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\2faf00f1-0ba6-4bca-93e7-972dd88da5f6\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Secure data flow between on-premises SQL Server, OPDG, and UMS Fabric. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\2faf00f1-0ba6-4bca-93e7-972dd88da5f6\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2: Authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access. UMS’s network design leverages the OPDG to securely bridge on-premises data sources with Microsoft Fabric. All data transfers occur through outbound connections, eliminating the need for inbound ports and minimizing exposure to external threats. Conditional Access policies are enforced via Entra ID to regulate access to the Fabric environment. ## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway ### Microsoft Fabric Definition Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single SaaS offering. It provides a secure, scalable foundation for data workflows, leveraging services such as Data Factory, Synapse, OneLake, and Power BI. ### On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) Role The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft agent installed on a dedicated virtual machine within UMS’s environment. It acts as a secure bridge, enabling cloud services like Microsoft Fabric to access on-premises data sources (e.g., SQL Server) without exposing internal networks to inbound traffic. The OPDG establishes outbound encrypted connections to Microsoft endpoints, facilitating data transfer, authentication, and connectivity testing. Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for deploying and configuring the OPDG virtual machine and agent. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall ensure the on-premises SQL Server is prepared and accessible for integration. ### VM Specifications | Component | Specification | |--------------------|----------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit)| | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ### NSG Rules for OPDG | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |--------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services| 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication| 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ### Task/Migration Rollout | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |----------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|--------| | Gateway VM Deployment | Provision VM, install OPDG agent, configure NSG rules | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending| | On-Premises DB Preparation | Ensure SQL Server readiness, network accessibility | UMS | Pending| Enfrasys Solutions recommends strict adherence to Microsoft’s security and connectivity guidelines to ensure reliable data integration and compliance. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall maintain the on-premises database environment and collaborate with Enfrasys Solutions during gateway deployment and testing. # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and access assignments for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environments. ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a single SaaS solution. Fabric administrators are responsible for managing the platform's configuration, enforcing governance policies, provisioning workspaces, monitoring usage, and ensuring compliance with organizational standards. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah assign Fabric Administrator responsibilities as follows: - Platform governance and security oversight - Workspace provisioning and lifecycle management - User access control and role assignment - Monitoring and auditing platform usage - Incident response and escalation Administrators are empowered with the following capabilities: | Capability | Description | |-------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Workspace Management | Create, configure, and delete workspaces | | User Access Control | Assign and revoke user roles and permissions | | Data Governance | Enforce data policies and compliance standards | | Audit and Monitoring | Monitor activity logs and generate compliance reports | | Incident Management | Respond to security incidents and escalate as required | ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric and Power BI support a range of native roles to facilitate collaboration and secure data access. Enfrasys Solutions has mapped UMS personnel to these roles based on the project requirements. ### Native Role Definitions - Data Engineer: Designs, builds, and maintains data pipelines and datasets. - Business User Admin: Manages business user access, ensures data relevance, and oversees workspace contributions. - Data Analyst: Interprets data, creates reports, and provides actionable insights. - Stakeholder: Reviews analytics outputs and provides feedback for decision-making. - Project Director: Oversees project execution, ensures alignment with organizational goals. ### Workspace-level Role Capabilities | Permission | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer | |-------------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------| | Manage Workspace | Yes | No | No | No | | Publish Content | Yes | Yes | No | No | | Edit Content | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | | View Content | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | | Assign Roles | Yes | No | No | No | ### UMS Workspace Role Mapping | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-----|---------------------|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias

Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall assign users to the mapped roles as per the above table. ## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access Enfrasys Solutions recommends a structured approach to workspace access within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI for Universiti Malaysia Sabah. Role mapping is performed by Enfrasys Solutions; user assignment and ongoing access management are the responsibility of Universiti Malaysia Sabah. Summary of responsibilities: - Role mapping and workspace configuration: Enfrasys Solutions - User assignment to mapped roles: Universiti Malaysia Sabah - Ongoing access review and compliance: Universiti Malaysia Sabah This approach ensures secure, auditable, and efficient access to analytics resources, aligning with UMS’s governance and operational requirements. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision This section details the architecture, workflow, access, and governance design for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric environment. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, data warehousing, and business intelligence. It integrates services such as Data Factory, Synapse, OneLake, and Power BI, enabling organizations to ingest, transform, analyze, and visualize data securely and at scale. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following data workflow design for UMS, incorporating both core pipeline and AutoML considerations: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | | DE01 | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation. The chosen data will be one of the programs selected from the overall Staging DB. | Data Engineering | | DE02 | The Project will primarily adopt the Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python) to enable custom feature engineering, algorithm tuning, and model optimization. | Data Science | | DE03 | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment to achieve more accurate and explainable results tailored to UMS’s datasets. | Data Science | | DE04 | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry to support model lifecycle management, versioning, and potential reuse across further adoption. | Data Science | | DE05 | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data. The scored outputs, predictions, probabilities, and performance metrics will be stored in the Gold Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DE06 | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results, ensuring consistency between AutoML and manual ML outputs. | Data Engineering | | DE07 | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) and Data Modelling will be implemented. | Power BI | | DE08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\2faf00f1-0ba6-4bca-93e7-972dd88da5f6\images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: High-level data workflow architecture. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\2faf00f1-0ba6-4bca-93e7-972dd88da5f6\images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: AutoML and manual ML workflow integration. ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions assigns itself as the owner for workflow design and implementation. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide data source access and user input as required. | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01| Data ingestion will utilize Azure Data Factory to connect to the UMS Staging Area (SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse).| Approved. | | DD02| Data will be processed through Bronze, Silver, and Gold Lakehouse layers for raw, cleansed, and transformed storage.| Approved. | | DD03| Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and Visual Query will be used for transformation and cleansing. | Approved. | | DD04| Machine Learning will be implemented using both AutoML and manual notebook-based approaches. | Approved. | | DD05| All ML outputs and scored results will be stored in the Gold Lakehouse. | Approved. | | DD06| Power BI Semantic Model will connect to Gold Lakehouse via OneLake for visualization. | Approved. | | DD07| On-Premises Data Gateway agent will be installed in a VM to bridge SQL Server and Fabric. | Approved. | | DD08| Model registry will be used for lifecycle management and versioning of trained models. | Approved. | | DD09| Data modeling and visualization will be performed in Power BI Desktop. | Approved. | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\2faf00f1-0ba6-4bca-93e7-972dd88da5f6\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 3: Data gateway bridging on-premises SQL Server and Fabric. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Conditional Access and MFA Microsoft Entra ID is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service, providing authentication, authorization, and security controls for users and resources. Conditional Access policies and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) are used to enforce secure access to Microsoft Fabric and related services. Enfrasys Solutions recommends enforcing MFA at the tenant level for UMS due to current licensing constraints. Conditional Access policies are proposed for future enhancement, pending license upgrades. | No. | Conditional Access | |------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | | DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | | DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | | DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\2faf00f1-0ba6-4bca-93e7-972dd88da5f6\images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 4: Secure access flow with Conditional Access. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\2faf00f1-0ba6-4bca-93e7-972dd88da5f6\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 5: Authentication and Conditional Access enforcement in Fabric. ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following VM specifications for hosting the On-Premises Data Gateway agent, ensuring optimal performance and compatibility. | Component | Specification | |-------------------|-----------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Azure Management Groups provide a hierarchical structure for organizing subscriptions and resources, enabling centralized governance and policy enforcement across the environment. ### Naming and Tagging Enfrasys Solutions recommends a standardized naming convention for all Microsoft Fabric resources to ensure clarity, traceability, and ease of management. #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |------------------|-------------|-------------------| | Resource Group | RG | Fabric-RG | | Virtual Machine | VM | DataGateway-VM01 | | Storage Account | SA | umsdatastore01 | | Data Factory | DF | umsdatafactory01 | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming -- | |-----|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK
Etc…| | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF
Etc..| | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL
Etc.. | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |------------------|-------------|-------------------| | Lakehouse | LK | Bronze_LK | | Dataflow | DF | Bronze_to_Silver_DF| | Semantic Model | SM | StudentPerformance_SM| | Pipeline | PL | Ingest_Data_PL | | Report | Report | StudentPerformance_Report| ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### Governance Disciplines Enfrasys Solutions will design and implement governance disciplines, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for providing data source access and user input. | Azure Tool | Description | Cost Management Discipline | |----------------------|---------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------| | Azure Cost Management| Native tool for tracking, analyzing, and optimizing cloud spend | Cost Monitoring, Budgeting | | Azure Policy | Enforces organizational standards and compliance | Resource Compliance, Tagging | | Azure Advisor | Provides recommendations for cost, security, and performance | Cost Optimization, Security Review | | Azure Monitor | Collects and analyzes telemetry for operational insights | Usage Analytics, Alerting | ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Trigger | Action | |---------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------| | Unauthorized resource creation | Automated alert to UMS admin | | Non-compliant naming/tagging | Resource flagged for review | | Budget threshold exceeded | Notification and spend analysis | | Policy non-compliance | Enforcement via Azure Policy | --- # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture and controls for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) integration. ## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a core Azure security feature that allows administrators to control inbound and outbound traffic to Azure resources based on rules. NSGs help enforce least-privilege network access and are essential for protecting cloud workloads. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure NSG rules to secure the OPDG VM, ensuring only authorized traffic flows to and from Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related services. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall review and approve compliance requirements for all NSG configurations. ### NSG Outbound Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ## 7.2 Encryption Design ### Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, and business intelligence workloads. It leverages enterprise-grade security, including encryption at rest and in transit. Enfrasys Solutions recommends Universiti Malaysia Sabah implement the following encryption controls for Fabric: - **Encryption in Transit:** All communications between OPDG, Fabric, and Power BI utilize SSL/TLS protocols (HTTPS, TCP port 443) to ensure data confidentiality and integrity during transfer. - **Encryption at Rest:** Data stored in Fabric is protected using Azure-managed encryption keys, complying with Microsoft’s default encryption standards. ### Power BI Power BI is a business analytics service that delivers insights through interactive reports and dashboards. Power BI enforces encryption for both data at rest and in transit. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure Power BI to: - Use SSL/TLS for all client-server and gateway communications. - Store datasets and reports encrypted using Azure Storage encryption. ### On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) The OPDG facilitates secure data transfer between on-premises sources and Microsoft cloud services. Enfrasys Solutions shall ensure: - All outbound connections from OPDG VM to Microsoft endpoints are encrypted using SSL/TLS. - Gateway credentials and configuration data are encrypted at rest. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall be responsible for validating compliance with institutional and regulatory encryption requirements. ### Encryption Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | SSL/TLS enforced for all OPDG outbound connections | Enabled for HTTPS traffic to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and authentication endpoints | | 2 | Encryption at rest for Fabric and Power BI | Azure-managed keys and default platform encryption standards | | 3 | Compliance review | Universiti Malaysia Sabah to validate encryption meets regulatory and institutional requirements | ## 7.3 Security Task Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------|--------| | NSG Rule Implementation | Configure outbound NSG rules for OPDG VM as per design | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending| | Encryption Setup | Enable SSL/TLS for OPDG, Fabric, and Power BI; configure encryption at rest | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending| | Compliance Validation | Review encryption and NSG configurations for regulatory/institutional compliance | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending| ![](security_network_topology.png) Figure 1: High-level security network topology for OPDG and Microsoft cloud integration. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the structured deployment and migration methodology for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), detailing responsibilities, timelines, and support models. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup (D-7 to D0) Enfrasys Solutions and Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall jointly execute preparatory activities to ensure readiness for migration. All technical tasks are assigned to Enfrasys Solutions, while UMS is responsible for business validation and asset confirmation. | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|--------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------------|--------------|---------------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | | ### Timeline - D-7: Design Document delivery and review (Enfrasys/UMS) - D-5: Server confirmation for staging (UMS) - D-3: Fabric environment setup (Enfrasys) - D0: OPDG installation and connectivity validation (Enfrasys/UMS) ## 8.2 Pilot (D+1 to D+5) Enfrasys Solutions will conduct a pilot migration to validate data workflows, connectivity, and system integrity. UMS shall perform business validation and user acceptance testing. ### Pilot Testing Steps - Deploy a subset of data from the staging area to Microsoft Fabric. - Validate connectivity via On-Premises Data Gateway. - Execute end-to-end data ingestion and transformation. - UMS to perform business validation and approve pilot outcomes. - Document and resolve any issues identified during pilot phase. ### Timeline - D+1: Initiate pilot migration (Enfrasys) - D+2: Data validation and workflow testing (Enfrasys/UMS) - D+3: User acceptance testing (UMS) - D+4: Issue resolution (Enfrasys) - D+5: Pilot sign-off (UMS) ## 8.3 Production Rollout (D+6 to D+14) Upon successful pilot completion, Enfrasys Solutions will execute the full migration to production. UMS shall monitor business processes and validate post-migration data. ### Production Rollout Steps - Migrate all targeted data workloads to Microsoft Fabric. - Validate production environment connectivity and performance. - UMS to monitor business processes and validate migrated data. - Enfrasys Solutions to provide technical oversight and resolve migration issues. ### Timeline - D+6: Production migration kickoff (Enfrasys) - D+7–D+13: Data migration and system validation (Enfrasys/UMS) - D+14: Final production sign-off (UMS) ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support (D+15 onward) Enfrasys Solutions shall provide post-migration support, including incident management, system monitoring, and knowledge transfer. UMS is responsible for ongoing business operations and reporting issues. ### Support Model - Enfrasys Solutions to deliver Tier 2/3 technical support for Microsoft Fabric and Data Gateway. - UMS to operate business processes and escalate technical incidents as required. - Scheduled knowledge transfer sessions and documentation handover. - Performance monitoring and optimization recommendations. ### Timeline - D+15: Post-migration support engagement begins (Enfrasys) - D+16 onward: Continuous support, monitoring, and improvement (Enfrasys/UMS) # 9.0 Appendix This section provides reference links, technical boundaries, and prerequisites for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) and Enfrasys Solutions to support solution implementation and governance. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Machines: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/ Windows Server Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/ .NET Framework Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/framework/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Subscription Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits Azure VM Size Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes ### Others Azure Compute SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/virtual-machines/ Azure VM Pricing Calculator: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/calculator/ ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Networking Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/networking/ Azure Network Security Groups: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/network-security/network-security-groups-overview/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Networking Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits#networking-limits NSG Rules and Best Practices: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/network-security/network-security-groups-best-practices ### Others Azure Networking SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/networking/ Azure Connectivity Troubleshooting: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/networking/troubleshoot-network-connectivity ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD): https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/ Azure Security Center: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/ ### Limits and Boundaries Entra ID Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/azure-ad-service-limits Azure Security Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/security-limits ### Others Azure Identity SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/active-directory/ Azure Security Best Practices: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/best-practices ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/ Azure Log Analytics: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/service-limits Log Analytics Workspace Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits ### Others Azure Monitoring SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/monitor/ Azure Diagnostic Settings: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Governance Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/ Azure Resource Manager: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Management Group Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#limits Azure Policy Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts#limits ### Others Azure Governance SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/resource-manager/ Azure Cost Management Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/ ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Fabric-specific ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/ Power BI Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/ Azure Data Lake Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/data-lake/ ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/service-limits Power BI Capacity Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-capacity-limits OneLake Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/onelake/limits ### Others Fabric SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/fabric/ Power BI SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/power-bi/ Fabric Naming Convention Guidance: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/architecture/naming-conventions ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\2faf00f1-0ba6-4bca-93e7-972dd88da5f6\images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 1: AutoML and manual machine learning workflow architecture in UMS Fabric.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving the following components and data flow:

1. **Fabric-RG**:  
   - Represents a resource group in Azure, likely hosting resources related to the "UMS Fabric."  
   - The resource group acts as a logical container for managing and organizing Azure resources.

2. **UMS Fabric**:  
   - A service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.  
   - It appears to be the core system or platform that external users interact with.

3. **Conditional Access**:  
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric.  
   - It enforces policies based on conditions such as user identity, device compliance, location, or risk level.

4. **External User**:  
   - Represents an end-user or client accessing the UMS Fabric.  
   - Access is mediated through the Conditional Access layer to ensure security.

**Data Flow**:  
- The arrow indicates a unidirectional flow of access or interaction from the UMS Fabric to the External User.  
- Conditional Access acts as a gatekeeper, ensuring that only authorized and compliant users can access the UMS Fabric resources.

This architecture emphasizes secure access control for external users interacting with the UMS Fabric, leveraging Azure's Conditional Access policies.

This image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**:  
   - The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:  
   - The authentication request is evaluated against Entra Conditional Access policies. This involves three key stages:
     - **Signal**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are collected. Examples include:
       - Users and groups.
       - Network location.
     - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
     - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, allowing or denying access based on the policy evaluation.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**:  
   - If access is granted, the user is directed to Microsoft Fabric.

4. **Microsoft Fabric Components**:  
   - Microsoft Fabric includes various integrated services and tools, such as:
     - Data Factory.
     - Synapse Data Engineering.
     - Synapse Data Science.
     - Synapse Data Warehousing.
     - Synapse Real-Time Analytics.
     - Power BI.
     - OneLake (a unified data lake for storage).

This diagram effectively demonstrates the secure access flow and integration of Microsoft Fabric with Entra Conditional Access policies, ensuring compliance and security in user authentication and resource access.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and analytics workflow within the Azure ecosystem, leveraging UMS Fabric, Data Factory, OneLake, and Power BI.

1. **Data Source**:  
   - The process begins with data ingestion from the **UMS Staging Area**, hosted on **Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse**. This serves as the primary data source.

2. **Data Ingestion**:  
   - **Azure Data Factory** is used to ingest data from the staging area into the UMS Fabric environment. This marks the start of the data pipeline.

3. **Bronze Layer**:  
   - The ingested data is stored in **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**, representing the raw data layer. This layer is used to store unprocessed data for further transformation.

4. **Silver Layer**:  
   - Data from the Bronze layer is processed and transformed using **Azure Data Factory** and stored in **XX_Silver_LK Tables**. This layer contains cleaned and enriched data, ready for advanced processing.

5. **Data Science Integration**:  
   - The Silver layer data is further processed using **Data Science tools** for advanced analytics, machine learning, or predictive modeling.

6. **Gold Layer**:  
   - The output from the Data Science process is stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**, representing the curated and business-ready data layer. This layer is optimized for reporting and analytics.

7. **Identity Management**:  
   - The **Identity_SM** component is integrated at this stage, likely for managing user access, security, or data governance.

8. **Data Lake Integration**:  
   - The entire workflow is connected to **OneLake**, which acts as a unified data lake for storage and integration across the pipeline.

9. **Visualization and Reporting**:  
   - The final curated data in the Gold layer is connected to **Power BI** via OneLake for visualization, reporting, and business intelligence.

This architecture demonstrates a modern data pipeline leveraging Azure services for data ingestion, transformation, advanced analytics, and visualization. It ensures scalability, security, and efficient data processing.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a data flow between three key components:

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:  
   - This represents an on-premises SQL Server 2017 database used as a staging area or data warehouse. It stores structured data for processing and analysis.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:  
   - The OPDG acts as a secure bridge between the on-premises SQL Server and cloud-based services. It facilitates data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened, ensuring secure communication.  
   - The gateway establishes an **outbound secure connection** to the cloud, eliminating the need for inbound connections, which enhances security.

3. **UMS Fabric**:  
   - This represents a cloud-based service or platform (likely part of a larger unified management system). It interacts with the on-premises data through the OPDG to access, process, or analyze the data stored in the SQL Server.

**Data Flow**:  
- Data flows bidirectionally between the SQL Server and the OPDG.  
- The OPDG securely connects to the UMS Fabric in the cloud using an outbound connection.  
- The UMS Fabric can retrieve, process, or send data back to the SQL Server through the OPDG, enabling seamless integration between on-premises and cloud environments.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure-based ecosystem.

### Components and Data Flow:
1. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Acts as the overarching framework for the workflow, encompassing all components.

2. **Data Source**:
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Represents the intermediate data layer (Silver tables) stored in **OneLake**. This serves as the input for machine learning processes.

3. **Machine Learning Workflows**:
   - **AutoML**:
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Automates the machine learning process, including feature selection, model training, and evaluation.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: Outputs the scored results from the AutoML experiment for further processing.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modeling (Using Notebook)**: Allows for custom machine learning model development and experimentation using notebooks.

4. **Data Output**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Represents the refined data layer (Gold tables) where processed and scored data is stored.
   - **Identity_SM**: A specific component or service for identity or metadata management, integrated with the Gold tables.

5. **Visualization**:
   - **Power BI**:
     - Connected to the **XX_Gold_LK Tables** via **OneLake** for data visualization and reporting.

### Connections:
- Data flows from **XX_Silver_LK Tables** to either the **AutoML** or **Manual Machine Learning** workflows.
- Processed data from both workflows is stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**.
- **Power BI** accesses the Gold tables through **OneLake** for visualization and insights.

This architecture demonstrates a hybrid approach to machine learning, combining automated and manual processes, with seamless integration into a unified data lake and visualization platform.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the project scope, document purpose, and intended audience for the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Power BI & Microsoft Fabric solution. ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) has initiated a project to design and implement an integrated data workflow leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The primary business objective is to enable seamless data ingestion, transformation, and visualization to support business-aligned analytics. The project scope encompasses the establishment of a dedicated Microsoft Fabric workspace, development of end-to-end ETL pipelines, execution of machine learning processes, and integration of Power BI for advanced reporting and insights. The solution is designed to align UMS's cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s capabilities, including Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Data Science for machine learning applications. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The Solution Design Document serves as the authoritative reference for the technical architecture, design decisions, and implementation approach for the UMS Power BI & Microsoft Fabric deployment. It details the proposed infrastructure, workflow design, and integration strategies, ensuring alignment with UMS’s business requirements and Microsoft best practices. The document is intended to facilitate informed decision-making, guide implementation activities, and provide a baseline for future enhancements. ## 2.3 Document Audience This document is intended for the following stakeholders: - UMS IT Department - UMS Analytics Team - UMS Business Stakeholders - Enfrasys Solutions Project Team - UMS Executive Management Responsibility for review and sign-off of this Solution Design Document is jointly assigned to Enfrasys Solutions and Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric, Power BI & Entra ID Overview This section provides formal definitions and integration roles for Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID within Universiti Malaysia Sabah's data platform solution. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, data integration, and business intelligence capabilities under a single SaaS environment. Fabric consolidates multiple Microsoft services, enabling organizations to manage the entire data lifecycle—from ingestion, transformation, storage, and analytics—within a unified workspace. Fabric Core Services: - **Data Factory**: Provides robust data integration and orchestration capabilities, allowing users to build ETL pipelines for seamless data movement and transformation across various sources. - **Synapse**: Delivers advanced analytics and big data processing, supporting complex queries, real-time analytics, and scalable data warehousing. - **Data Science**: Enables machine learning workflows, including model training, deployment, and operationalization, directly within the Fabric workspace. - **Lakehouse**: Combines the benefits of data lakes and data warehouses, offering scalable storage and analytics for structured and unstructured data. - **OneLake**: Acts as a unified storage layer for all Fabric workloads, simplifying data management, governance, and access. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah establish a dedicated Fabric workspace to support business-aligned data workflows, including end-to-end ETL pipelines, machine learning processes, and integrated analytics. | ID | Description | Workload Type | |----|-----------------------------------------------|--------------------| | 1 | Data ingestion and transformation workflows | Data Factory | | 2 | Advanced analytics and big data processing | Synapse | | 3 | Machine learning model development | Data Science | | 4 | Unified storage for analytics workloads | Lakehouse/OneLake | Enfrasys Solutions shall provide technical guidance on Fabric configuration and data workflow design. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide business requirements and data sources for integration. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is a cloud-based business analytics service that enables organizations to visualize and share insights from their data. It offers interactive dashboards, reports, and self-service analytics capabilities, empowering users to make informed decisions. Power BI Integration with Fabric: Power BI natively integrates with Microsoft Fabric, allowing users to connect directly to Fabric workspaces, Lakehouse, and other data sources for real-time analytics and visualization. This seamless integration supports end-to-end data workflows, from ingestion and transformation to business intelligence reporting. Enfrasys Solutions recommends leveraging Power BI within the Fabric workspace to deliver business-aligned analytics for Universiti Malaysia Sabah. Enfrasys Solutions shall provide guidance on Power BI workspace setup and report development. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall define reporting requirements and business KPIs. | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|---------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Power BI integration with Fabric workspace | Enabled for real-time analytics and visualization | | 2 | Data source connectivity | Direct connection to Lakehouse and ETL pipelines | | 3 | Report development responsibility | Enfrasys to guide, UMS to provide requirements | ## 3.3 Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides secure authentication, authorization, and conditional access for users, applications, and resources across Microsoft 365, Azure, and integrated services. Role in Authentication and Conditional Access: Entra ID enables centralized user authentication and role-based access control for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. It supports conditional access policies, ensuring only authorized Universiti Malaysia Sabah personnel can access sensitive data and analytics resources. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure Entra ID integration, role assignments, and conditional access policies. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall identify user groups and access requirements. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------| | 1 | Authentication method | Entra ID Single Sign-On (SSO) | | 2 | Conditional access for analytics | Enforced for sensitive data workspaces | | 3 | Role-based access assignment | UMS to define, Enfrasys to implement | # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture and connectivity decisions for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) to enable secure integration between on-premises data sources and Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) requires seamless, secure connectivity between its on-premises SQL Server 2017 data warehouse, a dedicated Data Gateway virtual machine (VM), and Microsoft Fabric in Azure. The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM acts as the intermediary, securely bridging the on-premises environment and cloud services. No inbound ports are required; all communications are initiated outbound from the gateway VM. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing and implementing the Data Gateway VM, including network configuration and security. UMS shall provide access to the on-premises SQL Server and ensure the VM is placed in a network segment with the necessary connectivity. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\18950b39-cd9d-406e-9b21-e91a531b6cbf\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Data gateway architecture connecting on-premises SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway ### Definition: Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway The Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway is a secure bridge that enables cloud services such as Microsoft Fabric and Power BI to access data from on-premises sources. It operates by establishing outbound encrypted connections, ensuring that no inbound firewall rules are necessary. The gateway supports scheduled refreshes, live queries, and secure authentication, facilitating hybrid data integration scenarios. ### Data Gateway VM Specification | Component | Specification | |-------------------|-------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | Enfrasys Solutions will provision and configure the Data Gateway VM according to the above specifications. UMS shall ensure the VM is granted access to the on-premises SQL Server and is located in a secure, reliable network segment. ### Outbound Connectivity and Security The Data Gateway VM requires outbound access to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. No inbound ports are required. The following Azure Network Security Group (NSG) rules must be applied to the VM subnet to enable secure operation: | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-----------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | Enfrasys Solutions will implement and validate these outbound NSG rules. UMS shall maintain network stability and monitor gateway VM connectivity. ## 4.3 Task and Rollout Responsibility | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|-------------| | Data Gateway VM Provisioning | Deploy VM with required OS, .NET Framework, vCPU, RAM; configure network and security settings | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | On-Premises Access Provision | Grant Data Gateway VM access to SQL Server 2017 staging/data warehouse | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending | | NSG Rule Implementation | Apply outbound NSG rules for Fabric, Power BI, authentication, and connectivity test endpoints | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Gateway Agent Installation | Install and configure Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway agent on VM | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Connectivity Validation | Test secure outbound connectivity from VM to Microsoft Fabric and related services | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | ## 4.4 Timeline for Gateway Deployment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|-------------| | Pre-Deployment (D-7) | Finalize VM specifications and network segment selection | Enfrasys Solutions / UMS | Scheduled | | Gateway Provisioning (D0) | Deploy VM, install gateway agent, apply NSG rules | Enfrasys Solutions | Scheduled | | Post-Deployment Validation (D+1)| Conduct connectivity and security validation, handover documentation | Enfrasys Solutions | Scheduled | # Appendix: Data Gateway and Network References ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/connect-data/service-gateway-onprem ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Network Security Groups (NSG) Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview ### Others Microsoft Fabric Connectivity Requirements: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/dataflows/service-data-gateway-connectivity # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI This section defines the roles and access responsibilities for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environments. ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that brings together data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a unified SaaS environment. Fabric provides centralized management, security, and governance capabilities for all analytics workloads. The Fabric Administrator is responsible for overseeing the configuration, governance, and security of the Fabric environment. Administrators ensure compliance, manage user access, monitor resource usage, and coordinate integration with Power BI and other Microsoft services. ### Administrator capabilities | Capability | Description | |-----------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Environment Configuration | Manage workspace creation, resource allocation, and service settings. | | Security & Compliance | Implement access controls, audit logs, and compliance policies. | | User Management | Assign and revoke user roles, monitor activity, and maintain user directory. | | Integration Oversight | Manage connections to Power BI, Azure, and external data sources. | | Monitoring & Reporting | Track usage, generate reports, and respond to incidents. | Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah assign qualified personnel to the Fabric Administrator role to ensure proper governance and operational continuity. ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric and Power BI support a range of native roles to facilitate collaboration and data management: - **Data Engineer:** Responsible for designing, building, and maintaining data pipelines and datasets within Fabric. - **Business User Admin:** Manages business user access, workspace configuration, and operational workflows. - **Data Analyst:** Performs data analysis, creates reports, and visualizes data using Power BI. - **Stakeholder:** Reviews analytics outputs, makes strategic decisions, and provides feedback. - **Project Director:** Oversees project progress, ensures alignment with organizational objectives, and manages stakeholder engagement. Enfrasys Solutions recommends mapping these roles to the appropriate workspace permissions to support UMS’s analytics initiatives. ## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access The following table presents the recommended role mapping for UMS personnel within Fabric and Power BI workspaces. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the mapping; Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall confirm and assign the Person In Charge (PIC) for each role. | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-----|----------------------|-----------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias
