Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall confirm the assignment of each PIC to ensure proper access and operational clarity. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision This section outlines the solution architecture, data workflow, access controls, platform sizing, resource organization, and governance for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s Microsoft Fabric implementation. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. It leverages OneLake as a centralized data lake and integrates with Power BI for visualization and reporting. Enfrasys Solutions has identified the following data workflow design considerations for Universiti Malaysia Sabah: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering| | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering| | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering| | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering| | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | | DE01 | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation. | Data Engineering| | DE02 | The Project will primarily adopt the Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python). | Data Science | | DE03 | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment. | Data Science | | DE04 | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry. | Data Science | | DE05 | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data. | Data Engineering| | DE06 | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results. | Data Engineering| | DE07 | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) and Data Modelling will be implemented | Power BI | | DE08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\18950b39-cd9d-406e-9b21-e91a531b6cbf\images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow architecture from ingestion to visualization. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\18950b39-cd9d-406e-9b21-e91a531b6cbf\images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: AutoML and manual machine learning workflow integration in Fabric. ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following design decisions, referencing the above considerations: | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Data ingestion from UMS SQL Server via Azure Data Factory and On-Premises Data Gateway | Adopt DC01, DC02; ensure secure and reliable data transfer to Bronze Lakehouse | | 2 | Layered Lakehouse architecture for data processing | Implement DC02, DC03, DC04 for Bronze, Silver, Gold layers | | 3 | Data transformation using Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and Visual Query | DC05, DC06; enforce modular and reusable transformation logic | | 4 | Machine Learning experimentation using both AutoML and manual approaches | DE01, DE02, DE03, DE04; store results in Gold Lakehouse | | 5 | Model scoring and registration for lifecycle management | DE05, DE06; utilize Fabric Model Registry | | 6 | Semantic Model and Power BI integration via OneLake | DC08, DC09, DE07, DE08; ensure seamless visualization and reporting | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\18950b39-cd9d-406e-9b21-e91a531b6cbf\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 3: Secure data gateway bridging on-premises SQL Server and Fabric. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Conditional Access Microsoft Entra ID Conditional Access is a security feature that enables organizations to configure policies that determine how users access cloud resources based on signals such as user identity, device compliance, and location. Enfrasys Solutions notes that Universiti Malaysia Sabah currently does not have the required Entra ID P1/P2 licensing for advanced Conditional Access. MFA is enforced at the tenant level. | No. | Conditional Access | Decision | |------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, Azure Storage | Deferred; UMS to review licensing and revisit if advanced CA is required | | DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | Deferred; subject to licensing upgrade | | DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | Enforced; MFA at tenant level | | DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | Enabled; subject to MFA enforcement | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\18950b39-cd9d-406e-9b21-e91a531b6cbf\images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 4: Conditional Access architecture for external user access. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\18950b39-cd9d-406e-9b21-e91a531b6cbf\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 5: Entra Conditional Access signal-decision-enforcement workflow. ### Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a security mechanism that requires users to provide multiple forms of verification before accessing resources, significantly reducing the risk of unauthorized access. Enfrasys Solutions recommends enforcing MFA at the tenant level for all UMS users accessing Microsoft Fabric. ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that securely bridges on-premises data sources with cloud services such as Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, without requiring inbound ports. The following VM specifications are recommended for hosting the OPDG: | Component | Specification | |------------------|----------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | Enfrasys Solutions shall provision the VM and install the gateway agent; Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall validate connectivity to on-premises SQL Server. ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Azure Management Groups are hierarchical containers used to organize subscriptions and resources for governance, policy enforcement, and access control. Enfrasys Solutions recommends structuring management groups for UMS Fabric resources to enable centralized policy management and cost tracking. ### Naming and Tagging A consistent naming convention is essential for resource discoverability, lifecycle management, and operational governance. #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |--------------------|-------------|------------------------------| | Resource Group | RG | Fabric-RG | | Virtual Machine | VM | OPDG-VM | | Data Gateway | DG | OPDG | | Storage Account | SA | umsdatastore01 | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming -- | |-----|------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK, StudentAffairs_Silver_LK, etc. | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF, StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF, etc. | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL, etc. | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | Resource | Abbreviation | |-------------------|-------------| | Lakehouse | LK | | Dataflow | DF | | Semantic Model | SM | | Pipeline | PL | | Report | Report | Enfrasys Solutions will define the naming and tagging standards; Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall validate alignment with business requirements. ## 6.6 Governance Consideration Governance in Microsoft Fabric and Azure ensures that resources are managed securely, efficiently, and in compliance with organizational policies. It includes cost management, policy enforcement, and violation remediation. ### Governance Disciplines | Azure Tool | Description | Cost Management Discipline | |----------------------|----------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------| | Azure Cost Management| Provides visibility, budgeting, and alerting for cloud spend | Budgeting, Monitoring, Alerts | | Azure Policy | Enforces resource compliance and standards | Policy Enforcement | | Azure Advisor | Offers recommendations for cost optimization and security | Optimization | ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-----------------|----------------------------------------------------|---------------------|------------------| | Resource Naming | Audit resource naming compliance | Enfrasys Solutions | Design | | Tagging | Enforce tagging for cost center and owner | Enfrasys Solutions | Design | | Cost Overrun | Trigger alert for budget threshold breach | Enfrasys Solutions | Design | | Policy Violation| Remediate non-compliant resources automatically | Enfrasys Solutions | Design | | Business Review | Validate resource organization and governance | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Validate | Enfrasys Solutions shall implement governance controls; Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall validate business and operational requirements. --- # Appendix ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/ Azure Management Groups: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/ Azure Resource Naming: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cloud-adoption-framework/ready/azure-best-practices/resource-naming On-Premises Data Gateway: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-install ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/limits-and-quotas Azure Subscription Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits ### Others Microsoft Fabric SLAs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/legal/sla/fabric/ Azure Cost Management documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/ Azure Policy documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/ # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section defines the security architecture for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environment, focusing on network protection and encryption standards. ## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSG) are a core Azure security feature that allow administrators to control inbound and outbound traffic to Azure resources at the subnet and VM level. NSGs use rule-based filtering to enforce access policies for workloads hosted in Azure. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure all NSG rules for the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide compliance requirements to guide rule creation and documentation. ### Inbound Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |------|----------|--------|--------------|-------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_Admin | 100 | Universiti Malaysia Sabah Admin IPs | * | OPDG VM Private IP | 3389 | TCP | Allow | | Deny_All | 4096 | * | * | OPDG VM Private IP | * | * | Deny | ### Outbound Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |------|----------|--------|--------------|-------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | | Deny_All | 4096 | * | * | * | * | * | Deny | ## 7.2 Encryption Design Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data warehousing, and business intelligence capabilities. Power BI is Microsoft’s cloud-based business analytics service that enables interactive visualizations and business intelligence reporting. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure all encryption protocols for gateway, Fabric, and Power BI services. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide compliance requirements for encryption and data protection. ### SSL/TLS Encryption for Gateway All communications between the On-Premises Data Gateway VM and Microsoft services (Fabric, Power BI, Entra ID) shall be secured using SSL/TLS encryption. This ensures confidentiality and integrity of data in transit. ### Microsoft Fabric Encryption Data transferred between the OPDG VM and Microsoft Fabric endpoints will utilize HTTPS (TLS 1.2 or higher), in accordance with Microsoft’s security standards. All access tokens and authentication flows will be encrypted. ### Power BI Encryption Power BI service endpoints enforce SSL/TLS encryption for all inbound and outbound data. The OPDG VM will connect to Power BI using HTTPS, ensuring secure data transfer and compliance with Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s regulatory requirements. ## 7.3 Security Task Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|------------|-----------|--------| | NSG Rule Configuration | Define and implement inbound/outbound NSG rules for OPDG VM | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Encryption Protocol Setup | Configure SSL/TLS for gateway, Fabric, and Power BI | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Compliance Requirement Submission | Provide regulatory and policy requirements for security controls | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending | ![](fabric_powerbi_security_topology.png) Figure 1: High-level security topology for OPDG connectivity and encryption. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the structured approach for deploying and migrating the solution at Universiti Malaysia Sabah, including pre-migration setup, pilot, production rollout, and post-migration support. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup Enfrasys Solutions will lead the deployment activities, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall provide necessary data and access to the environment. The following table summarizes the pre-migration tasks: | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------------|-------------|-------------------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB)| Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway| Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | | ## 8.2 Pilot The pilot phase will validate the migration approach and ensure system readiness. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for configuring the pilot environment, executing initial data ingestion, and monitoring performance. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide access to sample data and coordinate user testing. The pilot will include: - Deployment of Microsoft Fabric in a controlled environment. - Installation and configuration of On-Premises Data Gateway. - Limited data ingestion and validation with selected UMS stakeholders. - Feedback collection and issue resolution. ## 8.3 Production Upon successful pilot completion, the production rollout will commence. Enfrasys Solutions will execute the migration of full workloads, monitor system stability, and ensure operational continuity. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for granting access to production data sources and confirming operational readiness. - Migration of all designated data warehouse workloads to Microsoft Fabric. - Full configuration of data gateways and integration points. - User acceptance testing and sign-off by UMS. - Transition to operational support. ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support A structured support model will be implemented to ensure smooth transition and ongoing operations. The timeline is as follows: ### D-7 (Pre-Migration) - Final readiness review. - Backup of source data. - Confirmation of migration schedule. ### D0 (Cutover) - Execution of migration activities. - Real-time monitoring and issue resolution. ### D+1 (Post-Migration) - Post-migration validation. - User support and troubleshooting. - Documentation handover. | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-----------------|-----------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------| | Pre-Migration | Backup, readiness review | UMS | Not-Started | | Cutover | Migration execution, monitoring | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | Post-Migration | Validation, support, documentation | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | Enfrasys Solutions will provide ongoing support and knowledge transfer to Universiti Malaysia Sabah to ensure long-term operational success. # 9.0 Appendix ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Machines: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Subscription Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits ### Others Azure Compute SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/virtual-machines/ ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Network: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Networking Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-network-limits ### Others Azure Network SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/virtual-network/ ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/ ### Limits and Boundaries Entra ID Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-scale/service-limits ### Others Azure Security Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/ ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/monitoring-limits ### Others Azure Monitoring SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/monitor/ ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Governance Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Policy Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts ### Others Azure Management Groups: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/ ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Fabric-specific ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/ ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric Capacity and Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/real-time-analytics/limits ### Others Microsoft Fabric SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/microsoft-fabric/

This is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving the following components and data flow:

1. **Fabric-RG**:  
   - Represents a resource group in Azure, likely hosting resources related to the "UMS Fabric."  
   - The resource group acts as a logical container for managing and organizing Azure resources.

2. **UMS Fabric**:  
   - A service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.  
   - It appears to be the core system or platform that external users interact with.

3. **Conditional Access**:  
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric.  
   - It enforces policies based on conditions such as user identity, device compliance, location, or risk level.

4. **External User**:  
   - Represents an end-user or client accessing the UMS Fabric.  
   - Access is mediated through the Conditional Access layer to ensure security.

**Data Flow**:  
- The arrow indicates a unidirectional flow of access or interaction from the UMS Fabric to the External User.  
- Before granting access, the Conditional Access layer evaluates the user's request against predefined security policies.  
- If the conditions are met, the user is granted access to the UMS Fabric resources.

This architecture emphasizes secure access management for external users interacting with the UMS Fabric, leveraging Azure's Conditional Access capabilities.

This image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**:  
   - The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:  
   - The authentication request is evaluated against Entra Conditional Access policies. This involves three key stages:
     - **Signal**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are collected. Examples include:
       - Users and groups.
       - Network location.
     - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
     - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, allowing or denying access based on the policy configuration.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**:  
   - If access is granted, the user is directed to Microsoft Fabric.

4. **Microsoft Fabric and OneLake**:  
   - Microsoft Fabric provides a suite of tools and services, represented by icons for various components such as data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence.
   - OneLake is highlighted as a unified data lake within Microsoft Fabric, serving as a central repository for data storage and management.

This diagram effectively demonstrates the integration of Entra Conditional Access policies with Microsoft Fabric to ensure secure and conditional access to its services and data.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and analytics workflow within the Azure ecosystem, leveraging UMS Fabric and OneLake for data management and Power BI for visualization.

1. **Data Source**:  
   - The process begins with data ingestion from the **UMS Staging Area**, which is hosted on **Microsoft SQL Server 2017** and serves as the data warehouse.

2. **Data Ingestion**:  
   - **Azure Data Factory** is used to ingest data from the staging area into the UMS Fabric environment.

3. **Bronze Layer**:  
   - The ingested raw data is stored in **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**, representing the initial layer where data is minimally processed.

4. **Silver Layer**:  
   - Data is further processed and refined in the **XX_Silver_LK Tables**, where transformations and cleaning occur to prepare the data for advanced analytics.

5. **Data Science Integration**:  
   - The refined data is passed through **Azure Data Factory** to a **Data Science** component, enabling advanced analytics, machine learning, or predictive modeling.

6. **Gold Layer**:  
   - The output from the Data Science process is stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**, representing the final, highly curated dataset ready for consumption.

7. **Identity Management**:  
   - The **Identity_SM** component is integrated to manage security, access control, or identity-related operations for the data.

8. **Data Lake Integration**:  
   - The entire workflow is connected to **OneLake**, which acts as a unified data lake for storage and management.

9. **Visualization**:  
   - The curated data in the Gold Layer is connected to **Power BI** via OneLake, enabling business intelligence and visualization for end-users.

This architecture demonstrates a modern data pipeline leveraging Azure services for efficient data processing, storage, and analytics.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a data flow between three main components:

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:
   - Represents an on-premises SQL Server 2017 database used as a staging area or data warehouse.
   - It stores and processes data before it is transferred to other systems.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:
   - Acts as a bridge between the on-premises SQL Server and cloud-based services.
   - Facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened in the on-premises network.
   - Establishes an outbound secure connection to the cloud, ensuring data security and compliance.

3. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Represents a cloud-based service or platform that interacts with the on-premises data.
   - Receives data securely from the On-Premise Data Gateway for further processing, analysis, or integration.

**Data Flow**:
- Data flows from the SQL Server database to the On-Premise Data Gateway.
- The On-Premise Data Gateway establishes an outbound secure connection to the UMS Fabric, transferring data without requiring inbound ports.
- The UMS Fabric processes or utilizes the data as needed.

This architecture ensures secure, seamless integration between on-premises and cloud environments.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

### Description:
The diagram illustrates a data processing and machine learning workflow within a unified data platform, leveraging components such as **UMS Fabric**, **OneLake**, and **Power BI** for data storage, processing, and visualization.

1. **Data Source: XX_Silver_LK Tables**
   - Data originates from **XX_Silver_LK Tables**, which are part of the UMS Fabric ecosystem.
   - These tables serve as the input for machine learning workflows.

2. **Machine Learning Workflows**
   - **AutoML Workflow**:
     - Data from **XX_Silver_LK Tables** is fed into an **AutoML Experiment**.
     - The AutoML process automates model selection and training.
     - The results are scored using a notebook, producing outputs for further processing.
   - **Manual Machine Learning Workflow**:
     - Alternatively, data from **XX_Silver_LK Tables** is used for **Manual Machine Learning**.
     - This involves custom modeling using a notebook, allowing for more tailored model development.

3. **Processed Data Storage: XX_Gold_LK Tables**
   - The outputs from both AutoML and Manual Machine Learning workflows are stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**.
   - These tables represent the refined, processed data ready for downstream consumption.

4. **Identity_SM Component**
   - The **Identity_SM** module interacts with the **XX_Gold_LK Tables**, likely for identity resolution or data enrichment purposes.

5. **Visualization and Reporting: Power BI**
   - The processed data in **XX_Gold_LK Tables** is connected to **Power BI** via **OneLake**.
   - This enables advanced data visualization and reporting for business insights.

6. **Integration with OneLake**
   - **OneLake** acts as the unified data storage layer, facilitating seamless data access and integration across the workflow.

### Summary:
The architecture demonstrates a flexible machine learning pipeline with options for automated or manual model development. It integrates data storage, processing, and visualization components, ensuring a streamlined flow from raw data to actionable insights.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review

# 2.0 Executive Summary
This section summarizes the project context, document intent, and stakeholder audience for the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Microsoft Fabric and Power BI solution.

## 2.1 Project Overview
Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) has initiated a project to design a comprehensive data workflow leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The scope encompasses the establishment of a Fabric workspace, development of end-to-end ETL pipelines, execution of machine learning processes, and integration of Power BI for business-aligned analytics. The objectives are to enable seamless data ingestion, transformation, and visualization, aligning UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s core services: Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Data Science for machine learning applications.

## 2.2 Document Purpose
The Solution Design Document serves as the authoritative blueprint for the proposed architecture, workflow, and integration of Microsoft Fabric and Power BI within UMS. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for authoring this document, ensuring that all technical and business requirements are addressed. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall review and sign off on the document to confirm alignment with organizational objectives.

## 2.3 Document Audience
This document is intended for the following stakeholders:
- UMS Executive Leadership
- UMS IT Management
- UMS Data Analytics Team
- UMS Business Process Owners
- Enfrasys Solutions Project Architects
- Enfrasys Solutions Implementation Engineers

Both technical and business roles are included to ensure comprehensive understanding and alignment throughout the project lifecycle.

# 3.0 Microsoft Fabric, Power BI & Entra ID Overview
This section provides authoritative definitions and core features of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID, establishing the foundation for Universiti Malaysia Sabah's (UMS) data workflow and access design.

## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, data integration, and business intelligence within a single SaaS environment. Fabric consolidates multiple analytics workloads, enabling organizations to ingest, transform, store, and visualize data efficiently. Its architecture leverages OneLake—a unified data lake for all data assets—and integrates critical service components such as Data Factory, Synapse, Lakehouse, and Data Science.

### Core Features
- Unified workspace for analytics, ETL, and visualization.
- Seamless integration with OneLake for centralized data storage.
- Advanced orchestration and automation capabilities.
- Built-in governance and security controls.

### Service Components

| ID | Description                                      | Workload Type         |
|----|--------------------------------------------------|----------------------|
| 1  | Data Factory: Data integration and orchestration | Data Ingestion/ETL   |
| 2  | Synapse: Advanced analytics and big data         | Data Analytics       |
| 3  | Lakehouse: Unified storage for structured/unstructured data | Data Storage         |
| 4  | Data Science: Machine Learning and AI            | ML/AI                |
| 5  | OneLake: Centralized data lake                   | Data Storage         |

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the selection and configuration of Microsoft Fabric components. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall consume and operate within the Fabric workspace as provisioned.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\ccb0c0fb-39a1-450e-8d47-30f465864b06\images\slide_006_img_002.png)
Figure 1: Authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access.

## 3.2 Power BI

Power BI is a Microsoft business intelligence and analytics service designed to visualize, analyze, and share data insights across an organization. It provides interactive dashboards, reports, and data exploration tools, facilitating informed decision-making. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, allowing direct access to data stored in OneLake and processed through Fabric pipelines.

### Integration Points with Fabric
- Direct connectivity to Fabric Lakehouse and OneLake datasets.
- Embedded analytics within Fabric workspace.
- Real-time data visualization from Fabric ETL and ML workflows.

### Visualization Capabilities

| No. | Design Decision                    | Decision                           |
|-----|------------------------------------|------------------------------------|
| 1   | Visualization Platform             | Power BI                           |
| 2   | Data Source Integration            | Fabric Lakehouse, OneLake          |
| 3   | Business Analytics Alignment       | Custom dashboards for UMS business |

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for configuring Power BI integration and visualization templates. UMS shall utilize Power BI for business-aligned analytics and reporting.

## 3.3 Entra ID

Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is a cloud-based identity and access management solution that provides secure authentication, authorization, and Conditional Access for users and applications. Entra ID is essential for enforcing access policies, managing user identities, and integrating security controls across Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environments.

### Role in Authentication and Conditional Access
- Centralized user authentication for all Microsoft services.
- Conditional Access policies to enforce security requirements based on user, device, location, and risk signals.
- Integration with Microsoft Fabric to control inbound access and enforce compliance.

| No. | Policy                   | Decision                                    |
|-----|--------------------------|----------------------------------------------|
| 1   | Authentication           | Entra ID Single Sign-On                      |
| 2   | Conditional Access       | Entra ID Conditional Access Policy           |
| 3   | External User Access     | Policy evaluation before granting access     |

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing and implementing Entra ID authentication and Conditional Access policies. UMS shall manage user identities and comply with enforced access controls.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\ccb0c0fb-39a1-450e-8d47-30f465864b06\images\slide_006_img_001.png)
Figure 2: Conditional Access architecture for UMS Fabric workspace.

# 4.0 Network Design and Decision
This section outlines the network architecture and connectivity decisions for integrating Universiti Malaysia Sabah's on-premises data sources with Microsoft Fabric using a secure gateway approach.

## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview
Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall implement a secure data flow architecture to enable seamless integration between its on-premises SQL Server environment and Microsoft Fabric. Data is transferred from the UMS SQL Server 2017 staging area/data warehouse to Fabric via the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG), which is installed on a dedicated virtual machine (VM). The OPDG establishes outbound-only connections to Microsoft Fabric and related Microsoft cloud services, eliminating the need for inbound ports and enhancing security posture.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\ccb0c0fb-39a1-450e-8d47-30f465864b06\images\slide_011_img_004.png)
Figure 1: Secure data flow from UMS SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric via OPDG.

## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway

### Definition: Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway (On-Premises Data Gateway)
The Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway, also known as the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG), is a Microsoft service that enables secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and Microsoft cloud services such as Fabric, Power BI, and Azure Analysis Services. The gateway acts as a bridge, facilitating encrypted outbound communication without requiring inbound network access, thus maintaining compliance and security.

### Gateway Installation and VM Specification
Enfrasys Solutions shall deploy the OPDG agent on a dedicated virtual machine within the UMS network. This VM is provisioned to meet Microsoft’s recommended specifications for optimal gateway performance and reliability. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for ensuring the readiness and accessibility of the on-premises SQL Server data source prior to gateway deployment.

#### VM Specifications

| Component        | Specification                                  |
|------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) |
| .NET Framework   | Version 4.8                                    |
| vCPU             | 8 cores                                        |
| RAM              | Minimum 8 GB                                   |

### Outbound Security Rules (NSG Configuration)
Enfrasys Solutions shall configure the necessary Network Security Group (NSG) rules to allow outbound traffic from the OPDG VM to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and authentication endpoints. These rules ensure secure communication and compliance with Azure networking best practices.

| Name                       | Priority | Source                | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                 | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|----------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services  | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication  | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160     | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                              | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

## 4.3 Task and Rollout Responsibility Matrix

| Gateway VM Provisioning    | Provision VM with required OS, .NET Framework, vCPU, RAM                                        | Enfrasys Solutions   | Pending     |
| OPDG Agent Installation    | Install and configure OPDG agent on VM                                                          | Enfrasys Solutions   | Pending     |
| NSG Rule Configuration     | Implement outbound-only NSG rules as per design                                                 | Enfrasys Solutions   | Pending     |
| On-Premises Data Source Preparation | Ensure SQL Server is accessible, network/firewall ready for outbound gateway connectivity | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending     |
| Connectivity Testing       | Validate outbound connectivity and data flow                                                    | Enfrasys Solutions   | Pending     |

## 4.4 Design Decisions

| No. | Design Decision                                                                                           | Decision                        |
|-----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------|
| 1   | Gateway deployment on dedicated VM with Microsoft-recommended specs                                       | Approved                        |
| 2   | Outbound-only NSG configuration to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and authentication endpoints               | Approved                        |
| 3   | Universiti Malaysia Sabah responsible for on-premises data source readiness                               | Confirmed                       |

# 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI
This section outlines the role definitions, access permissions, and assignment responsibilities for Universiti Malaysia Sabah within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.

## 5.1 Microsoft Fabric Administrator Role
Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform from Microsoft that unifies data integration, engineering, and visualization capabilities across multiple workloads, including Power BI, Data Factory, and Synapse. Fabric administrators are responsible for managing platform-wide settings, overseeing workspace creation and lifecycle, enforcing security and governance policies, and monitoring user activity.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah assign Fabric Administrator responsibilities to personnel with oversight of data analytics and platform governance. Administrators shall:
- Manage workspace provisioning and access.
- Configure tenant-level security and compliance settings.
- Monitor usage and audit logs.
- Assign and review workspace roles.

## 5.2 Microsoft-native Roles in Fabric & Power BI
Microsoft Fabric and Power BI provide a set of built-in roles to facilitate secure, efficient collaboration and data management. These roles include:

| Permission         | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer |
|--------------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------|
| Manage workspace   | Yes   | No          | No     | No     |
| Add/remove users   | Yes   | No          | No     | No     |
| Publish content    | Yes   | Yes         | Yes    | No     |
| Edit content       | Yes   | Yes         | Yes    | No     |
| View content       | Yes   | Yes         | Yes    | Yes    |
| Delete content     | Yes   | Yes         | No     | No     |

Common Microsoft-native roles include:
- Data Engineer: Designs and manages data pipelines, datasets, and integration workflows.
- Data Scientist: Performs advanced analytics, builds models, and interprets data insights.
- Contributor: Publishes and edits content within workspaces.
- Viewer: Accesses reports and dashboards without edit permissions.

Enfrasys Solutions shall perform role mapping to ensure alignment between UMS business requirements and Microsoft-native roles.

## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access Mapping
The following table details the recommended workspace role assignments for Universiti Malaysia Sabah, based on project stakeholders and responsibilities. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for role mapping, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall assign users to each role.

| 3   | Workspace Viewer    | Data Analyst / Stakeholder / Project Director | Director, Dr Farashazillah Binti Yahya, Stakeholders: Roslee Bin Talip, Dayangku Rozlina BTE PG. Aliuddin, Norhanieyati Binti Norhaizat, Zaidie @ Mohd Zaidie Bin Adilai, Professor Madya TS Dr Mohd Hanafi Bin Ahmad Hijazi |

# 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision
This section outlines the design, workflow, access, platform, resource organization, and governance decisions for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric deployment.

## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a single SaaS solution. It leverages OneLake as a unified data lake and supports advanced ETL, ML, and reporting workflows.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS adopt the following data workflow design considerations:

| ID   | Description                                                                                                                         | Workload Type      |
|------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|
| DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server.                                                                | Data Factory       |
| DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server.                                               | Data Engineering   |
| DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse.                                                    | Data Engineering   |
| DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse.                                                   | Data Engineering   |
| DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering   |
| DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold).                         | Data Factory       |
| DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Science       |
| DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse.                                        | Power BI           |
| DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                                             | Power BI           |
| DE01 | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation. The chosen data will be one of the programs selected from the overall Staging DB. | Data Engineering   |
| DE02 | The Project will primarily adopt the Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python) to enable custom feature engineering, algorithm tuning, and model optimization. | Data Science       |
| DE03 | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment to achieve more accurate and explainable results tailored to UMS’s datasets. | Data Science       |
| DE04 | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry to support model lifecycle management, versioning, and potential reuse across further adoption. | Data Science       |
| DE05 | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data. The scored outputs, predictions, probabilities, and performance metrics will be stored in the Gold Lakehouse. | Data Engineering   |
| DE06 | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results, ensuring consistency between AutoML and manual ML outputs. | Data Engineering   |
| DE07 | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) and Data Modelling will be implemented. | Power BI           |
| DE08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI           |

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\ccb0c0fb-39a1-450e-8d47-30f465864b06\images\slide_009_img_003.png)
Figure 1: High-level data workflow architecture.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\ccb0c0fb-39a1-450e-8d47-30f465864b06\images\slide_021_img_005.png)
Figure 2: Machine learning and AutoML workflow in Microsoft Fabric.

## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions

Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following design decisions for UMS’s Fabric data workflow:

| No. | Design Decision                                                                                                   | Decision                                                                                          |
|-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1   | Data ingestion from UMS Staging Area (SQL Server 2017) via Azure Data Factory into Bronze Lakehouse.              | Implemented as primary ETL pipeline.                                                              |
| 2   | Bronze, Silver, and Gold Lakehouse layers to structure data transformation and quality.                           | Adopted for separation of raw, cleansed, and curated data.                                        |
| 3   | Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and Visual Query for transformation and cleansing in Silver Lakehouse.                  | Enabled for flexible and scalable data engineering.                                                |
| 4   | Machine learning (AutoML and Manual ML) using Fabric Notebooks, with results stored in Gold Lakehouse.            | Adopted to support advanced analytics and model lifecycle management.                              |
| 5   | Semantic Model connects to Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) for data modeling.                            | Implemented for unified data modeling and reporting.                                               |
| 6   | Power BI Desktop connects to Semantic Model via OneLake for visualization and reporting.                          | Adopted as standard reporting workflow.                                                           |
| 7   | Model registry for trained ML models to support versioning and reuse.                                             | Enabled within Fabric Model Registry.                                                             |
| 8   | Scoring notebooks for applying models to new data, storing outputs in Gold Lakehouse.                             | Standardized for consistent analytics outputs.                                                     |

## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions

UMS shall implement robust access controls, leveraging Microsoft Entra ID for identity management and Power BI/Fabric workspace roles for data governance.

### Conditional Access
Conditional Access is a Microsoft Entra ID feature that allows organizations to enforce access policies based on user, location, device, and application context.

| ID  | Description                                                                                  | Decision                                      |
|-----|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| AC01| Require conditional access policies for all Fabric and Power BI workspace logins.             | Enforced via Entra ID; UMS to configure.      |

### MFA
Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a security mechanism requiring two or more verification methods for access, reducing the risk of unauthorized entry.

| ID  | Description                                                                                  | Decision                                      |
|-----|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| AC02| Enforce MFA for all administrative and contributor roles in Fabric and Power BI workspaces.   | Mandatory for UMS personnel.                  |

### Workspace Permissions

Microsoft Fabric and Power BI workspaces support role-based access control (RBAC) for granular permission management.

| ID  | Description                                                                                  | Decision                                      |
|-----|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| AC03| Assign workspace roles (Admin, Contributor, Member, Viewer) based on job function and data sensitivity. | Enfrasys to design; UMS to implement.         |

| Permission          | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer |
|---------------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------|
| Manage workspace    | Yes   | No          | No     | No     |
| Edit content        | Yes   | Yes         | No     | No     |
| View content        | Yes   | Yes         | Yes    | Yes    |
| Share content       | Yes   | Yes         | No     | No     |
| Publish datasets    | Yes   | Yes         | No     | No     |
| Access reports      | Yes   | Yes         | Yes    | Yes    |

## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size

Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following VM specifications for UMS’s Fabric and Power BI workloads:

| Component        | Specification                           |
|------------------|-----------------------------------------|
| Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) |
| .NET Framework   | Version 4.8                             |
| vCPU             | 8 cores                                 |
| RAM              | Minimum 8 GB                            |

## 6.5 Resource Organization Design

### Management Groups

Azure Management Groups are logical containers used to organize subscriptions for governance, policy enforcement, and resource management. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS establish management groups for staging, production, and analytics workloads to ensure clear separation and compliance.

### Naming and Tagging

Consistent naming conventions and tagging are critical for resource discoverability, cost management, and operational governance.

#### Azure Naming Convention

| Resource     | Abbreviation | Example                      |
|--------------|-------------|------------------------------|
| Capacity     | CAP         | stagingareadevcapacity01      |
| Workspace    | WS          | StudentAffairs_dev_WS         |
| Lakehouse    | LK          | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK      |
| Dataflow     | DF          | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF |
| Semantic Model | SM        | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM |
| Pipeline     | PL          | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL |
| Report       | Report      | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report |

#### Fabric Naming Convention

| No. | Item in Fabric   | Naming <Identity>-<Scenario/Activity>-<FabricItemName>          |
|-----|------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1   | Fabric Capacity  | stagingareadevcapacity01                                      |
| 2   | Workspace        | StudentAffairs_dev_WS                                         |
| 3   | Lakehouse        | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK<br>StudentAffairs_Silver_LK<br>Etc…  |
| 4   | Dataflow         | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF<br>StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF<br>Etc.. |
| 5   | Semantic Model   | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM                          |
| 6   | Pipeline         | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL<br>Etc..                        |
| 7   | Report           | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report                      |

#### Acronyms

| Resource     | Abbreviation |
|--------------|-------------|
| Capacity     | CAP         |
| Workspace    | WS          |
| Lakehouse    | LK          |
| Dataflow     | DF          |
| Semantic Model | SM        |
| Pipeline     | PL          |
| Report       | Report      |

## 6.6 Governance Consideration

### Governance Disciplines

Enfrasys Solutions will design governance policies; UMS shall ensure operational compliance.

| Discipline          | Owner            | Tool                   |
|---------------------|------------------|------------------------|
| Cost Management     | UMS              | Azure Cost Management  |
| Resource Tagging    | UMS              | Azure Policy           |
| Access Control      | Enfrasys         | Entra ID, RBAC         |
| Data Retention      | UMS              | Fabric Data Policies   |
| Audit & Compliance  | UMS              | Azure Monitor, Purview |

| Azure Tool              | Description                                 | Cost Management Discipline   |
|-------------------------|---------------------------------------------|-----------------------------|
| Azure Cost Management   | Tracks and analyzes cloud expenditure       | Cost Management             |
| Azure Policy            | Enforces tagging and resource standards     | Resource Tagging            |
| Entra ID                | Manages identity and access                 | Access Control              |
| Azure Monitor           | Provides audit and operational insights     | Audit & Compliance          |
| Purview                 | Ensures data governance and retention       | Data Retention              |

### Violation Triggers and Actions

| Trigger                                         | Action                                  | Responsible      |
|-------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------|------------------|
| Unauthorized access attempt                     | Block access, alert security team        | UMS              |
| Missing resource tags                           | Apply tags, notify resource owner        | UMS              |
| Cost threshold exceeded                         | Notify finance, review resource usage    | UMS              |
| Data retention policy breach                    | Archive or delete data, escalate         | UMS              |
| Audit log anomaly                               | Investigate, escalate to compliance      | UMS              |

# 7.0 Security Design and Decision
This section outlines the security architecture for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), focusing on Azure Network Security Groups (NSG) and encryption strategies for data flows involving Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.

## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview
Azure Network Security Groups (NSG) are a core Azure security feature that enables granular control of inbound and outbound network traffic to Azure resources. NSGs use rules to allow or deny traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol, ensuring secure connectivity and compliance with organizational policies.

Enfrasys Solutions shall configure all NSG rules, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall monitor NSG activity and maintain compliance.

### Outbound NSG Rules

| Name                       | Priority | Source                | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                      | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|----------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services  | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication  | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160    | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                   | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

## 7.2 Encryption Design

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data and analytics workloads across Microsoft services, providing secure, scalable, and robust data processing capabilities. Power BI is a business intelligence service that enables users to visualize and share insights from their data securely.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) implement end-to-end encryption for all gateway, Fabric, and Power BI data flows. All communications between the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM and Microsoft cloud services shall be protected using SSL/TLS encryption.

### Gateway Encryption
- The OPDG VM communicates with Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints exclusively over HTTPS (TCP port 443), ensuring all data in transit is encrypted using SSL/TLS.
- Authentication traffic to Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) also uses HTTPS, securing identity and token exchanges.

### Fabric and Power BI Data Flow Encryption
- Data transfers between the gateway and Microsoft Fabric, as well as Power BI, are encrypted using SSL/TLS, preventing unauthorized access or interception.
- Connectivity tests to Microsoft endpoints are limited to HTTP for network status checks, but no sensitive data is transmitted.

### Responsibility Assignment

| Item                        | Activities                                                        | Action By            | Status      |
|-----------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------|-------------|
| NSG Rule Configuration      | Create and apply outbound NSG rules for OPDG VM                   | Enfrasys Solutions   | Pending     |
| Encryption Implementation   | Ensure SSL/TLS encryption for gateway, Fabric, and Power BI flows | Enfrasys Solutions   | Pending     |
| NSG Monitoring              | Monitor NSG logs and compliance                                   | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Ongoing     |
| Encryption Monitoring       | Audit SSL/TLS certificate validity and encryption status           | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Ongoing     |

![](diagram/security_network_flow.png)
Figure 1: Secure network flow between OPDG VM, Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI endpoints.

# 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach
This section outlines the structured deployment and migration workflow recommended for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), including pre-migration, pilot, production, and post-migration activities.

## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup
Enfrasys Solutions recommends a comprehensive pre-migration phase to ensure readiness and alignment between stakeholders. The following table details the planned activities, ownership, and status.

| No | Activities                                                       | Planned Start   | Action By       | Status       | Remarks           |
|----|------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------|-----------------|-------------|-------------------|
| 1  | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off         | Fri, 7-Nov-25   | Enfrasys, UMS   | Not-Started  |                   |
| 2  | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25  | UMS             | Not-Started  |                   |
| 3  | Fabric Environment Setup                                         | Wed, 12-Nov-25  | Enfrasys        | Not-Started  |                   |
| 4  | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS   | Not-Started  |                   |

Enfrasys Solutions shall execute technology setup and initial configuration, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall confirm data sources and participate in gateway installation.

## 8.2 Pilot
The pilot environment will serve as a controlled test bed for validating migration processes, configuration, and data integrity. Enfrasys Solutions shall provision the pilot Microsoft Fabric workspace, configure connectivity to the staging database, and install the On-Premises Data Gateway. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide access credentials, validate data ingestion, and review pilot outcomes.

Responsibilities:
- Enfrasys Solutions: Pilot workspace setup, gateway installation, migration testing, documentation.
- Universiti Malaysia Sabah: Data source confirmation, credential provision, pilot acceptance, feedback.

## 8.3 Production
Upon successful pilot completion, the production environment will be established and migrated. Enfrasys Solutions shall perform the production deployment, including final Fabric workspace configuration, data source integration, and operational validation. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall oversee acceptance testing and sign-off.

Production Go-Live Steps:
- Enfrasys Solutions: Execute production migration, monitor initial data loads, resolve migration issues, provide operational documentation.
- Universiti Malaysia Sabah: Validate migrated data, perform user acceptance testing, confirm operational readiness, sign off.

## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support
Post-migration activities ensure smooth transition and operational stability. Enfrasys Solutions will provide immediate support and knowledge transfer, after which Universiti Malaysia Sabah will assume ongoing support responsibilities.

Timeline Table:

| Item              | Activities                                             | Action By       | Status       |
|-------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|-----------------|-------------|
| D-7 (Pre-Migration) | Final review, user communication, backup confirmation | Enfrasys, UMS   | Scheduled    |
| D0 (Cutover)      | Migration execution, initial validation, issue resolution | Enfrasys        | Scheduled    |
| D+1 (Post-Migration Support) | Knowledge transfer, support handover, monitoring | Enfrasys, UMS   | Scheduled    |

Enfrasys Solutions shall provide post-migration support and knowledge transfer, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall accept operational responsibility and monitor ongoing performance.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving the following components and data flow:

1. **Fabric-RG**:  
   - Represents a resource group in Azure, likely hosting resources related to the "UMS Fabric."  
   - The resource group acts as a logical container for managing and organizing Azure resources.

2. **UMS Fabric**:  
   - A service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.  
   - It appears to be the core system or platform that external users interact with.

3. **Conditional Access**:  
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric.  
   - It enforces policies based on conditions such as user identity, device compliance, location, and risk level.

4. **External User**:  
   - Represents an end-user or client accessing the UMS Fabric.  
   - The user is subject to Conditional Access policies before being granted access.

**Data Flow**:  
- The arrow indicates a unidirectional flow from the UMS Fabric to the External User.  
- Before the external user can access the UMS Fabric, their request is evaluated against the Conditional Access policies.  
- If the conditions are met, access is granted, ensuring secure interaction with the UMS Fabric.

This architecture emphasizes secure access control for external users interacting with the UMS Fabric, leveraging Azure's Conditional Access capabilities.

This image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**:  
   - The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:  
   - The authentication request is evaluated against Entra Conditional Access policies. This involves three key stages:
     - **Signal (Step 3)**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are analyzed. Examples include:
       - Users and groups.
       - Network location.
     - **Decision (Step 4)**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
     - **Enforcement (Step 5)**: The decision is enforced, either granting or denying access based on the policy evaluation.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**:  
   - If access is granted, the user is allowed inbound access to Microsoft Fabric.

4. **Microsoft Fabric and OneLake**:  
   - Microsoft Fabric is depicted as a suite of integrated services, including various components such as:
     - Data Factory.
     - Synapse Data Engineering.
     - Synapse Data Science.
     - Synapse Data Warehousing.
     - Power BI.
     - Synapse Real-Time Analytics.
   - OneLake is highlighted as a unified data lake within the Microsoft Fabric ecosystem.

This diagram effectively communicates the flow of authentication and access control, emphasizing the role of Entra Conditional Access policies in securing access to Microsoft Fabric and its components.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and analytics workflow within the Azure ecosystem, leveraging UMS Fabric, Data Factory, OneLake, and Power BI.

1. **Data Source**:  
   - The process begins with data ingestion from the **UMS Staging Area**, which is hosted on **Microsoft SQL Server 2017** and serves as the data warehouse.

2. **Data Ingestion**:  
   - **Azure Data Factory** is used to ingest data from the staging area into the UMS Fabric environment.

3. **Bronze Layer**:  
   - The ingested data is stored in **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**, representing the raw data layer. This layer is used for initial storage without transformations.

4. **Silver Layer**:  
   - Data is processed and transformed using **Azure Data Factory** and moved to **XX_Silver_LK Tables**, which represent the cleaned and enriched data layer.

5. **Data Science Integration**:  
   - The **Data Science** component is integrated into the pipeline, enabling advanced analytics, machine learning, or predictive modeling on the Silver Layer data.

6. **Gold Layer**:  
   - The processed and refined data is stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**, representing the final, curated data layer ready for consumption.

7. **Identity Management**:  
   - The **Identity_SM** component is used for managing security, access control, or identity-related operations on the Gold Layer data.

8. **Data Lake Integration**:  
   - The entire workflow is integrated with **OneLake**, which acts as a unified data lake for storage and sharing across the ecosystem.

9. **Visualization and Reporting**:  
   - The curated data in the Gold Layer is connected to **Power BI** via OneLake for visualization, reporting, and business intelligence.

This architecture demonstrates a modern data pipeline leveraging Azure services for efficient data processing, transformation, and analytics.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a data flow between three main components:

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:  
   - This represents an on-premises SQL Server 2017 database used as a staging area or data warehouse. It stores structured data for processing and analysis.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:  
   - The OPDG acts as a bridge between the on-premises SQL Server and cloud-based services. It facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened in the on-premises network.  
   - The gateway establishes an outbound secure connection to the cloud, ensuring data security and compliance.

3. **UMS Fabric**:  
   - This is a cloud-based service or platform that interacts with the on-premises data through the OPDG. It likely represents a data processing, analytics, or integration layer in the cloud.

**Data Flow**:  
- Data flows bidirectionally between the SQL Server and the OPDG.  
- The OPDG securely connects to the UMS Fabric in the cloud using an outbound connection, eliminating the need for inbound ports.  
- The UMS Fabric processes or utilizes the data for further operations, such as analytics, reporting, or integration with other systems.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure-based ecosystem.

### Components and Data Flow:
1. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Acts as the overarching framework for the workflow, encompassing all components.

2. **Data Source: XX_Silver_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the intermediate data layer (Silver tables) stored in **OneLake**.
   - Serves as the input for machine learning processes.

3. **Machine Learning Options**:
   - **AutoML**:
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Automates the machine learning process, including feature selection, model training, and evaluation.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: Outputs predictions or scores based on the trained model.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modeling (Using Notebook)**: Allows for custom model development and experimentation using notebooks.

4. **Processed Data: XX_Gold_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the refined data layer (Gold tables) generated after machine learning processes.
   - Stores the final outputs for downstream consumption.

5. **Identity_SM**:
   - A specialized component for identity or metadata management, likely used for data governance or security.

6. **Power BI**:
   - Connects to the **XX_Gold_LK Tables** via **OneLake**.
   - Enables visualization and reporting of the processed data.

### Workflow Summary:
- Data from **XX_Silver_LK Tables** is processed through either **AutoML** or **Manual Machine Learning** workflows.
- The outputs are stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables** and managed by **Identity_SM**.
- The refined data is visualized in **Power BI**, leveraging the integration with **OneLake** for seamless connectivity.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.
