# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the project objectives, document purpose, and intended audience for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s Microsoft Fabric and Power BI solution. ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) has engaged Enfrasys Solutions to design a comprehensive data workflow leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The primary business objective is to enable end-to-end data ingestion, transformation, and visualization for UMS’s business use cases. The project scope includes establishing a Fabric workspace, developing ETL pipelines, executing machine learning processes, and integrating Power BI for actionable analytics. The solution will utilize Microsoft Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Fabric Data Science for machine learning applications. This design aligns with UMS’s cloud adoption strategy and aims to deliver scalable, secure, and business-aligned analytics capabilities. ## 2.2 Document Purpose This Solution Design Document is prepared by Enfrasys Solutions to formally detail the technical architecture, design decisions, and implementation approach for the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI deployment at Universiti Malaysia Sabah. The document serves as the authoritative reference for the solution’s infrastructure, workflow, and governance. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the preparation and accuracy of this document; Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall review and sign off on its contents as part of the project governance process. ## 2.3 Document Audience This document is intended for the following stakeholders: - UMS Executive Leadership - UMS IT Management - UMS Data Engineering Teams - UMS Business Analytics Teams - Enfrasys Solutions Project Managers - Enfrasys Solutions Solution Architects Both technical and business roles are included to ensure alignment between strategic objectives and technical implementation. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric, Power BI & Entra ID Overview This section provides formal definitions, core features, and service components for Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID. Enfrasys Solutions will deliver technical context; Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall validate the relevance for business alignment. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a single SaaS environment. It enables organizations to ingest, transform, store, and visualize data securely and efficiently, leveraging cloud-native capabilities. ### Core Features - End-to-end data workflow orchestration - Centralized data lake (OneLake) - Integrated analytics and machine learning - Seamless workspace management ### Service Components | ID | Description | Workload Type | |----|---------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------| | 1 | Data Factory: Data integration and orchestration | ETL/Data Integration | | 2 | Synapse: Advanced analytics and big data processing | Analytics/Big Data | | 3 | Data Science: Machine Learning application | ML/Data Science | | 4 | OneLake: Unified, secure data storage | Data Lake/Storage | Enfrasys Solutions recommends establishing a Fabric workspace for UMS, developing end-to-end ETL pipelines, and conducting machine learning processes to support business analytics. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is a business analytics service by Microsoft that provides interactive visualizations and business intelligence capabilities with an interface simple enough for end users to create their own reports and dashboards. It enables organizations to visualize data, share insights, and drive informed decision-making. ### Visualization and Integration - Interactive dashboards and reports - Real-time data monitoring - Integration with Microsoft Fabric for seamless data access and visualization Enfrasys Solutions will integrate Power BI within the Fabric environment to deliver business-aligned analytics for Universiti Malaysia Sabah. ## 3.3 Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is a cloud-based identity and access management service. It enables secure authentication, authorization, and user management for applications and resources, supporting conditional access policies and compliance requirements. ### Identity Management and Conditional Access - Centralized user identity management - Multi-factor authentication (MFA) - Conditional access policies for enhanced security Enfrasys Solutions will provide technical guidance for Entra ID integration, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall validate and align identity management requirements with organizational policies. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network connectivity architecture and gateway deployment for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric solution. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview The network architecture connects UMS’s on-premises SQL Server (staging/data warehouse), the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM, and the UMS Fabric cloud environment. Secure data flows are established from the SQL Server through the OPDG to Fabric, ensuring compliance with Azure networking best practices. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\81bdbb4e-f27e-4746-a509-173409972b37\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Secure data flow from on-premises SQL Server to UMS Fabric via OPDG. ## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway ### Definition: Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway The Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway (On-Premises Data Gateway, OPDG) is a Microsoft service that securely bridges cloud-based applications (such as Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure Logic Apps) with on-premises data sources. The gateway enables encrypted, outbound-only connectivity, ensuring that no inbound ports are exposed and that data transfers remain compliant and secure. ### OPDG Role and Installation The OPDG will be deployed as a dedicated virtual machine within the UMS on-premises environment. It acts as the intermediary between SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric, facilitating secure data transfer and authentication. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing and implementing the OPDG, including VM provisioning, gateway installation, and configuration. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide access to the on-premises SQL Server and necessary network permissions. #### VM Specifications | Component | Specification | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ### Network Security Group (NSG) Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ### Task/Migration Rollout | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |---------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------|------------| | OPDG VM Provision | Provision VM with required OS, CPU, RAM, and .NET Framework | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | OPDG Installation | Install and configure On-Premises Data Gateway agent | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | NSG Configuration | Apply outbound rules for HTTPS, authentication, and connectivity | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | On-Premises Access | Provide access to SQL Server and internal network | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending | ## 4.3 Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Gateway VM to be deployed on-premises with secure outbound-only connectivity | Approved; minimizes attack surface and aligns with Microsoft best practices | | 2 | NSG rules to restrict outbound traffic to Microsoft endpoints only | Approved; ensures compliance and reduces risk | | 3 | VM sizing to meet Microsoft gateway requirements | Approved; ensures performance and reliability | | 4 | Enfrasys to manage gateway deployment; UMS to provide access | Approved; clear responsibility assignment | # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and access mappings for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence in a single SaaS solution. It enables organizations to manage, analyze, and visualize data securely and efficiently. Fabric Administrators are responsible for overseeing the governance, configuration, and security of the Fabric environment. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS assign Fabric Administrators to perform the following duties: | Capability | Description | |------------|-------------| | Environment Management | Configure, monitor, and maintain Fabric workspaces and resources. | | Access Control | Assign, review, and revoke user permissions across workspaces. | | Security Policy Enforcement | Implement and monitor security policies, including data protection and compliance. | | Resource Allocation | Manage resource quotas, workspace creation, and capacity planning. | | Audit & Compliance | Conduct regular audits and generate compliance reports. | | Integration Oversight | Manage integrations with Power BI, Azure, and other data platforms. | UMS shall designate personnel to the Fabric Administrator role and ensure they are trained on platform governance and security best practices. ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS utilize the following native roles within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI to ensure proper segregation of duties and streamlined workflow: - **Data Engineer**: Responsible for designing, building, and maintaining data pipelines and datasets. - **Business User Admin**: Manages workspace access for business users and oversees data usage policies. - **Data Analyst**: Performs data analysis, creates reports, and develops dashboards for business insights. - **Stakeholder**: Reviews analytics outputs, provides feedback, and participates in decision-making. - **Project Director**: Oversees project progress, ensures alignment with organizational objectives, and approves key deliverables. ## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following workspace role mapping for Universiti Malaysia Sabah. UMS shall assign personnel to each role as indicated below: | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-----|---------------------|--------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias

Enfrasys Solutions will provide guidance and best practices for role assignment and access management. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision This section details the design, workflow, access, resource organization, VM sizing, and governance for Microsoft Fabric within Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) data analytics environment. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, data warehousing, and business intelligence capabilities. It leverages OneLake for centralized data storage and supports seamless data movement, transformation, and visualization through services such as Data Factory, Synapse, and Power BI. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following data workflow considerations for UMS, covering both ETL and ML workloads: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, Visual Query in SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (bronze to silver, silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used for ML process; results stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling implemented in Semantic Model using Gold Lakehouse data. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop connects to Semantic Model via OneLake for visualization. | Power BI | | DE01 | Cleaned data in Silver Lakehouse is input for ML experimentation (selected from Staging DB). | Data Engineering | | DE02 | Manual ML approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark/Python) for feature engineering, algorithm tuning, model optimization. | Data Science | | DE03 | Model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning performed in Notebook for tailored results. | Data Science | | DE04 | Trained models registered in Fabric Model Registry for lifecycle management, versioning, reuse. | Data Science | | DE05 | Scoring Notebooks apply models to new/unseen data; scored outputs stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DE06 | Gold Lakehouse is standardized repository for modeled/scored results (AutoML/manual ML outputs). | Data Engineering | | DE07 | Semantic Model connects to Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake); data modeling implemented. | Power BI | | DE08 | Power BI Desktop connects to Semantic Model via OneLake for visualization. | Power BI | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\81bdbb4e-f27e-4746-a509-173409972b37\images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow architecture leveraging Fabric, Data Factory, OneLake, and Power BI. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\81bdbb4e-f27e-4746-a509-173409972b37\images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: Machine learning workflow within Fabric, integrating AutoML, manual ML, and Power BI. ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01| Data ingestion from UMS Staging Area (SQL Server 2017 DW) via Data Factory. | Enfrasys Solutions will design and implement ETL pipelines. UMS to provide source access. | | DD02| Multi-layer Lakehouse structure (Bronze, Silver, Gold) for data transformation. | Enfrasys Solutions will architect Lakehouse layers. UMS to approve structure. | | DD03| Data cleansing/transformation using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, Visual Query. | Enfrasys Solutions will configure workflows. | | DD04| ML experimentation with AutoML and manual ML using Fabric Notebooks. | Enfrasys Solutions will design ML pipelines. UMS to supply datasets and approve models. | | DD05| Models registered in Fabric Model Registry for lifecycle management. | Enfrasys Solutions will implement registry process. | | DD06| Semantic Model and Power BI integration via OneLake Direct Lake. | Enfrasys Solutions will configure Power BI connection. | ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Conditional Access Microsoft Entra ID provides identity and access management for Azure and Microsoft 365 environments. Conditional Access is a policy-based security feature that allows organizations to control access to applications based on user, location, device compliance, and risk signals. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\81bdbb4e-f27e-4746-a509-173409972b37\images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 3: Secure access architecture for UMS Fabric using Conditional Access. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\81bdbb4e-f27e-4746-a509-173409972b37\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 4: Authentication and enforcement flow for Microsoft Fabric via Entra Conditional Access. #### Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) MFA is enforced at the tenant level for UMS, as Conditional Access policies cannot be fully applied due to licensing limitations (UMS currently does not have Entra ID P1/P2). Enfrasys Solutions recommends future evaluation of Conditional Access once appropriate licensing is acquired. | No. | Conditional Access | |------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Configure policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, Azure Storage | | DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | | DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | | DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | **Access Limitations:** UMS account is not eligible for Conditional Access due to current licensing. MFA is enforced at the tenant level. Private Link integration is deferred for future implementation. Additional licenses (Entra ID P1/P2) are required for advanced Conditional Access. **Future Options:** UMS may revisit Conditional Access and Private Link once licensing is upgraded and requirements are reassessed. ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size | Component | Specification | |------------------|-------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | Enfrasys Solutions will provision and configure the OPDG VM based on these specifications. UMS is responsible for validating compatibility with internal applications. ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Azure Management Groups provide a hierarchical structure for organizing subscriptions and resources, enabling centralized governance, policy assignment, and access management. Enfrasys Solutions will design management group hierarchy for UMS’s Fabric workloads. UMS shall review and approve the structure. ### Naming and Tagging Consistent naming conventions and tagging are essential for resource discoverability, cost tracking, and operational management. #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |--------------------|-------------|------------------------| | Resource Group | RG | Fabric-RG | | Virtual Machine | VM | OPDG-VM | | Storage Account | SA | umsdatastorage01 | | Data Factory | DF | umsdatafactory01 | | Lakehouse | LK | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK | | Workspace | WS | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming -- | |-----|--------------------|----------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK, StudentAffairs_Silver_LK, etc.| | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF, StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF, etc. | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL, etc. | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | Resource | Abbreviation | |------------------|-------------| | Lakehouse | LK | | Dataflow | DF | | Semantic Model | SM | | Pipeline | PL | | Workspace | WS | UMS is responsible for providing final approval of naming conventions and tags. ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### Governance Disciplines Azure governance encompasses cost management, resource consistency, security, and compliance. Enfrasys Solutions will design governance policies for UMS’s Fabric environment. | Azure Tool | Description | Cost Management Discipline | |-----------------------|---------------------------------------------|----------------------------------| | Azure Cost Management | Tracks and analyzes cloud spending | Budgeting, cost allocation | | Azure Policy | Enforces resource compliance | Resource consistency | | Azure Blueprints | Automates deployment of compliant resources | Standardization | | Azure Monitor | Provides operational insights | Usage tracking | ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |---------------|-----------------------------------------|---------------------|---------| | Naming Violation | Review and remediate resource names | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Tagging Violation | Audit and enforce tagging standards | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Cost Overrun | Alert and optimize resource usage | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Access Violation | Investigate and remediate access issues | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Policy Violation | Enforce Azure Policy compliance | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Data Source Provision | Provide access to staging DB and approve structure | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending | | Naming Approval | Review and approve naming conventions | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending | --- **Ownership Assignment:** - Enfrasys Solutions: Workflow design, VM specification, governance policy, resource organization, access configuration. - Universiti Malaysia Sabah: Data source provisioning, naming/tagging approval, licensing review for Conditional Access. --- **Timelines:** - Pre-Migration (D-7): UMS to provide data sources and approve naming conventions. - Migration/Deployment (D0): Enfrasys Solutions to implement workflow, VM, governance, and access. - Post-Migration Support (D+1): Enfrasys Solutions to monitor, remediate violations, and optimize environment. # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture for Universiti Malaysia Sabah, focusing on Azure Network Security Group (NSG) configuration and encryption standards for all workloads. ## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a core security feature of Microsoft Azure, providing granular control over inbound and outbound traffic to Azure resources. NSGs use rule-based filtering to permit or deny network traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol parameters. Enfrasys Solutions recommends NSGs to enforce secure connectivity for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine, ensuring compliance with Microsoft best practices. ### Inbound Rules No inbound rules are required for the OPDG VM in the current design, as all communication is outbound to Microsoft cloud services. ### Outbound Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |----------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | Enfrasys Solutions shall configure these outbound rules in Azure. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall validate compliance with internal and regulatory requirements. ## 7.2 Encryption Design Encryption is fundamental to protecting sensitive data and ensuring compliance with Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s security requirements. ### SSL/TLS Encryption SSL/TLS (Secure Sockets Layer/Transport Layer Security) are cryptographic protocols designed to secure communications over networks. All connections between the OPDG VM and Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related cloud endpoints will use TLS 1.2 or higher, ensuring data confidentiality and integrity during transit. ### Data-at-Rest Encryption Data-at-rest encryption protects stored information from unauthorized access. Azure Storage automatically encrypts data at rest using AES 256-bit encryption. All data stored in Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and associated Azure services will be encrypted at rest, leveraging Azure-managed keys. ### Data-in-Transit Encryption Data-in-transit encryption ensures that information moving between the OPDG VM and Microsoft cloud services is protected from interception. All outbound traffic permitted by NSG rules will utilize HTTPS (TCP port 443), enforcing TLS encryption for data in transit. Enfrasys Solutions shall implement and validate encryption configurations for all relevant Azure resources. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall review and confirm compliance with university and regulatory standards. ## 7.3 Security Task Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------|---------------| | Configure NSG Outbound Rules | Set up NSG rules for OPDG VM as per design | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Implement SSL/TLS Encryption | Ensure all outbound connections use TLS 1.2 or higher | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Enable Data-at-Rest Encryption | Validate Azure Storage and related services use AES 256-bit encryption | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Compliance Validation | Review NSG and encryption settings for regulatory and university policy compliance | Universiti Malaysia Sabah| Pending | ## 7.4 Security Architecture Diagram ![](security_architecture_azure_nsg.png) Figure 1: Azure NSG and encryption architecture for OPDG connectivity. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the formal deployment and migration workflow for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), detailing responsibilities, timelines, and activities for each phase. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup (D-7) Enfrasys Solutions will lead all technical setup and deployment activities. UMS shall provide necessary resources, confirm infrastructure readiness, and review deliverables. The following table summarizes the pre-migration tasks: | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|--------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|------------------|------------|------------------------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started| | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started| | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started| | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started| | ## 8.2 Pilot (D-3) The pilot phase will commence three days prior to production cutover. Enfrasys Solutions will execute a limited migration and validate end-to-end data workflows, connectivity, and user access. UMS shall provide feedback on pilot outcomes and participate in user acceptance testing. Pilot activities include: - Migrating a subset of data from the staging area database to Microsoft Fabric. - Validating On-Premises Data Gateway connectivity and performance. - Conducting initial user access and permissions testing. - Collecting feedback from UMS stakeholders for refinement. ## 8.3 Production (D0) On cutover day, Enfrasys Solutions will execute the full migration to production. UMS shall provide operational support and coordinate with stakeholders for go-live readiness. Production activities include: - Migrating all required datasets to Microsoft Fabric. - Enabling full user access for UMS personnel. - Performing final validation of data workflows and reporting. - Monitoring system performance and resolving any immediate issues. ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support (D+1) Post-migration support will begin immediately after go-live. Enfrasys Solutions will provide operational support, address user-reported issues, and monitor system stability. UMS shall submit feedback and escalate any concerns. Support tasks include: - Providing technical support for migrated workloads. - Monitoring Fabric environment health and gateway connectivity. - Conducting post-migration review with UMS stakeholders. - Documenting lessons learned and improvement actions. # 9.0 Appendix This section provides reference materials, technical prerequisites, and authoritative Microsoft documentation relevant to Universiti Malaysia Sabah's solution. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for documenting technical details; Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall review and provide feedback. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Machines: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/ Windows Server Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/ .NET Framework Guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/framework/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Subscription Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits VM Size and Quota Restrictions: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes ### Others Azure Compute SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/virtual-machines/ Azure VM Pricing Calculator: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/calculator/ ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Network Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/ Azure Network Security Groups: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/network-security/network-security-groups-overview ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Networking Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-network-limits NSG Rules and Restrictions: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/network-security/network-security-groups-overview#nsg-rules ### Others Azure Networking SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/virtual-network/ Azure Network Pricing: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/virtual-network/ ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/ Azure Security Center Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/ ### Limits and Boundaries Entra ID Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/azure-ad-service-limits Azure Security Boundaries: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/overview ### Others Azure Identity SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/active-directory/ Azure Security Best Practices: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/best-practices ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/ Azure Log Analytics Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/limits Log Analytics Workspace Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-overview#workspace-limits ### Others Azure Monitoring SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/monitor/ Azure Monitoring Pricing: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/monitor/ ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Governance Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/ Azure Resource Manager Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Policy Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts Management Group and Subscription Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/management-groups ### Others Azure Governance SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/azure-resource-manager/ Azure Cost Management Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/ ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Microsoft Fabric-specific ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/ Fabric Dataflow Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/dataflows/ ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric Capacity Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/service-limits Fabric Workspace Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/workspaces/ ### Others Microsoft Fabric SLAs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/legal/sla/fabric/ Fabric Pricing and Licensing: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/service-pricing

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

**Description:**

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving Azure Resource Groups, a UMS Fabric component, and Conditional Access for external users. The components and their interactions are as follows:

1. **Fabric-RG (Azure Resource Group):**
   - Represents a logical container in Azure that holds related resources for the UMS Fabric.
   - The icon suggests it is part of the Azure ecosystem, managing and organizing resources.

2. **UMS Fabric:**
   - A service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.
   - Likely a custom or third-party solution designed for user management or service delivery.

3. **Conditional Access:**
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric.
   - Enforces policies based on user identity, device compliance, location, or other conditions.

4. **External User:**
   - Represents an end-user or client accessing the UMS Fabric.
   - Access is mediated by Conditional Access policies to ensure secure and compliant interactions.

**Data Flow:**
- The external user initiates a request to access the UMS Fabric.
- The request is routed through Conditional Access, which evaluates the user's credentials and compliance with security policies.
- Upon successful validation, the user is granted access to the UMS Fabric hosted within the Fabric-RG.

This architecture emphasizes secure access control for external users interacting with Azure-hosted resources.

This image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**:  
   - The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:  
   - The authentication request is evaluated against Entra Conditional Access policies. This involves three key stages:
     - **Signal**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are analyzed. Examples include:
       - Users and groups.
       - Network location.
     - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
     - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether the user is granted or denied access.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**:  
   - If access is granted, the user is allowed to interact with Microsoft Fabric.  
   - Microsoft Fabric includes various components and services, such as:
     - Data Factory.
     - Synapse Data Engineering.
     - Synapse Data Science.
     - Synapse Data Warehousing.
     - Synapse Real-Time Analytics.
     - Power BI.
   - The data is stored and managed in **OneLake**, a unified data lake for Microsoft Fabric.

This diagram effectively demonstrates the integration of conditional access policies with Microsoft Fabric to ensure secure and controlled access.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and analytics workflow within the Azure ecosystem, leveraging UMS Fabric, Data Factory, OneLake, and Power BI.

1. **Data Source**:  
   - The process begins with data ingestion from the **UMS Staging Area**, hosted on **Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse**. This serves as the primary data source.

2. **Data Ingestion**:  
   - **Azure Data Factory** is used to ingest data from the staging area into the UMS Fabric environment. This is the first step in the ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) pipeline.

3. **Bronze Layer**:  
   - The ingested raw data is stored in **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**. This layer represents the raw, unprocessed data.

4. **Silver Layer**:  
   - Data from the Bronze layer is processed and transformed using **Azure Data Factory** and stored in **XX_Silver_LK Tables**. This layer contains cleaned and enriched data, ready for further analysis.

5. **Data Science Integration**:  
   - The Silver layer data is further processed using **Data Science tools**. This step involves advanced analytics, machine learning, or predictive modeling.

6. **Gold Layer**:  
   - The output from the Data Science process is stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**. This layer contains highly curated, business-ready data.

7. **Identity Management**:  
   - The **Identity_SM** component is integrated at this stage, likely for managing access control, security, or user-specific data processing.

8. **Data Lake Integration**:  
   - The entire workflow is connected to **OneLake**, which acts as a unified data lake for storage and integration across the pipeline.

9. **Visualization and Reporting**:  
   - The final processed data in the Gold layer is connected to **Power BI** via OneLake for visualization, reporting, and business intelligence.

This architecture demonstrates a modern data pipeline leveraging Azure services for scalable, secure, and efficient data processing and analytics.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a data flow between three components:

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:
   - Represents an on-premises SQL Server 2017 database used as a staging area or data warehouse.
   - It stores and processes data before it is transferred to other systems.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:
   - Acts as a bridge between the on-premises SQL Server and cloud-based services.
   - Facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened in the on-premises network.
   - Establishes an outbound secure connection to the cloud, ensuring data security and compliance.

3. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Represents a cloud-based service or platform that consumes data from the on-premises SQL Server.
   - The connection to the UMS Fabric is established via the On-Premise Data Gateway.

**Data Flow**:
- Data flows from the SQL Server database to the On-Premise Data Gateway.
- The On-Premise Data Gateway securely transmits the data to the UMS Fabric using an outbound connection.
- The UMS Fabric processes or utilizes the data for further operations.

This architecture ensures secure and seamless integration between on-premises and cloud environments.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

### Description:

1. **Data Source:**
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Represents the intermediate data layer stored in OneLake, part of the UMS Fabric ecosystem. This layer serves as the input for machine learning processes.

2. **Machine Learning Processes:**
   - **AutoML**:
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Automated machine learning processes are applied to the data from the XX_Silver_LK Tables.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results of the AutoML experiment are scored and evaluated using a notebook.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modeling (Using Notebook)**: Data from the XX_Silver_LK Tables is manually processed and modeled using a notebook for custom machine learning workflows.

3. **Output Data Layer:**
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Represents the refined and processed data layer, which is the output of the machine learning processes (both AutoML and manual).
   - **Identity_SM**: A specific component or service that interacts with the XX_Gold_LK Tables for further processing or identity-related operations.

4. **Visualization and Reporting:**
   - The XX_Gold_LK Tables are connected to **Power BI** via **OneLake**, enabling data visualization and reporting.

5. **Integration:**
   - The entire workflow is encapsulated within the **UMS Fabric**, which integrates OneLake for data storage and connectivity.

### Data Flow:
- Data flows from the XX_Silver_LK Tables to either the AutoML or Manual Machine Learning processes.
- Processed data is stored in the XX_Gold_LK Tables.
- The XX_Gold_LK Tables are connected to Power BI for visualization, with OneLake serving as the underlying data storage and connectivity layer.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Microsoft Fabric Solution Design # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise summary of the project objectives, the purpose of this document, and its intended audience. ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is undertaking a strategic initiative to modernize its data workflow and analytics capabilities. The project is scoped to design and implement an end-to-end data workflow encompassing data ingestion, transformation, and visualization within Microsoft Fabric. Key objectives include establishing a Fabric workspace, developing robust ETL pipelines, enabling machine learning processes, and integrating Power BI for business-aligned analytics. The solution will leverage Microsoft Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Data Science components for machine learning applications. This initiative aligns UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s comprehensive platform capabilities. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The purpose of this Solution Design Document is to formally define the technical architecture, workflow, and integration approach for the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI implementation at Universiti Malaysia Sabah. The document serves as the authoritative reference for solution design, guiding implementation, migration, and subsequent operations. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for authoring this document, and Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall review and sign off on its contents. ## 2.3 Document Audience This document is intended for the following stakeholders: - Enfrasys Solutions (consulting and implementation team) - Universiti Malaysia Sabah project sponsors and business stakeholders - UMS IT department (system administrators and technical leads) - UMS analytics and business users Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for document authorship, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for review and sign-off. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Overview This section provides foundational definitions and technology context for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) data platform modernization, as recommended by Enfrasys Solutions. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, data integration, and business intelligence capabilities within a single SaaS environment. Fabric streamlines the end-to-end data workflow, enabling organizations to ingest, transform, analyze, and visualize data securely and efficiently. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for explaining the technical components and their applicability to UMS’s business requirements. ### Data Factory Fabric Data Factory is a cloud-based data integration and orchestration service. It enables organizations to create, schedule, and manage data pipelines for ingesting data from diverse sources, transforming datasets, and loading them into analytical stores such as Lakehouse or OneLake. ### Synapse Fabric Synapse provides advanced data analytics and big data processing capabilities. It combines data warehousing, big data analytics, and real-time analytics into a unified workspace, supporting complex ETL, SQL, and Spark workloads. ### Data Science Fabric Data Science offers integrated machine learning tools and environments for developing, training, and deploying predictive models. It supports collaborative ML workflows and seamless integration with the rest of the Fabric ecosystem. ### Lakehouse Lakehouse is a core storage architecture within Fabric, combining the reliability and performance of data warehouses with the flexibility of data lakes. It enables structured and unstructured data storage, supporting analytics and machine learning workloads. ### OneLake OneLake is Fabric’s unified, cloud-native data lake. It provides centralized, scalable storage for all data assets, facilitating secure data sharing and governance across multiple analytics workloads. UMS shall provide business context and requirements to inform the design and implementation of these Fabric components. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business intelligence platform designed for data visualization, analytics, and reporting. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for explaining the technology and its integration with Fabric; UMS shall provide business context for analytics requirements. ### Power BI Service The Power BI Service is a cloud-based platform for publishing, sharing, and collaborating on interactive reports and dashboards. It enables real-time data monitoring and supports integration with Microsoft Fabric for seamless analytics workflows. ### Power BI Desktop Power BI Desktop is a Windows application for designing, modeling, and visualizing data. It allows users to connect to multiple data sources, transform and shape data, and build sophisticated reports before publishing them to the Power BI Service. ### Integration with Fabric Power BI natively integrates with Microsoft Fabric, allowing users to connect to Lakehouse, OneLake, and other Fabric data assets. This integration ensures secure, scalable, and governed analytics directly on enterprise data. ## 3.3 Entra ID (Azure Active Directory) Microsoft Entra ID, formerly known as Azure Active Directory (Azure AD), is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. It enables secure authentication, authorization, and governance for users, applications, and resources across Microsoft 365, Azure, and Fabric environments. Entra ID provides single sign-on, conditional access, and role-based access controls, ensuring secure data platform operations. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for technical explanation; UMS shall define business roles and access requirements. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture, connectivity, and gateway requirements for integrating Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s on-premises SQL Server environment with Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) requires secure and reliable connectivity between its on-premises SQL Server, the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG), and Microsoft Fabric. The OPDG will be deployed within a virtual machine in UMS's environment, acting as a secure bridge to facilitate data transfer between the on-premises SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric. All connections from the OPDG to Microsoft Fabric are established via outbound secure channels, eliminating the need to open inbound ports on UMS’s network perimeter. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\73997dd1-b897-4e04-adb9-3edd4f1a4551\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Data flow between UMS SQL Server, OPDG, and Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway ### Definition: Microsoft Fabric and On-Premises Data Gateway Microsoft Fabric is a comprehensive analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a single SaaS environment. It enables organizations to centralize data management and analytics workloads, leveraging cloud scalability and security. The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that allows secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and cloud services such as Microsoft Fabric. The OPDG establishes outbound connections, ensuring data is transmitted securely without exposing internal resources to the internet. ### OPDG Architecture and Responsibilities Enfrasys Solutions recommends deploying the OPDG agent on a dedicated virtual machine within UMS’s secure network segment. The OPDG will connect to the on-premises SQL Server and facilitate data transfer to Microsoft Fabric. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the installation, configuration, and validation of the OPDG agent and VM. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide access credentials and network permissions to the on-premises SQL Server as required. ### VM Specifications for OPDG Deployment | Component | Specification | |------------------|-------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ### Task/Migration Rollout | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|------------| | OPDG VM Provisioning | Deploy VM per specification; OS and .NET installation | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | OPDG Agent Installation | Install and configure OPDG agent | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | SQL Server Access Setup | Provide DB access credentials and firewall rules | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending | | Connectivity Validation | Test secure outbound connection to Microsoft Fabric | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | ## 4.3 Data Flow and Security Considerations The OPDG architecture ensures that all data transfers between UMS’s SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric occur over encrypted outbound connections. No inbound ports are exposed, minimizing the attack surface and maintaining compliance with UMS’s security requirements. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for maintaining the security and availability of the on-premises SQL Server, while Enfrasys Solutions manages the OPDG deployment and ongoing connectivity monitoring. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\73997dd1-b897-4e04-adb9-3edd4f1a4551\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2: Authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric via Entra Conditional Access. ## 4.4 Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------| | 1 | OPDG to be deployed on dedicated VM with minimum 8 vCPU, 8 GB RAM, Windows OS | Approved | | 2 | All OPDG connections to Microsoft Fabric to use outbound encrypted channels | Approved | | 3 | Enfrasys Solutions to own OPDG deployment and connectivity validation | Approved | | 4 | Universiti Malaysia Sabah to provide SQL Server access and maintain DB security | Approved | # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI This section details the roles, responsibilities, and access mapping for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) within the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environment. ## 5.1 Microsoft Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, data warehousing, and business intelligence capabilities within a single SaaS environment. It provides centralized management, governance, and collaboration for data workflows across an organization. The Microsoft Fabric Administrator is responsible for overseeing the platform’s configuration, security, resource allocation, and compliance. Administrators manage workspace provisioning, enforce access policies, monitor usage, and ensure data governance standards are upheld. ### Administrator capabilities | Capability | Description | |---------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Platform Configuration | Set up and manage Fabric environment settings, including tenant-wide policies| | Security Management | Define and enforce access controls, authentication, and data protection | | Resource Provisioning | Create and manage workspaces, assign roles, and allocate resources | | Monitoring & Reporting | Track usage, audit activities, and generate compliance reports | | Integration Management | Connect Fabric with external data sources and services | | Governance Enforcement | Implement data governance, retention, and compliance policies | Enfrasys Solutions shall map administrator responsibilities and recommend best practices. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall assign qualified personnel to the administrator role. ## 5.2 Microsoft Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric supports a range of native roles to facilitate collaboration and workflow management: - **Data Engineer**: Designs, builds, and maintains data pipelines and datasets. - **Business User Admin**: Manages business user access, workspace permissions, and user onboarding. - **Data Analyst**: Creates reports, dashboards, and analyzes data for business insights. - **Stakeholder**: Reviews analytics outputs, provides feedback, and participates in decision-making. - **Project Director**: Oversees project execution, ensures alignment with organizational goals, and manages stakeholder engagement. Enfrasys Solutions shall define role responsibilities and map them to UMS requirements. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall assign personnel to each role based on operational needs. ## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access The following table maps workspace roles to suggested functional roles and identifies UMS personnel-in-charge (PIC) for each assignment. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for role mapping; Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for user assignment. | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-----|-----------------------|------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias

Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall complete user assignments and maintain role accuracy as organizational needs evolve. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Design and Decision This section outlines the solution design for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environment, addressing data workflow, access, platform, resource organization, and governance. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence capabilities in a single SaaS environment. Power BI is Microsoft’s industry-leading business intelligence tool for interactive data visualization and reporting, seamlessly integrated with Fabric. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following data workflow considerations for UMS: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering| | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering| | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering| | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering| | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | | DE01 | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation. | Data Engineering| | DE02 | The Project will primarily adopt the Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python). | Data Science | | DE03 | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment. | Data Science | | DE04 | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry. | Data Science | | DE05 | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data. | Data Engineering| | DE06 | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results. | Data Engineering| | DE07 | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) and Data Modelling will be implemented | Power BI | | DE08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions has designed the data workflow for UMS to ensure robust, scalable, and secure analytics. The workflow is structured as follows: - Data is ingested from the UMS Staging Area (Microsoft SQL Server 2017) using Azure Data Factory. - Raw data is stored in Bronze Lakehouse tables, then cleansed and transformed into Silver Lakehouse tables. - Machine learning experimentation (both AutoML and manual notebook-based approaches) is performed on Silver Lakehouse data. - Trained models are registered in the Fabric Model Registry; scoring notebooks apply models to new data, storing results in Gold Lakehouse tables. - Gold Lakehouse serves as the repository for curated, modeled, and scored results. - Identity management is enforced for secure access to Gold Lakehouse. - OneLake provides unified data access for Power BI, enabling direct visualization and reporting. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\73997dd1-b897-4e04-adb9-3edd4f1a4551\images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: High-level data workflow architecture. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\73997dd1-b897-4e04-adb9-3edd4f1a4551\images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: Machine learning workflow integration in Fabric. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Conditional Access Microsoft Entra ID provides conditional access policies to enforce security for cloud resources. However, UMS currently operates under Entra ID Free, which does not support conditional access policies. Enfrasys Solutions recommends enforcing MFA at the tenant level for all users accessing Fabric and Power BI. | No. | Conditional Access | |------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | | DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | | DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | | DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\73997dd1-b897-4e04-adb9-3edd4f1a4551\images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 3: Conditional access architecture for UMS Fabric. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend\api_uploads\73997dd1-b897-4e04-adb9-3edd4f1a4551\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 4: Authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric. ### Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Enfrasys Solutions recommends enforcing MFA at the tenant level for all UMS users accessing Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, as conditional access policies are not available under the current licensing. ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following VM specifications for supporting Power BI and Fabric workloads. UMS shall provide operational input regarding deployment and maintenance. | Component | Specification | |------------------|--------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Enfrasys Solutions recommends organizing Azure resources for UMS using management groups to ensure clear governance, policy enforcement, and billing separation. ### Naming and Tagging UMS shall implement the following naming conventions for Fabric resources, with operational input from UMS stakeholders. #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |-----------------|-------------|------------------------| | Resource Group | RG | Fabric-RG | | Virtual Machine | VM | BI-VM01 | | Storage Account | SA | umsdatastorage01 | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming -- | |-----|------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK
Etc… | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF
Etc.. | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL
Etc.. | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | No. | Acronym | Meaning | |-----|---------|-----------------| | 1 | LK | Lakehouse | | 2 | DF | Dataflow | | 3 | SM | Semantic Model | | 4 | PL | Pipeline | ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### Governance Disciplines Enfrasys Solutions recommends implementing the following governance disciplines for UMS’s Fabric and Power BI environment. | Azure Tool | Description | Cost Management Discipline | |-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------| | Cost Management | Azure native tool for monitoring and managing spend | Budgeting, Alerting, Analysis| | Policy | Enforce resource compliance and tagging | Resource Governance | | Management Groups | Hierarchical organization for policy and billing | Policy Enforcement | | Azure Monitor | Performance and operational monitoring | Usage Optimization | ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |----------------|---------------------------------------------------|--------------------|-----------------| | Cost Overrun | Trigger budget alerts, review usage | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | | Policy Violation| Audit tagging, enforce compliance | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | | Resource Naming| Review and approve naming convention | UMS | Planned | | Security Breach| Review access logs, enforce MFA | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | --- Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the design and implementation of workflow, access, VM, and governance. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for providing operational input and approving naming conventions. # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section defines the security architecture and operational controls for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Azure-based deployment, including network segmentation, encryption standards, and compliance responsibilities. ## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a fundamental security feature within Microsoft Azure, providing granular control over inbound and outbound traffic to resources. NSGs allow administrators to define rules that permit or deny traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol, thereby protecting virtual machines and services from unauthorized access. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing, implementing, and validating NSG rules. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall review and approve the configuration for compliance alignment. ### Outbound NSG Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |--------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services| 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication| 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | *No inbound NSG rules are required for OPDG as per current design; all inbound traffic is managed via Azure platform controls.* ## 7.2 Encryption Design ### Microsoft Fabric Encryption Overview Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, and business intelligence. Fabric leverages Azure’s built-in encryption mechanisms to secure data both at rest and in transit. #### Data-at-Rest Encryption All data stored within Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and OPDG is encrypted using Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) with AES-256, ensuring that information is protected from unauthorized access. Enfrasys Solutions will configure storage accounts and associated resources to enforce encryption at rest. #### Data-in-Transit Encryption Data exchanged between OPDG, Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI is protected using SSL/TLS protocols. All communications utilize HTTPS endpoints (TCP port 443), providing end-to-end encryption and integrity. Enfrasys Solutions will validate all gateway and service configurations to ensure SSL/TLS is enforced. #### Power BI Encryption Power BI is a cloud-based business analytics service that enables secure sharing, visualization, and analysis of data. Power BI inherently encrypts data at rest and in transit, leveraging Microsoft’s compliance framework. Enfrasys Solutions will ensure that Power BI workspaces and datasets adhere to UMS’s encryption policies. #### OPDG Encryption On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a secure bridge for transferring data between UMS’s on-premises environment and Microsoft cloud services. Enfrasys Solutions will configure OPDG to use HTTPS for all outbound connections, ensuring data-in-transit encryption. ### Encryption Responsibilities | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|-------------| | NSG Rule Implementation | Deploy and validate outbound NSG rules for OPDG VM | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Encryption Configuration | Configure and validate SSL/TLS, SSE, and HTTPS endpoints | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Compliance Review | Review encryption and NSG configuration for regulatory alignment | Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) | Pending | ## 7.3 Security Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|---------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Outbound NSG rules for OPDG | Only allow HTTPS and essential HTTP traffic to Microsoft endpoints, deny all other traffic | | 2 | Data-at-rest encryption | Enforce Azure Storage Service Encryption (AES-256) for all Fabric, Power BI, and OPDG data | | 3 | Data-in-transit encryption | Require SSL/TLS for all communications between OPDG, Fabric, and Power BI | | 4 | Compliance review | UMS to review and approve all security configurations for regulatory compliance | ![](./images/azure_nsg_opdg_topology.png) Figure 1: OPDG outbound network topology and NSG enforcement. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the structured deployment and migration methodology for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), assigning clear responsibilities and timelines to Enfrasys Solutions and UMS. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup The pre-migration phase establishes foundational tasks and prerequisites to ensure a seamless transition. Responsibilities are distinctly assigned to Enfrasys Solutions and UMS, with timelines aligned to project milestones. | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|-----------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|-------------------|--------------|--------------------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | | ### Timeline Structure - D-7: Initiate design document review and server confirmation. - D-4: Begin Fabric environment setup. - D0: Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) and finalize connectivity. ## 8.2 Pilot Enfrasys Solutions recommends establishing a pilot environment to validate migration processes and ensure operational readiness. The pilot will utilize a subset of UMS data within a dedicated staging area, leveraging the newly provisioned Fabric environment and On-Premises Data Gateway. Key objectives include: - Testing data ingestion workflows from the staging area DB. - Validating connectivity and access controls. - Assessing performance and resolving initial issues. Enfrasys Solutions will oversee pilot configuration, with UMS providing sample data and user feedback. ## 8.3 Production Following successful pilot validation, Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall implement the production rollout. The production phase involves: - Full-scale migration of data warehouse assets to the Fabric environment. - Activation of all data pipelines and integration services. - User onboarding and access provisioning. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for orchestrating the migration cutover (D0), monitoring system health, and ensuring compliance with security and operational standards. UMS shall coordinate internal communications and user acceptance testing. ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Post-migration support ensures operational stability and knowledge transfer. Activities include: - Monitoring system performance and resolving post-cutover issues (D+1 to D+7). - Conducting handover sessions and documentation reviews. - Providing ongoing support and escalation pathways. Enfrasys Solutions will deliver post-migration support and facilitate handover to UMS IT administrators. UMS is responsible for internal adoption, user training, and feedback collection. # 9.0 Appendix This section provides reference links and technical boundaries for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) as per Enfrasys Solutions’ design and operational recommendations. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Machines: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/ On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG): https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-install ### Limits and Boundaries Azure VM Size Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes Azure Subscription Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits ### Others Azure Compute SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/virtual-machines/ Microsoft .NET Framework Requirements: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/framework/get-started/system-requirements ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Network Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview Azure Network Security Groups: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/network-security-groups/network-security-groups-overview ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Networking Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-network-limits NSG Rules and Best Practices: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/network-security-groups/network-security-groups-how-to ### Others Azure Network SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/virtual-network/ Azure Outbound Connectivity: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/outbound-connectivity ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD): https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/ Azure Security Center Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-intro ### Limits and Boundaries Azure AD Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-scale/service-limits Azure Security Boundaries: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/overview ### Others Azure Security SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/active-directory/ Identity and Authentication Guidance: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/ ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview Azure Log Analytics: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/limits Log Analytics Workspace Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits ### Others Azure Monitoring SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/monitor/ Diagnostic Settings Guidance: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Resource Naming and Tagging: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cloud-adoption-framework/ready/azure-best-practices/resource-naming Azure Management Groups and Subscriptions: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/management-groups ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Policy Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/definition-structure Azure Subscription Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits ### Others Azure Governance SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/azure-policy/ Resource Organization Guidance: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cloud-adoption-framework/decision-guides/resource-organization ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/ Power BI Service Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/fundamentals/service-overview ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric Capacity Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/enterprise/capacity-management Power BI Dataset and Workspace Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-limits ### Others Power BI SLAs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/power-bi/ Fabric Dataflow and Lakehouse Guidance: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/dataflows/overview
